built with pdoc

pycode_kg

pycode_kg: A tool to build a searchable knowledge graph from Python repositories.

Pure AST extraction → SQLite (authoritative) → LanceDB (semantic index).

Public API

Primary entry point::

from pycode_kg import PyCodeKG

kg = PyCodeKG(repo_root, db_path, lancedb_dir)
stats = kg.build(wipe=True)
result = kg.query("database connection setup")
pack = kg.pack("configuration loading")
pack.save("context.md")

Individual layers::

from pycode_kg import CodeGraph, GraphStore, SemanticIndex

Result types::

from pycode_kg import BuildStats, QueryResult, SnippetPack, Snippet

Low-level primitives (v0 contract, locked)::

from pycode_kg import Node, Edge

KGModule SDK (build new domain KGs)::

from pycode_kg import KGModule, KGExtractor, PyCodeKGExtractor, NodeSpec, EdgeSpec

 1"""
 2pycode_kg: A tool to build a searchable knowledge graph from Python repositories.
 3
 4Pure AST extraction → SQLite (authoritative) → LanceDB (semantic index).
 5
 6Public API
 7----------
 8Primary entry point::
 9
10    from pycode_kg import PyCodeKG
11
12    kg = PyCodeKG(repo_root, db_path, lancedb_dir)
13    stats = kg.build(wipe=True)
14    result = kg.query("database connection setup")
15    pack = kg.pack("configuration loading")
16    pack.save("context.md")
17
18Individual layers::
19
20    from pycode_kg import CodeGraph, GraphStore, SemanticIndex
21
22Result types::
23
24    from pycode_kg import BuildStats, QueryResult, SnippetPack, Snippet
25
26Low-level primitives (v0 contract, locked)::
27
28    from pycode_kg import Node, Edge
29
30KGModule SDK (build new domain KGs)::
31
32    from pycode_kg import KGModule, KGExtractor, PyCodeKGExtractor, NodeSpec, EdgeSpec
33"""
34
35__version__ = "0.14.1"
36__author__ = "Eric G. Suchanek, PhD"
37
38# Low-level primitives (locked v0 contract)
39# Layered classes
40from pycode_kg.graph import CodeGraph
41from pycode_kg.index import Embedder, SeedHit, SemanticIndex, SentenceTransformerEmbedder
42
43# Orchestrator + result types
44from pycode_kg.kg import BuildStats, PyCodeKG, QueryResult, Snippet, SnippetPack
45
46# KGModule SDK
47from pycode_kg.module import EdgeSpec, KGExtractor, KGModule, NodeSpec, PyCodeKGExtractor
48from pycode_kg.pycodekg import DEFAULT_MODEL, Edge, Node
49from pycode_kg.snapshots import (
50    Snapshot,
51    SnapshotDelta,
52    SnapshotManager,
53    SnapshotManifest,
54    SnapshotMetrics,
55)
56from pycode_kg.store import DEFAULT_RELS, GraphStore, ProvMeta
57
58__all__ = [
59    # primitives
60    "Node",
61    "Edge",
62    "DEFAULT_MODEL",
63    # layers
64    "CodeGraph",
65    "GraphStore",
66    "ProvMeta",
67    "DEFAULT_RELS",
68    "Embedder",
69    "SentenceTransformerEmbedder",
70    "SemanticIndex",
71    "SeedHit",
72    # KGModule SDK
73    "KGModule",
74    "KGExtractor",
75    "PyCodeKGExtractor",
76    "NodeSpec",
77    "EdgeSpec",
78    # orchestrator
79    "PyCodeKG",
80    # result types
81    "BuildStats",
82    "QueryResult",
83    "Snippet",
84    "SnippetPack",
85    # snapshots
86    "Snapshot",
87    "SnapshotDelta",
88    "SnapshotManifest",
89    "SnapshotManager",
90    "SnapshotMetrics",
91]
@dataclass(frozen=True)
class Node: 
54@dataclass(frozen=True)
55class Node:
56    """
57    Graph node.
58
59    :param id: Stable node id (e.g. fn:pkg/util.py:parse_file)
60    :param kind: module | class | function | method | symbol
61    :param name: Short name
62    :param qualname: Qualified name within module
63    :param module_path: Repo-relative module path
64    :param lineno: Starting line number
65    :param end_lineno: Ending line number (if available)
66    :param docstring: Extracted docstring (may be None)
67    """
68
69    id: str
70    kind: str
71    name: str
72    qualname: str | None
73    module_path: str | None
74    lineno: int | None
75    end_lineno: int | None
76    docstring: str | None

Graph node.

Parameters
  • id: Stable node id (e.g. fn:pkg/util.py: parse_file)
  • kind: module | class | function | method | symbol
  • name: Short name
  • qualname: Qualified name within module
  • module_path: Repo-relative module path
  • lineno: Starting line number
  • end_lineno: Ending line number (if available)
  • docstring: Extracted docstring (may be None)
Node( id: str, kind: str, name: str, qualname: str | None, module_path: str | None, lineno: int | None, end_lineno: int | None, docstring: str | None)
id: str
kind: str
name: str
qualname: str | None
module_path: str | None
lineno: int | None
end_lineno: int | None
docstring: str | None
@dataclass(frozen=True)
class Edge: 
79@dataclass(frozen=True)
80class Edge:
81    """
82    Graph edge.
83
84    :param src: Source node id
85    :param rel: Relationship type
86    :param dst: Destination node id
87    :param evidence: Optional evidence dict (lineno, expr, etc.)
88    """
89
90    src: str
91    rel: str
92    dst: str
93    evidence: dict | None = None

Graph edge.

Parameters
  • src: Source node id
  • rel: Relationship type
  • dst: Destination node id
  • evidence: Optional evidence dict (lineno, expr, etc.)
Edge(src: str, rel: str, dst: str, evidence: dict | None = None)
src: str
rel: str
dst: str
evidence: dict | None = None
DEFAULT_MODEL = 'BAAI/bge-small-en-v1.5'
class CodeGraph: 
 22class CodeGraph:
 23    """
 24    Pure, deterministic AST extraction from a Python repository.
 25
 26    Wraps the low-level ``extract_repo`` function with a cached,
 27    object-oriented interface.  No side effects; calling :meth:`extract`
 28    twice on the same root returns the same result.
 29
 30    Example::
 31
 32        graph = CodeGraph("/path/to/repo")
 33        graph.extract()
 34        print(f"{len(graph.nodes)} nodes, {len(graph.edges)} edges")
 35
 36    :param repo_root: Path to the repository root directory.
 37    """
 38
 39    def __init__(
 40        self,
 41        repo_root: str | Path,
 42        include: set[str] | None = None,
 43        exclude: set[str] | None = None,
 44    ) -> None:
 45        """Initialise the graph for a given repository root.
 46
 47        :param repo_root: Path to the repository root directory.
 48        :param include: Set of top-level directory names to include in extraction.
 49                       When non-empty, only these directories are indexed.
 50                       When empty/None, all directories are indexed.
 51        :param exclude: Set of directory names to prune at every walk depth
 52                       (e.g., ``{"tests", "benchmarks"}``).
 53        """
 54        self.repo_root: Path = Path(repo_root).resolve()
 55        self.include: set[str] = include or set()
 56        self.exclude: set[str] = exclude or set()
 57        self._nodes: list[Node] | None = None
 58        self._edges: list[Edge] | None = None
 59
 60    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
 61    # Public API
 62    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
 63
 64    def extract(self, *, force: bool = False) -> CodeGraph:
 65        """
 66        Run AST extraction (cached after first call).
 67
 68        :param force: Re-extract even if already cached.
 69        :return: self (for chaining)
 70        """
 71        if self._nodes is None or force:
 72            self._nodes, self._edges = extract_repo(
 73                self.repo_root, include=self.include, exclude=self.exclude
 74            )
 75        return self
 76
 77    @property
 78    def nodes(self) -> list[Node]:
 79        """Extracted nodes (calls :meth:`extract` if needed)."""
 80        if self._nodes is None:
 81            self.extract()
 82        return self._nodes  # type: ignore[return-value]
 83
 84    @property
 85    def edges(self) -> list[Edge]:
 86        """Extracted edges (calls :meth:`extract` if needed)."""
 87        if self._edges is None:
 88            self.extract()
 89        return self._edges  # type: ignore[return-value]
 90
 91    def result(self) -> tuple[list[Node], list[Edge]]:
 92        """Return the extracted nodes and edges as a tuple.
 93
 94        :return: ``(nodes, edges)`` tuple, triggering extraction if not yet done.
 95        """
 96        return self.nodes, self.edges
 97
 98    def stats(self) -> dict:
 99        """
100        Return a summary of extracted nodes and edges by kind/relation.
101
102        :return: dict with ``node_counts``, ``edge_counts``, ``total_nodes``,
103                 ``total_edges``.
104        """
105        node_counts: Counter = Counter(n.kind for n in self.nodes)
106        edge_counts: Counter = Counter(e.rel for e in self.edges)
107        return {
108            "repo_root": str(self.repo_root),
109            "total_nodes": len(self.nodes),
110            "total_edges": len(self.edges),
111            "node_counts": dict(node_counts),
112            "edge_counts": dict(edge_counts),
113        }
114
115    def __repr__(self) -> str:
116        """Return a developer-readable representation of this CodeGraph.
117
118        :return: String including repo root, and node/edge counts if already extracted.
119        """
120        extracted = self._nodes is not None
121        if extracted:
122            return (
123                f"CodeGraph(repo_root={self.repo_root!r}, "
124                f"nodes={len(self._nodes)}, edges={len(self._edges)})"  # type: ignore[arg-type]
125            )
126        return f"CodeGraph(repo_root={self.repo_root!r}, not yet extracted)"

Pure, deterministic AST extraction from a Python repository.

Wraps the low-level extract_repo function with a cached, object-oriented interface. No side effects; calling extract() twice on the same root returns the same result.

Example::

graph = CodeGraph("/path/to/repo")
graph.extract()
print(f"{len(graph.nodes)} nodes, {len(graph.edges)} edges")
Parameters
  • repo_root: Path to the repository root directory.
CodeGraph( repo_root: str | pathlib.Path, include: set[str] | None = None, exclude: set[str] | None = None) 
39    def __init__(
40        self,
41        repo_root: str | Path,
42        include: set[str] | None = None,
43        exclude: set[str] | None = None,
44    ) -> None:
45        """Initialise the graph for a given repository root.
46
47        :param repo_root: Path to the repository root directory.
48        :param include: Set of top-level directory names to include in extraction.
49                       When non-empty, only these directories are indexed.
50                       When empty/None, all directories are indexed.
51        :param exclude: Set of directory names to prune at every walk depth
52                       (e.g., ``{"tests", "benchmarks"}``).
53        """
54        self.repo_root: Path = Path(repo_root).resolve()
55        self.include: set[str] = include or set()
56        self.exclude: set[str] = exclude or set()
57        self._nodes: list[Node] | None = None
58        self._edges: list[Edge] | None = None

Initialise the graph for a given repository root.

Parameters
  • repo_root: Path to the repository root directory.
  • include: Set of top-level directory names to include in extraction. When non-empty, only these directories are indexed. When empty/None, all directories are indexed.
  • exclude: Set of directory names to prune at every walk depth (e.g., {"tests", "benchmarks"}).
repo_root: pathlib.Path
include: set[str]
exclude: set[str]
def extract(self, *, force: bool = False) -> CodeGraph: 
64    def extract(self, *, force: bool = False) -> CodeGraph:
65        """
66        Run AST extraction (cached after first call).
67
68        :param force: Re-extract even if already cached.
69        :return: self (for chaining)
70        """
71        if self._nodes is None or force:
72            self._nodes, self._edges = extract_repo(
73                self.repo_root, include=self.include, exclude=self.exclude
74            )
75        return self

Returns

self (for chaining)

77    @property
78    def nodes(self) -> list[Node]:
79        """Extracted nodes (calls :meth:`extract` if needed)."""
80        if self._nodes is None:
81            self.extract()
82        return self._nodes  # type: ignore[return-value]

84    @property
85    def edges(self) -> list[Edge]:
86        """Extracted edges (calls :meth:`extract` if needed)."""
87        if self._edges is None:
88            self.extract()
89        return self._edges  # type: ignore[return-value]

Extracted edges (calls extract() if needed).

def result( self) -> tuple[list[Node], list[Edge]]: 
91    def result(self) -> tuple[list[Node], list[Edge]]:
92        """Return the extracted nodes and edges as a tuple.
93
94        :return: ``(nodes, edges)`` tuple, triggering extraction if not yet done.
95        """
96        return self.nodes, self.edges

Return the extracted nodes and edges as a tuple.

Returns

(nodes, edges) tuple, triggering extraction if not yet done.

def stats(self) -> dict: 
 98    def stats(self) -> dict:
 99        """
100        Return a summary of extracted nodes and edges by kind/relation.
101
102        :return: dict with ``node_counts``, ``edge_counts``, ``total_nodes``,
103                 ``total_edges``.
104        """
105        node_counts: Counter = Counter(n.kind for n in self.nodes)
106        edge_counts: Counter = Counter(e.rel for e in self.edges)
107        return {
108            "repo_root": str(self.repo_root),
109            "total_nodes": len(self.nodes),
110            "total_edges": len(self.edges),
111            "node_counts": dict(node_counts),
112            "edge_counts": dict(edge_counts),
113        }

Return a summary of extracted nodes and edges by kind/relation.

Returns

dict with node_counts, edge_counts, total_nodes, total_edges.

class GraphStore: 
118class GraphStore:
119    """
120    SQLite-backed authoritative store for the Code Knowledge Graph.
121
122    Manages the ``nodes`` and ``edges`` tables and provides graph
123    traversal primitives used by the query layer.
124
125    Example::
126
127        store = GraphStore("pycodekg.sqlite")
128        store.write(nodes, edges, wipe=True)
129        print(store.stats())
130
131        # fetch a single node
132        n = store.node("fn:src/foo.py:bar")
133
134        # expand from seeds
135        meta = store.expand({"fn:src/foo.py:bar"}, hop=2)
136
137    :param db_path: Path to the SQLite database file (created if absent).
138    """
139
140    def __init__(self, db_path: str | Path) -> None:
141        """Initialise the store against a SQLite database file.
142
143        :param db_path: Path to the SQLite database file (created if absent).
144        """
145        self.db_path = Path(db_path)
146        self._con: sqlite3.Connection | None = None
147
148    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
149    # Connection management
150    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
151
152    @property
153    def con(self) -> sqlite3.Connection:
154        """Lazy SQLite connection (created on first access)."""
155        if self._con is None:
156            self.db_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
157            self._con = sqlite3.connect(
158                str(self.db_path),
159                check_same_thread=False,  # safe: read-heavy; writes serialised by SQLite WAL
160            )
161            self._con.executescript(_SCHEMA_SQL)
162        return self._con
163
164    def close(self) -> None:
165        """Close the underlying SQLite connection."""
166        if self._con is not None:
167            self._con.close()
168            self._con = None
169
170    def __enter__(self) -> GraphStore:
171        """Enter the context manager.
172
173        :return: This :class:`GraphStore` instance.
174        """
175        return self
176
177    def __exit__(self, *_: object) -> None:
178        """Exit the context manager, closing the SQLite connection."""
179        self.close()
180
181    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
182    # Write
183    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
184
185    def clear(self) -> None:
186        """Delete all nodes and edges."""
187        self.con.execute("DELETE FROM edges;")
188        self.con.execute("DELETE FROM nodes;")
189        self.con.commit()
190
191    def write(
192        self,
193        nodes: Sequence[Node],
194        edges: Sequence[Edge],
195        *,
196        wipe: bool = False,
197    ) -> None:
198        """
199        Persist a complete graph to SQLite.
200
201        :param nodes: Node list from :class:`~pycode_kg.graph.CodeGraph`.
202        :param edges: Edge list from :class:`~pycode_kg.graph.CodeGraph`.
203        :param wipe: If ``True``, clear existing data before writing.
204        """
205        if wipe:
206            self.clear()
207        self._upsert_nodes(nodes)
208        self._upsert_edges(edges)
209
210    def _upsert_nodes(self, nodes: Iterable[Node]) -> None:
211        """Insert or update a batch of nodes in the ``nodes`` table.
212
213        :param nodes: Iterable of :class:`~pycode_kg.pycodekg.Node` objects to persist.
214        """
215        rows = [
216            (
217                n.id,
218                n.kind,
219                n.name,
220                n.qualname,
221                n.module_path,
222                n.lineno,
223                n.end_lineno,
224                n.docstring,
225            )
226            for n in nodes
227        ]
228        self.con.executemany(
229            """
230            INSERT INTO nodes
231              (id, kind, name, qualname, module_path, lineno, end_lineno, docstring)
232            VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
233            ON CONFLICT(id) DO UPDATE SET
234              kind=excluded.kind,
235              name=excluded.name,
236              qualname=excluded.qualname,
237              module_path=excluded.module_path,
238              lineno=excluded.lineno,
239              end_lineno=excluded.end_lineno,
240              docstring=excluded.docstring
241            """,
242            rows,
243        )
244        self.con.commit()
245
246    def _upsert_edges(self, edges: Iterable[Edge]) -> None:
247        """Insert or update a batch of edges in the ``edges`` table.
248
249        :param edges: Iterable of :class:`~pycode_kg.pycodekg.Edge` objects to persist.
250        """
251        rows = [
252            (
253                e.src,
254                e.rel,
255                e.dst,
256                (json.dumps(e.evidence, ensure_ascii=False) if e.evidence is not None else None),
257            )
258            for e in edges
259        ]
260        self.con.executemany(
261            """
262            INSERT INTO edges (src, rel, dst, evidence)
263            VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)
264            ON CONFLICT(src, rel, dst) DO UPDATE SET
265              evidence=excluded.evidence
266            """,
267            rows,
268        )
269        self.con.commit()
270
271    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
272    # Read — single node
273    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
274
275    def node(self, node_id: str) -> dict | None:
276        """
277        Fetch a single node by id.
278
279        :param node_id: Stable node identifier.
280        :return: Node dict or ``None`` if not found.
281        """
282        row = self.con.execute(
283            """
284            SELECT id, kind, name, qualname, module_path, lineno, end_lineno, docstring
285            FROM nodes WHERE id = ?
286            """,
287            (node_id,),
288        ).fetchone()
289        return _row_to_node(row) if row else None
290
291    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
292    # Read — filtered node lists
293    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
294
295    def query_nodes(
296        self,
297        *,
298        kinds: Sequence[str] | None = None,
299        module: str | None = None,
300    ) -> list[dict]:
301        """
302        Return nodes matching optional filters.
303
304        :param kinds: Restrict to these node kinds (e.g. ``["function", "method"]``).
305        :param module: Restrict to nodes in this module path (exact match).
306        :return: List of node dicts.
307        """
308        clauses: list[str] = []
309        params: list[object] = []
310
311        if kinds:
312            placeholders = ",".join("?" for _ in kinds)
313            clauses.append(f"kind IN ({placeholders})")
314            params.extend(kinds)
315
316        if module is not None:
317            clauses.append("module_path = ?")
318            params.append(module)
319
320        where = f"WHERE {' AND '.join(clauses)}" if clauses else ""
321        rows = self.con.execute(
322            f"""
323            SELECT id, kind, name, qualname, module_path, lineno, end_lineno, docstring
324            FROM nodes {where}
325            ORDER BY module_path, lineno
326            """,
327            params,
328        ).fetchall()
329        return [_row_to_node(r) for r in rows]
330
331    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
332    # Read — edges
333    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
334
335    def edges_within(self, node_ids: set[str]) -> list[dict]:
336        """
337        Return all edges where both ``src`` and ``dst`` are in *node_ids*.
338
339        :param node_ids: Set of node IDs to restrict to.
340        :return: List of edge dicts with keys ``src``, ``rel``, ``dst``, ``evidence``.
341        """
342        if not node_ids:
343            return []
344
345        self.con.execute("DROP TABLE IF EXISTS _tmp_ids;")
346        self.con.execute("CREATE TEMP TABLE _tmp_ids (id TEXT PRIMARY KEY);")
347        self.con.executemany("INSERT INTO _tmp_ids (id) VALUES (?)", [(i,) for i in node_ids])
348        rows = self.con.execute(
349            """
350            SELECT e.src, e.rel, e.dst, e.evidence
351            FROM edges e
352            JOIN _tmp_ids s ON s.id = e.src
353            JOIN _tmp_ids d ON d.id = e.dst
354            """
355        ).fetchall()
356        return [{"src": r[0], "rel": r[1], "dst": r[2], "evidence": r[3]} for r in rows]
357
358    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
359    # Graph traversal
360    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
361
362    def expand(
363        self,
364        seed_ids: set[str],
365        *,
366        hop: int = 1,
367        rels: tuple[str, ...] = DEFAULT_RELS,
368    ) -> dict[str, ProvMeta]:
369        """
370        Expand the graph from *seed_ids* up to *hop* hops.
371
372        Returns a mapping from every reachable node ID to its
373        :class:`ProvMeta` (minimum hop distance and originating seed).
374
375        :param seed_ids: Starting node IDs (hop 0).
376        :param hop: Maximum number of hops to traverse.
377        :param rels: Edge relation types to follow.
378        :return: ``{node_id: ProvMeta}`` for all reachable nodes.
379        """
380        rels = tuple(rels)
381        meta: dict[str, ProvMeta] = {sid: ProvMeta(best_hop=0, via_seed=sid) for sid in seed_ids}
382        frontier: set[str] = set(seed_ids)
383
384        for h in range(1, hop + 1):
385            nxt: set[str] = set()
386            for nid in frontier:
387                rows = self.con.execute(
388                    f"""
389                    SELECT src, dst FROM edges
390                    WHERE (src = ? OR dst = ?)
391                      AND rel IN ({",".join("?" for _ in rels)})
392                    """,
393                    (nid, nid, *rels),
394                ).fetchall()
395                for src, dst in rows:
396                    for cand in (src, dst):
397                        if cand not in meta:
398                            meta[cand] = ProvMeta(
399                                best_hop=h,
400                                via_seed=meta[nid].via_seed,
401                            )
402                            nxt.add(cand)
403                        elif h < meta[cand].best_hop:
404                            meta[cand] = ProvMeta(
405                                best_hop=h,
406                                via_seed=meta[nid].via_seed,
407                            )
408                            nxt.add(cand)
409            frontier = nxt
410
411        return meta
412
413    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
414    # Symbol resolution
415    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
416
417    def resolve_symbols(self) -> int:
418        """
419        Add ``RESOLVES_TO`` edges from ``sym:`` stub nodes to their
420        first-party definitions (``fn:``, ``cls:``, ``m:``, ``mod:``).
421
422        The AST visitor emits ``CALLS → sym:Foo`` for any call whose target
423        cannot be resolved at walk time (imported names, attribute accesses,
424        etc.).  This post-build step links those stubs to the canonical
425        definition nodes by matching on the ``name`` field, making fan-in
426        queries (who calls X?) work correctly via graph traversal.
427
428        Matching is by name only, so a ``sym:close`` stub will acquire
429        ``RESOLVES_TO`` edges to *every* in-repo node named ``close``.
430        Agents can use the graph context to disambiguate.  The operation is
431        idempotent — duplicate edges are silently ignored.
432
433        :return: Number of new ``RESOLVES_TO`` edges written.
434        """
435        sym_rows = self.con.execute(
436            "SELECT id, name, qualname FROM nodes WHERE kind = 'symbol'"
437        ).fetchall()
438
439        def_rows = self.con.execute(
440            """
441            SELECT id, kind, name, qualname, module_path
442            FROM nodes
443            WHERE kind != 'symbol' AND name IS NOT NULL
444            """
445        ).fetchall()
446
447        name_to_defs: dict[str, list[str]] = {}
448        dotted_to_defs: dict[str, list[str]] = {}
449        for def_id, def_kind, def_name, def_qualname, def_module_path in def_rows:
450            name_to_defs.setdefault(def_name, []).append(def_id)
451
452            module_dotted_variants = _module_to_dotted_variants(def_module_path)
453            if def_kind == "module":
454                for module_dotted in module_dotted_variants:
455                    dotted_to_defs.setdefault(module_dotted, []).append(def_id)
456            elif module_dotted_variants and def_qualname:
457                for module_dotted in module_dotted_variants:
458                    dotted_key = f"{module_dotted}.{def_qualname}"
459                    dotted_to_defs.setdefault(dotted_key, []).append(def_id)
460
461        edges: list[tuple[str, str, str, str]] = []
462        for sym_id, sym_name, sym_qualname in sym_rows:
463            if not sym_name:
464                continue
465
466            candidates: list[str] = []
467            mode = "name_fallback"
468            confidence = "medium"
469
470            if sym_qualname and "." in sym_qualname:
471                exact = dotted_to_defs.get(sym_qualname, [])
472                if exact:
473                    candidates = exact
474                    mode = "exact_qualname"
475                    confidence = "high"
476
477            if not candidates:
478                candidates = name_to_defs.get(sym_name, [])
479                if len(candidates) > 1:
480                    mode = "name_fallback_ambiguous"
481                    confidence = "low"
482
483            for def_id in candidates:
484                evidence = {
485                    "resolution_mode": mode,
486                    "confidence": confidence,
487                    "symbol": sym_id,
488                }
489                if mode == "name_fallback_ambiguous":
490                    evidence["candidate_count"] = len(candidates)
491                edges.append(
492                    (
493                        sym_id,
494                        "RESOLVES_TO",
495                        def_id,
496                        json.dumps(evidence, ensure_ascii=False),
497                    )
498                )
499
500        if edges:
501            self.con.executemany(
502                """
503                INSERT INTO edges (src, rel, dst, evidence)
504                VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)
505                ON CONFLICT(src, rel, dst) DO UPDATE SET
506                  evidence=excluded.evidence
507                """,
508                edges,
509            )
510            self.con.commit()
511
512        return len(edges)
513
514    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
515    # Caller lookup (fan-in)
516    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
517
518    def callers_of(self, node_id: str, *, rel: str = "CALLS") -> list[dict]:
519        """
520        Return all nodes that have a *rel* edge pointing at *node_id*,
521        including cross-module callers that reference it via ``sym:`` stubs.
522
523        Two-phase lookup:
524
525        1. Direct: ``edges WHERE dst = node_id AND rel = rel``
526        2. Indirect: find all ``sym:`` stubs with a ``RESOLVES_TO`` edge to
527           *node_id*, then collect their incoming *rel* edges.
528
529        Caller nodes are deduplicated and returned as node dicts.
530
531        :param node_id: Target node identifier (e.g. ``fn:src/foo.py:bar``).
532        :param rel: Relation type to invert (default ``"CALLS"``).
533        :return: List of caller node dicts, deduplicated.
534        """
535        direct = self.con.execute(
536            "SELECT src, evidence FROM edges WHERE dst = ? AND rel = ?",
537            (node_id, rel),
538        ).fetchall()
539
540        stubs = self.con.execute(
541            "SELECT src FROM edges WHERE dst = ? AND rel = 'RESOLVES_TO'",
542            (node_id,),
543        ).fetchall()
544
545        stub_callers: list[tuple[str, str | None, str]] = []
546        for (stub_id,) in stubs:
547            rows = self.con.execute(
548                "SELECT src, evidence FROM edges WHERE dst = ? AND rel = ?",
549                (stub_id, rel),
550            ).fetchall()
551            for src_id, evidence in rows:
552                stub_callers.append((src_id, evidence, stub_id))
553
554        seen: set[str] = set()
555        result: list[dict] = []
556        for caller_id, evidence in direct:
557            if caller_id in seen:
558                continue
559            seen.add(caller_id)
560            n = self.node(caller_id)
561            if n:
562                lineno = _parse_call_site_lineno(evidence)
563                if lineno is not None:
564                    n = {**n, "call_site_lineno": lineno}
565                result.append(n)
566
567        for caller_id, evidence, _stub_id in stub_callers:
568            if caller_id in seen:
569                continue
570            if not self._is_compatible_stub_caller(caller_id, evidence, node_id):
571                continue
572            seen.add(caller_id)
573            n = self.node(caller_id)
574            if n:
575                lineno = _parse_call_site_lineno(evidence)
576                if lineno is not None:
577                    n = {**n, "call_site_lineno": lineno}
578                result.append(n)
579
580        return result
581
582    def _is_compatible_stub_caller(
583        self, caller_id: str, evidence: str | None, target_node_id: str
584    ) -> bool:
585        """Validate a stub-resolved caller against import hints from its module.
586
587        If the call expression name is imported in the caller's module, retain
588        the caller only when one such import symbol resolves to ``target_node_id``.
589        When no import hints exist, caller is kept (no extra signal available).
590
591        :param caller_id: Caller node ID from an incoming CALLS edge.
592        :param evidence: JSON-encoded edge evidence from the CALLS edge.
593        :param target_node_id: Target node currently being reverse-resolved.
594        :return: ``True`` if caller is compatible with target resolution.
595        """
596        if not evidence:
597            return True
598        try:
599            ev = json.loads(evidence)
600        except (TypeError, json.JSONDecodeError):
601            return True
602
603        expr = ev.get("expr")
604        if not isinstance(expr, str) or not expr:
605            return True
606
607        short_name = expr.split(".")[-1]
608        caller_node = self.node(caller_id)
609        if not caller_node:
610            return True
611
612        module_path = caller_node.get("module_path")
613        if not module_path:
614            return True
615
616        module_id = node_id("module", module_path, None)
617        import_rows = self.con.execute(
618            "SELECT dst FROM edges WHERE src = ? AND rel = 'IMPORTS'",
619            (module_id,),
620        ).fetchall()
621
622        matching_import_symbols = [
623            sym_id
624            for (sym_id,) in import_rows
625            if sym_id.startswith("sym:") and sym_id.split(".")[-1] == short_name
626        ]
627        if not matching_import_symbols:
628            return True
629
630        for sym_id in matching_import_symbols:
631            hit = self.con.execute(
632                "SELECT 1 FROM edges WHERE src = ? AND rel = 'RESOLVES_TO' AND dst = ? LIMIT 1",
633                (sym_id, target_node_id),
634            ).fetchone()
635            if hit:
636                return True
637        return False
638
639    def edges_from(
640        self, node_id: str, *, rel: str = "CALLS", limit: int | None = None
641    ) -> list[dict]:
642        """
643        Return all edges originating from *node_id* with the given relation type.
644
645        :param node_id: Source node identifier (e.g. ``fn:src/foo.py:bar``).
646        :param rel: Relation type to match (default ``"CALLS"``).
647        :param limit: Maximum number of edges to return (``None`` for unlimited).
648        :return: List of edge dicts with keys ``src``, ``rel``, ``dst``, ``evidence``.
649        """
650        query = "SELECT src, rel, dst, evidence FROM edges WHERE src = ? AND rel = ?"
651        params: list[object] = [node_id, rel]
652        if limit is not None:
653            query += " LIMIT ?"
654            params.append(limit)
655        rows = self.con.execute(query, params).fetchall()
656        return [{"src": r[0], "rel": r[1], "dst": r[2], "evidence": r[3]} for r in rows]
657
658    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
659    # Stats
660    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
661
662    def stats(self) -> dict:
663        """
664        Return node and edge counts by kind/relation.
665
666        ``meaningful_nodes`` excludes ``sym:`` infrastructure stubs so callers
667        get an accurate count of real code entities (modules, classes,
668        functions, methods).
669
670        :return: dict with ``total_nodes``, ``meaningful_nodes``,
671                 ``total_edges``, ``node_counts``, ``edge_counts``.
672        """
673        node_rows = self.con.execute("SELECT kind, COUNT(*) FROM nodes GROUP BY kind").fetchall()
674        edge_rows = self.con.execute("SELECT rel, COUNT(*) FROM edges GROUP BY rel").fetchall()
675        total_nodes = self.con.execute("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM nodes").fetchone()[0]
676        total_edges = self.con.execute("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM edges").fetchone()[0]
677        symbol_count = self.con.execute(
678            "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM nodes WHERE kind = 'symbol'"
679        ).fetchone()[0]
680        return {
681            "db_path": str(self.db_path),
682            "total_nodes": total_nodes,
683            "meaningful_nodes": total_nodes - symbol_count,
684            "total_edges": total_edges,
685            "node_counts": {r[0]: r[1] for r in node_rows},
686            "edge_counts": {r[0]: r[1] for r in edge_rows},
687        }
688
689    def __repr__(self) -> str:
690        """Return a developer-readable representation of this GraphStore.
691
692        :return: String of the form ``GraphStore(db_path=...)``.
693        """
694        return f"GraphStore(db_path={self.db_path!r})"

SQLite-backed authoritative store for the Code Knowledge Graph.

Manages the nodes and edges tables and provides graph traversal primitives used by the query layer.

Example::

store = GraphStore("pycodekg.sqlite")
store.write(nodes, edges, wipe=True)
print(store.stats())

# fetch a single node
n = store.node("fn:src/foo.py:bar")

# expand from seeds
meta = store.expand({"fn:src/foo.py:bar"}, hop=2)
Parameters
  • db_path: Path to the SQLite database file (created if absent).
GraphStore(db_path: str | pathlib.Path) 
140    def __init__(self, db_path: str | Path) -> None:
141        """Initialise the store against a SQLite database file.
142
143        :param db_path: Path to the SQLite database file (created if absent).
144        """
145        self.db_path = Path(db_path)
146        self._con: sqlite3.Connection | None = None

Initialise the store against a SQLite database file.

Parameters
  • db_path: Path to the SQLite database file (created if absent).
db_path
con: sqlite3.Connection 
152    @property
153    def con(self) -> sqlite3.Connection:
154        """Lazy SQLite connection (created on first access)."""
155        if self._con is None:
156            self.db_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
157            self._con = sqlite3.connect(
158                str(self.db_path),
159                check_same_thread=False,  # safe: read-heavy; writes serialised by SQLite WAL
160            )
161            self._con.executescript(_SCHEMA_SQL)
162        return self._con

Lazy SQLite connection (created on first access).

def close(self) -> None: 
164    def close(self) -> None:
165        """Close the underlying SQLite connection."""
166        if self._con is not None:
167            self._con.close()
168            self._con = None

Close the underlying SQLite connection.

def clear(self) -> None: 
185    def clear(self) -> None:
186        """Delete all nodes and edges."""
187        self.con.execute("DELETE FROM edges;")
188        self.con.execute("DELETE FROM nodes;")
189        self.con.commit()

Delete all nodes and edges.

def write( self, nodes: Sequence[Node], edges: Sequence[Edge], *, wipe: bool = False) -> None: 
191    def write(
192        self,
193        nodes: Sequence[Node],
194        edges: Sequence[Edge],
195        *,
196        wipe: bool = False,
197    ) -> None:
198        """
199        Persist a complete graph to SQLite.
200
201        :param nodes: Node list from :class:`~pycode_kg.graph.CodeGraph`.
202        :param edges: Edge list from :class:`~pycode_kg.graph.CodeGraph`.
203        :param wipe: If ``True``, clear existing data before writing.
204        """
205        if wipe:
206            self.clear()
207        self._upsert_nodes(nodes)
208        self._upsert_edges(edges)

Persist a complete graph to SQLite.

Parameters
  • nodes: Node list from ~pycode_kg.graph.CodeGraph.
  • edges: Edge list from ~pycode_kg.graph.CodeGraph.
  • wipe: If True, clear existing data before writing.
def node(self, node_id: str) -> dict | None: 
275    def node(self, node_id: str) -> dict | None:
276        """
277        Fetch a single node by id.
278
279        :param node_id: Stable node identifier.
280        :return: Node dict or ``None`` if not found.
281        """
282        row = self.con.execute(
283            """
284            SELECT id, kind, name, qualname, module_path, lineno, end_lineno, docstring
285            FROM nodes WHERE id = ?
286            """,
287            (node_id,),
288        ).fetchone()
289        return _row_to_node(row) if row else None

Fetch a single node by id.

Parameters
  • node_id: Stable node identifier.
Returns

Node dict or None if not found.

def query_nodes( self, *, kinds: Sequence[str] | None = None, module: str | None = None) -> list[dict]: 
295    def query_nodes(
296        self,
297        *,
298        kinds: Sequence[str] | None = None,
299        module: str | None = None,
300    ) -> list[dict]:
301        """
302        Return nodes matching optional filters.
303
304        :param kinds: Restrict to these node kinds (e.g. ``["function", "method"]``).
305        :param module: Restrict to nodes in this module path (exact match).
306        :return: List of node dicts.
307        """
308        clauses: list[str] = []
309        params: list[object] = []
310
311        if kinds:
312            placeholders = ",".join("?" for _ in kinds)
313            clauses.append(f"kind IN ({placeholders})")
314            params.extend(kinds)
315
316        if module is not None:
317            clauses.append("module_path = ?")
318            params.append(module)
319
320        where = f"WHERE {' AND '.join(clauses)}" if clauses else ""
321        rows = self.con.execute(
322            f"""
323            SELECT id, kind, name, qualname, module_path, lineno, end_lineno, docstring
324            FROM nodes {where}
325            ORDER BY module_path, lineno
326            """,
327            params,
328        ).fetchall()
329        return [_row_to_node(r) for r in rows]

Return nodes matching optional filters.

Parameters
  • kinds: Restrict to these node kinds (e.g. ["function", "method"]).
  • module: Restrict to nodes in this module path (exact match).
Returns

List of node dicts.

def edges_within(self, node_ids: set[str]) -> list[dict]: 
335    def edges_within(self, node_ids: set[str]) -> list[dict]:
336        """
337        Return all edges where both ``src`` and ``dst`` are in *node_ids*.
338
339        :param node_ids: Set of node IDs to restrict to.
340        :return: List of edge dicts with keys ``src``, ``rel``, ``dst``, ``evidence``.
341        """
342        if not node_ids:
343            return []
344
345        self.con.execute("DROP TABLE IF EXISTS _tmp_ids;")
346        self.con.execute("CREATE TEMP TABLE _tmp_ids (id TEXT PRIMARY KEY);")
347        self.con.executemany("INSERT INTO _tmp_ids (id) VALUES (?)", [(i,) for i in node_ids])
348        rows = self.con.execute(
349            """
350            SELECT e.src, e.rel, e.dst, e.evidence
351            FROM edges e
352            JOIN _tmp_ids s ON s.id = e.src
353            JOIN _tmp_ids d ON d.id = e.dst
354            """
355        ).fetchall()
356        return [{"src": r[0], "rel": r[1], "dst": r[2], "evidence": r[3]} for r in rows]

Return all edges where both src and dst are in node_ids.

Parameters
  • node_ids: Set of node IDs to restrict to.
Returns

List of edge dicts with keys src, rel, dst, evidence.

def expand( self, seed_ids: set[str], *, hop: int = 1, rels: tuple[str, ...] = ('CONTAINS', 'CALLS', 'IMPORTS', 'INHERITS')) -> dict[str, ProvMeta]: 
362    def expand(
363        self,
364        seed_ids: set[str],
365        *,
366        hop: int = 1,
367        rels: tuple[str, ...] = DEFAULT_RELS,
368    ) -> dict[str, ProvMeta]:
369        """
370        Expand the graph from *seed_ids* up to *hop* hops.
371
372        Returns a mapping from every reachable node ID to its
373        :class:`ProvMeta` (minimum hop distance and originating seed).
374
375        :param seed_ids: Starting node IDs (hop 0).
376        :param hop: Maximum number of hops to traverse.
377        :param rels: Edge relation types to follow.
378        :return: ``{node_id: ProvMeta}`` for all reachable nodes.
379        """
380        rels = tuple(rels)
381        meta: dict[str, ProvMeta] = {sid: ProvMeta(best_hop=0, via_seed=sid) for sid in seed_ids}
382        frontier: set[str] = set(seed_ids)
383
384        for h in range(1, hop + 1):
385            nxt: set[str] = set()
386            for nid in frontier:
387                rows = self.con.execute(
388                    f"""
389                    SELECT src, dst FROM edges
390                    WHERE (src = ? OR dst = ?)
391                      AND rel IN ({",".join("?" for _ in rels)})
392                    """,
393                    (nid, nid, *rels),
394                ).fetchall()
395                for src, dst in rows:
396                    for cand in (src, dst):
397                        if cand not in meta:
398                            meta[cand] = ProvMeta(
399                                best_hop=h,
400                                via_seed=meta[nid].via_seed,
401                            )
402                            nxt.add(cand)
403                        elif h < meta[cand].best_hop:
404                            meta[cand] = ProvMeta(
405                                best_hop=h,
406                                via_seed=meta[nid].via_seed,
407                            )
408                            nxt.add(cand)
409            frontier = nxt
410
411        return meta

Expand the graph from seed_ids up to hop hops.

Returns a mapping from every reachable node ID to its ProvMeta (minimum hop distance and originating seed).

Parameters
  • seed_ids: Starting node IDs (hop 0).
  • hop: Maximum number of hops to traverse.
  • rels: Edge relation types to follow.
Returns

{node_id: ProvMeta} for all reachable nodes.

def resolve_symbols(self) -> int: 
417    def resolve_symbols(self) -> int:
418        """
419        Add ``RESOLVES_TO`` edges from ``sym:`` stub nodes to their
420        first-party definitions (``fn:``, ``cls:``, ``m:``, ``mod:``).
421
422        The AST visitor emits ``CALLS → sym:Foo`` for any call whose target
423        cannot be resolved at walk time (imported names, attribute accesses,
424        etc.).  This post-build step links those stubs to the canonical
425        definition nodes by matching on the ``name`` field, making fan-in
426        queries (who calls X?) work correctly via graph traversal.
427
428        Matching is by name only, so a ``sym:close`` stub will acquire
429        ``RESOLVES_TO`` edges to *every* in-repo node named ``close``.
430        Agents can use the graph context to disambiguate.  The operation is
431        idempotent — duplicate edges are silently ignored.
432
433        :return: Number of new ``RESOLVES_TO`` edges written.
434        """
435        sym_rows = self.con.execute(
436            "SELECT id, name, qualname FROM nodes WHERE kind = 'symbol'"
437        ).fetchall()
438
439        def_rows = self.con.execute(
440            """
441            SELECT id, kind, name, qualname, module_path
442            FROM nodes
443            WHERE kind != 'symbol' AND name IS NOT NULL
444            """
445        ).fetchall()
446
447        name_to_defs: dict[str, list[str]] = {}
448        dotted_to_defs: dict[str, list[str]] = {}
449        for def_id, def_kind, def_name, def_qualname, def_module_path in def_rows:
450            name_to_defs.setdefault(def_name, []).append(def_id)
451
452            module_dotted_variants = _module_to_dotted_variants(def_module_path)
453            if def_kind == "module":
454                for module_dotted in module_dotted_variants:
455                    dotted_to_defs.setdefault(module_dotted, []).append(def_id)
456            elif module_dotted_variants and def_qualname:
457                for module_dotted in module_dotted_variants:
458                    dotted_key = f"{module_dotted}.{def_qualname}"
459                    dotted_to_defs.setdefault(dotted_key, []).append(def_id)
460
461        edges: list[tuple[str, str, str, str]] = []
462        for sym_id, sym_name, sym_qualname in sym_rows:
463            if not sym_name:
464                continue
465
466            candidates: list[str] = []
467            mode = "name_fallback"
468            confidence = "medium"
469
470            if sym_qualname and "." in sym_qualname:
471                exact = dotted_to_defs.get(sym_qualname, [])
472                if exact:
473                    candidates = exact
474                    mode = "exact_qualname"
475                    confidence = "high"
476
477            if not candidates:
478                candidates = name_to_defs.get(sym_name, [])
479                if len(candidates) > 1:
480                    mode = "name_fallback_ambiguous"
481                    confidence = "low"
482
483            for def_id in candidates:
484                evidence = {
485                    "resolution_mode": mode,
486                    "confidence": confidence,
487                    "symbol": sym_id,
488                }
489                if mode == "name_fallback_ambiguous":
490                    evidence["candidate_count"] = len(candidates)
491                edges.append(
492                    (
493                        sym_id,
494                        "RESOLVES_TO",
495                        def_id,
496                        json.dumps(evidence, ensure_ascii=False),
497                    )
498                )
499
500        if edges:
501            self.con.executemany(
502                """
503                INSERT INTO edges (src, rel, dst, evidence)
504                VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)
505                ON CONFLICT(src, rel, dst) DO UPDATE SET
506                  evidence=excluded.evidence
507                """,
508                edges,
509            )
510            self.con.commit()
511
512        return len(edges)

Add RESOLVES_TO edges from sym: stub nodes to their first-party definitions (fn:, cls:, m:, mod:).

The AST visitor emits CALLS → sym:Foo for any call whose target cannot be resolved at walk time (imported names, attribute accesses, etc.). This post-build step links those stubs to the canonical definition nodes by matching on the name field, making fan-in queries (who calls X?) work correctly via graph traversal.

Matching is by name only, so a sym:close stub will acquire RESOLVES_TO edges to every in-repo node named close. Agents can use the graph context to disambiguate. The operation is idempotent — duplicate edges are silently ignored.

Returns

Number of new RESOLVES_TO edges written.

def callers_of(self, node_id: str, *, rel: str = 'CALLS') -> list[dict]: 
518    def callers_of(self, node_id: str, *, rel: str = "CALLS") -> list[dict]:
519        """
520        Return all nodes that have a *rel* edge pointing at *node_id*,
521        including cross-module callers that reference it via ``sym:`` stubs.
522
523        Two-phase lookup:
524
525        1. Direct: ``edges WHERE dst = node_id AND rel = rel``
526        2. Indirect: find all ``sym:`` stubs with a ``RESOLVES_TO`` edge to
527           *node_id*, then collect their incoming *rel* edges.
528
529        Caller nodes are deduplicated and returned as node dicts.
530
531        :param node_id: Target node identifier (e.g. ``fn:src/foo.py:bar``).
532        :param rel: Relation type to invert (default ``"CALLS"``).
533        :return: List of caller node dicts, deduplicated.
534        """
535        direct = self.con.execute(
536            "SELECT src, evidence FROM edges WHERE dst = ? AND rel = ?",
537            (node_id, rel),
538        ).fetchall()
539
540        stubs = self.con.execute(
541            "SELECT src FROM edges WHERE dst = ? AND rel = 'RESOLVES_TO'",
542            (node_id,),
543        ).fetchall()
544
545        stub_callers: list[tuple[str, str | None, str]] = []
546        for (stub_id,) in stubs:
547            rows = self.con.execute(
548                "SELECT src, evidence FROM edges WHERE dst = ? AND rel = ?",
549                (stub_id, rel),
550            ).fetchall()
551            for src_id, evidence in rows:
552                stub_callers.append((src_id, evidence, stub_id))
553
554        seen: set[str] = set()
555        result: list[dict] = []
556        for caller_id, evidence in direct:
557            if caller_id in seen:
558                continue
559            seen.add(caller_id)
560            n = self.node(caller_id)
561            if n:
562                lineno = _parse_call_site_lineno(evidence)
563                if lineno is not None:
564                    n = {**n, "call_site_lineno": lineno}
565                result.append(n)
566
567        for caller_id, evidence, _stub_id in stub_callers:
568            if caller_id in seen:
569                continue
570            if not self._is_compatible_stub_caller(caller_id, evidence, node_id):
571                continue
572            seen.add(caller_id)
573            n = self.node(caller_id)
574            if n:
575                lineno = _parse_call_site_lineno(evidence)
576                if lineno is not None:
577                    n = {**n, "call_site_lineno": lineno}
578                result.append(n)
579
580        return result

Return all nodes that have a rel edge pointing at node_id, including cross-module callers that reference it via sym: stubs.

Two-phase lookup:

  1. Direct: edges WHERE dst = node_id AND rel = rel
  2. Indirect: find all sym: stubs with a RESOLVES_TO edge to node_id, then collect their incoming rel edges.

Caller nodes are deduplicated and returned as node dicts.

Parameters
  • **node_id: Target node identifier (e.g. fn:src/foo.py**: bar).
  • rel: Relation type to invert (default "CALLS").
Returns

List of caller node dicts, deduplicated.

def edges_from( self, node_id: str, *, rel: str = 'CALLS', limit: int | None = None) -> list[dict]: 
639    def edges_from(
640        self, node_id: str, *, rel: str = "CALLS", limit: int | None = None
641    ) -> list[dict]:
642        """
643        Return all edges originating from *node_id* with the given relation type.
644
645        :param node_id: Source node identifier (e.g. ``fn:src/foo.py:bar``).
646        :param rel: Relation type to match (default ``"CALLS"``).
647        :param limit: Maximum number of edges to return (``None`` for unlimited).
648        :return: List of edge dicts with keys ``src``, ``rel``, ``dst``, ``evidence``.
649        """
650        query = "SELECT src, rel, dst, evidence FROM edges WHERE src = ? AND rel = ?"
651        params: list[object] = [node_id, rel]
652        if limit is not None:
653            query += " LIMIT ?"
654            params.append(limit)
655        rows = self.con.execute(query, params).fetchall()
656        return [{"src": r[0], "rel": r[1], "dst": r[2], "evidence": r[3]} for r in rows]

Return all edges originating from node_id with the given relation type.

Parameters
  • **node_id: Source node identifier (e.g. fn:src/foo.py**: bar).
  • rel: Relation type to match (default "CALLS").
  • limit: Maximum number of edges to return (None for unlimited).
Returns

List of edge dicts with keys src, rel, dst, evidence.

def stats(self) -> dict: 
662    def stats(self) -> dict:
663        """
664        Return node and edge counts by kind/relation.
665
666        ``meaningful_nodes`` excludes ``sym:`` infrastructure stubs so callers
667        get an accurate count of real code entities (modules, classes,
668        functions, methods).
669
670        :return: dict with ``total_nodes``, ``meaningful_nodes``,
671                 ``total_edges``, ``node_counts``, ``edge_counts``.
672        """
673        node_rows = self.con.execute("SELECT kind, COUNT(*) FROM nodes GROUP BY kind").fetchall()
674        edge_rows = self.con.execute("SELECT rel, COUNT(*) FROM edges GROUP BY rel").fetchall()
675        total_nodes = self.con.execute("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM nodes").fetchone()[0]
676        total_edges = self.con.execute("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM edges").fetchone()[0]
677        symbol_count = self.con.execute(
678            "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM nodes WHERE kind = 'symbol'"
679        ).fetchone()[0]
680        return {
681            "db_path": str(self.db_path),
682            "total_nodes": total_nodes,
683            "meaningful_nodes": total_nodes - symbol_count,
684            "total_edges": total_edges,
685            "node_counts": {r[0]: r[1] for r in node_rows},
686            "edge_counts": {r[0]: r[1] for r in edge_rows},
687        }

Return node and edge counts by kind/relation.

meaningful_nodes excludes sym: infrastructure stubs so callers get an accurate count of real code entities (modules, classes, functions, methods).

Returns

dict with total_nodes, meaningful_nodes, total_edges, node_counts, edge_counts.

class ProvMeta: 
 86class ProvMeta:
 87    """
 88    Provenance metadata for a node returned by :meth:`GraphStore.expand`.
 89
 90    :param best_hop: Minimum hop distance from any seed node.
 91    :param via_seed: ID of the seed node that yielded the shortest path.
 92    """
 93
 94    __slots__ = ("best_hop", "via_seed")
 95
 96    def __init__(self, best_hop: int, via_seed: str) -> None:
 97        """Initialise provenance metadata for a graph node.
 98
 99        :param best_hop: Minimum hop distance from any seed node.
100        :param via_seed: ID of the seed node that yielded the shortest path.
101        """
102        self.best_hop = best_hop
103        self.via_seed = via_seed
104
105    def __repr__(self) -> str:
106        """Return a developer-readable representation of this ProvMeta.
107
108        :return: String of the form ``ProvMeta(best_hop=..., via_seed=...)``.
109        """
110        return f"ProvMeta(best_hop={self.best_hop}, via_seed={self.via_seed!r})"

Provenance metadata for a node returned by GraphStore.expand().

Parameters
  • best_hop: Minimum hop distance from any seed node.
  • via_seed: ID of the seed node that yielded the shortest path.
ProvMeta(best_hop: int, via_seed: str) 
 96    def __init__(self, best_hop: int, via_seed: str) -> None:
 97        """Initialise provenance metadata for a graph node.
 98
 99        :param best_hop: Minimum hop distance from any seed node.
100        :param via_seed: ID of the seed node that yielded the shortest path.
101        """
102        self.best_hop = best_hop
103        self.via_seed = via_seed

Initialise provenance metadata for a graph node.

Parameters
  • best_hop: Minimum hop distance from any seed node.
  • via_seed: ID of the seed node that yielded the shortest path.
best_hop
via_seed
DEFAULT_RELS = ('CONTAINS', 'CALLS', 'IMPORTS', 'INHERITS')
class Embedder: 
 96class Embedder:
 97    """
 98    Abstract embedding backend.
 99
100    Subclass and implement :meth:`embed_texts` to plug in any model.
101
102    :param dim: Embedding dimension (must be set by subclass ``__init__``).
103    """
104
105    dim: int
106
107    def embed_texts(self, texts: list[str]) -> list[list[float]]:
108        """
109        Embed a list of strings.
110
111        :param texts: Input strings.
112        :return: List of float32 vectors, one per input.
113        """
114        raise NotImplementedError
115
116    def embed_query(self, query: str) -> list[float]:
117        """
118        Embed a single query string.
119
120        Default implementation calls :meth:`embed_texts` with a one-element list.
121
122        :param query: Query string.
123        :return: Float32 vector.
124        """
125        return self.embed_texts([query])[0]

Abstract embedding backend.

Subclass and implement embed_texts() to plug in any model.

Parameters
  • dim: Embedding dimension (must be set by subclass __init__).
dim: int
def embed_texts(self, texts: list[str]) -> list[list[float]]: 
107    def embed_texts(self, texts: list[str]) -> list[list[float]]:
108        """
109        Embed a list of strings.
110
111        :param texts: Input strings.
112        :return: List of float32 vectors, one per input.
113        """
114        raise NotImplementedError

Embed a list of strings.

Parameters
  • texts: Input strings.
Returns

List of float32 vectors, one per input.

def embed_query(self, query: str) -> list[float]: 
116    def embed_query(self, query: str) -> list[float]:
117        """
118        Embed a single query string.
119
120        Default implementation calls :meth:`embed_texts` with a one-element list.
121
122        :param query: Query string.
123        :return: Float32 vector.
124        """
125        return self.embed_texts([query])[0]

Embed a single query string.

Default implementation calls embed_texts() with a one-element list.

Parameters
  • query: Query string.
Returns

Float32 vector.

class SentenceTransformerEmbedder(pycode_kg.Embedder): 
128class SentenceTransformerEmbedder(Embedder):
129    """
130    Local embedding via ``sentence-transformers``.
131
132    :param model_name: HuggingFace model name or local path.
133                       Defaults to :data:`~pycode_kg.pycodekg.DEFAULT_MODEL`.
134    """
135
136    def __init__(self, model_name: str = DEFAULT_MODEL) -> None:
137        """Load the sentence-transformer model.
138
139        :param model_name: HuggingFace model name or local path.
140        """
141        import os  # pylint: disable=import-outside-toplevel
142
143        from sentence_transformers import (  # pylint: disable=import-outside-toplevel
144            SentenceTransformer,
145        )
146        from transformers import logging as hf_logging  # pylint: disable=import-outside-toplevel
147
148        hf_logging.set_verbosity_error()
149
150        local_path = _local_model_path(model_name)
151        _prev_tqdm = os.environ.get("TQDM_DISABLE")
152        os.environ["TQDM_DISABLE"] = "1"
153        try:
154            if local_path.exists():
155                self.model = SentenceTransformer(str(local_path))
156            else:
157                try:
158                    self.model = SentenceTransformer(model_name, local_files_only=True)
159                except OSError:
160                    self.model = SentenceTransformer(model_name)
161        finally:
162            if _prev_tqdm is None:
163                os.environ.pop("TQDM_DISABLE", None)
164            else:
165                os.environ["TQDM_DISABLE"] = _prev_tqdm
166        self.model_name = model_name
167        self.dim: int = self.model.get_embedding_dimension() or 384
168
169    def embed_texts(self, texts: list[str]) -> list[list[float]]:
170        """Embed a list of strings into float32 vectors.
171
172        :param texts: Input strings to embed.
173        :return: List of float32 vectors, one per input string.
174        """
175        vecs = self.model.encode(texts, normalize_embeddings=True, show_progress_bar=False)
176        return [np.asarray(v, dtype="float32").tolist() for v in vecs]
177
178    def embed_query(self, query: str) -> list[float]:
179        """Embed a single query string into a float32 vector.
180
181        :param query: Query string to embed.
182        :return: Float32 vector representation of the query.
183        """
184        vec = self.model.encode([query], normalize_embeddings=True)[0]
185        return np.asarray(vec, dtype="float32").tolist()
186
187    def __repr__(self) -> str:
188        """Return a developer-readable representation of this embedder.
189
190        :return: String of the form ``SentenceTransformerEmbedder(model=..., dim=...)``.
191        """
192        return f"SentenceTransformerEmbedder(model={self.model_name!r}, dim={self.dim})"

Local embedding via sentence-transformers.

Parameters
  • model_name: HuggingFace model name or local path. Defaults to ~pycode_kg.pycodekg.DEFAULT_MODEL.
SentenceTransformerEmbedder(model_name: str = 'BAAI/bge-small-en-v1.5') 
136    def __init__(self, model_name: str = DEFAULT_MODEL) -> None:
137        """Load the sentence-transformer model.
138
139        :param model_name: HuggingFace model name or local path.
140        """
141        import os  # pylint: disable=import-outside-toplevel
142
143        from sentence_transformers import (  # pylint: disable=import-outside-toplevel
144            SentenceTransformer,
145        )
146        from transformers import logging as hf_logging  # pylint: disable=import-outside-toplevel
147
148        hf_logging.set_verbosity_error()
149
150        local_path = _local_model_path(model_name)
151        _prev_tqdm = os.environ.get("TQDM_DISABLE")
152        os.environ["TQDM_DISABLE"] = "1"
153        try:
154            if local_path.exists():
155                self.model = SentenceTransformer(str(local_path))
156            else:
157                try:
158                    self.model = SentenceTransformer(model_name, local_files_only=True)
159                except OSError:
160                    self.model = SentenceTransformer(model_name)
161        finally:
162            if _prev_tqdm is None:
163                os.environ.pop("TQDM_DISABLE", None)
164            else:
165                os.environ["TQDM_DISABLE"] = _prev_tqdm
166        self.model_name = model_name
167        self.dim: int = self.model.get_embedding_dimension() or 384

Load the sentence-transformer model.

Parameters
  • model_name: HuggingFace model name or local path.
model_name
dim: int
def embed_texts(self, texts: list[str]) -> list[list[float]]: 
169    def embed_texts(self, texts: list[str]) -> list[list[float]]:
170        """Embed a list of strings into float32 vectors.
171
172        :param texts: Input strings to embed.
173        :return: List of float32 vectors, one per input string.
174        """
175        vecs = self.model.encode(texts, normalize_embeddings=True, show_progress_bar=False)
176        return [np.asarray(v, dtype="float32").tolist() for v in vecs]

def embed_query(self, query: str) -> list[float]: 
178    def embed_query(self, query: str) -> list[float]:
179        """Embed a single query string into a float32 vector.
180
181        :param query: Query string to embed.
182        :return: Float32 vector representation of the query.
183        """
184        vec = self.model.encode([query], normalize_embeddings=True)[0]
185        return np.asarray(vec, dtype="float32").tolist()

Returns

class SemanticIndex: 
231class SemanticIndex:
232    """
233    LanceDB-backed semantic vector index for the Code Knowledge Graph.
234
235    Reads nodes from a :class:`~pycode_kg.store.GraphStore` (via its SQLite
236    database), embeds them, and stores the vectors in LanceDB.  The index
237    is **derived and disposable** — it can be rebuilt from SQLite at any
238    time without data loss.
239
240    Example::
241
242        embedder = SentenceTransformerEmbedder()
243        idx = SemanticIndex("./lancedb", embedder=embedder)
244        idx.build(store, wipe=True)
245
246        hits = idx.search("database connection setup", k=8)
247        for h in hits:
248            print(h.id, h.distance)
249
250    :param lancedb_dir: Directory for the LanceDB database.
251    :param embedder: Embedding backend.  Defaults to
252                     :class:`SentenceTransformerEmbedder` with
253                     :data:`~pycode_kg.pycodekg.DEFAULT_MODEL`.
254    :param table: LanceDB table name.  Defaults to ``"pycodekg_nodes"``.
255    :param index_kinds: Node kinds to embed.
256    """
257
258    def __init__(
259        self,
260        lancedb_dir: str | Path,
261        *,
262        embedder: Embedder | None = None,
263        table: str = _DEFAULT_TABLE,
264        index_kinds: Sequence[str] = _DEFAULT_KINDS,
265    ) -> None:
266        """Initialise the semantic index.
267
268        :param lancedb_dir: Directory for the LanceDB database.
269        :param embedder: Embedding backend. Defaults to :class:`SentenceTransformerEmbedder`.
270        :param table: LanceDB table name. Defaults to ``"pycodekg_nodes"``.
271        :param index_kinds: Node kinds to include in the index.
272        """
273        self.lancedb_dir = Path(lancedb_dir)
274        self.embedder: Embedder = embedder or SentenceTransformerEmbedder()
275        self.table_name = table
276        self.index_kinds = tuple(index_kinds)
277        self._tbl = None  # lazy LanceDB table handle
278
279    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
280    # Build
281    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
282
283    def build(
284        self,
285        store: GraphStore,
286        *,
287        wipe: bool = False,
288        batch_size: int = 256,
289        quiet: bool = True,
290    ) -> dict:
291        """
292        Build (or rebuild) the vector index from *store*.
293
294        :param store: Authoritative :class:`~pycode_kg.store.GraphStore`.
295        :param wipe: If ``True``, delete all existing vectors first.
296        :param batch_size: Number of nodes to embed per batch.
297        :param quiet: If ``True`` (default), suppress progress output from LanceDB and
298                      sentence-transformers during ingestion.
299        :return: Stats dict with ``indexed_rows``, ``dim``, ``table``,
300                 ``lancedb_dir``, ``kinds``.
301        """
302        if quiet:
303            suppress_ingestion_logging()
304
305        nodes = self._read_nodes(store)
306        tbl = self._open_table(wipe=wipe)
307
308        indexed = 0
309        for i in range(0, len(nodes), batch_size):
310            chunk = nodes[i : i + batch_size]
311            texts = [_build_index_text(n) for n in chunk]
312            vecs = self.embedder.embed_texts(texts)
313
314            # upsert: delete existing IDs then add fresh rows
315            ids = [n["id"] for n in chunk]
316            if ids:
317                pred = " OR ".join([f"id = '{_escape(nid)}'" for nid in ids])
318                tbl.delete(pred)
319
320            rows = [
321                {
322                    "id": n["id"],
323                    "kind": n["kind"],
324                    "name": n["name"],
325                    "qualname": n["qualname"] or "",
326                    "module_path": n["module_path"] or "",
327                    "text": text,
328                    "vector": vec,
329                }
330                for n, text, vec in zip(chunk, texts, vecs)
331            ]
332            tbl.add(rows)
333            indexed += len(rows)
334
335        self._tbl = tbl
336        return {
337            "indexed_rows": indexed,
338            "dim": self.embedder.dim,
339            "table": self.table_name,
340            "lancedb_dir": str(self.lancedb_dir),
341            "kinds": list(self.index_kinds),
342        }
343
344    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
345    # Search
346    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
347
348    def search(self, query: str, k: int = 8) -> list[SeedHit]:
349        """
350        Semantic vector search.
351
352        :param query: Natural-language query string.
353        :param k: Number of results to return.
354        :return: List of :class:`SeedHit` ordered by ascending distance.
355        """
356        tbl = self._get_table()
357        qvec = self.embedder.embed_query(query)
358        raw = tbl.search(qvec).limit(k).to_list()
359
360        hits: list[SeedHit] = []
361        for rank, row in enumerate(raw):
362            dist = _extract_distance(row, rank)
363            hits.append(
364                SeedHit(
365                    id=row["id"],
366                    kind=row.get("kind", ""),
367                    name=row.get("name", ""),
368                    qualname=row.get("qualname", ""),
369                    module_path=row.get("module_path", ""),
370                    distance=dist,
371                    rank=rank,
372                )
373            )
374        return hits
375
376    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
377    # Internal helpers
378    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
379
380    def _read_nodes(self, store: GraphStore) -> list[dict]:
381        """Read indexable nodes from the store filtered by ``index_kinds``.
382
383        :param store: Authoritative :class:`~pycode_kg.store.GraphStore` to query.
384        :return: List of node dicts for kinds in :attr:`index_kinds`.
385        """
386        return store.query_nodes(kinds=list(self.index_kinds))
387
388    def _open_table(self, *, wipe: bool = False):
389        """Open the LanceDB table, creating it with the correct schema if absent.
390
391        :param wipe: If ``True``, delete all existing rows after opening.
392        :return: LanceDB table handle.
393        """
394        import lancedb  # pylint: disable=import-outside-toplevel
395
396        self.lancedb_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
397        db = lancedb.connect(str(self.lancedb_dir))  # type: ignore[attr-defined]
398
399        if self.table_name in db.list_tables().tables:
400            if wipe:
401                db.drop_table(self.table_name)
402            else:
403                try:
404                    return db.open_table(self.table_name)
405                except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001  # pylint: disable=broad-exception-caught
406                    # Table directory exists on disk but is corrupt/incomplete
407                    # (e.g. from a previously interrupted build — empty _versions
408                    # or data directories).  Drop and recreate it cleanly.
409                    logging.getLogger(__name__).warning(
410                        "LanceDB table %r appears corrupt (%s); dropping and recreating.",
411                        self.table_name,
412                        exc,
413                    )
414                    db.drop_table(self.table_name)
415
416        # Create with a dummy row to establish schema, then remove it
417        dummy = {
418            "id": "__dummy__",
419            "kind": "dummy",
420            "name": "__dummy__",
421            "qualname": "",
422            "module_path": "",
423            "text": "__dummy__",
424            "vector": np.zeros((self.embedder.dim,), dtype="float32").tolist(),
425        }
426        tbl = db.create_table(self.table_name, data=[dummy])
427        tbl.delete("id = '__dummy__'")
428        return tbl
429
430    def _get_table(self):
431        """Return the cached LanceDB table handle, opening it if not yet loaded.
432
433        :return: LanceDB table handle.
434        """
435        if self._tbl is None:
436            import lancedb  # pylint: disable=import-outside-toplevel
437
438            db = lancedb.connect(str(self.lancedb_dir))  # type: ignore[attr-defined]
439            self._tbl = db.open_table(self.table_name)
440        return self._tbl
441
442    def __repr__(self) -> str:
443        """Return a developer-readable representation of this SemanticIndex.
444
445        :return: String including lancedb_dir, table name, and embedder details.
446        """
447        return (
448            f"SemanticIndex(lancedb_dir={self.lancedb_dir!r}, "
449            f"table={self.table_name!r}, embedder={self.embedder!r})"
450        )

LanceDB-backed semantic vector index for the Code Knowledge Graph.

Reads nodes from a ~pycode_kg.store.GraphStore (via its SQLite database), embeds them, and stores the vectors in LanceDB. The index is derived and disposable — it can be rebuilt from SQLite at any time without data loss.

Example::

embedder = SentenceTransformerEmbedder()
idx = SemanticIndex("./lancedb", embedder=embedder)
idx.build(store, wipe=True)

hits = idx.search("database connection setup", k=8)
for h in hits:
    print(h.id, h.distance)
Parameters
  • lancedb_dir: Directory for the LanceDB database.
  • embedder: Embedding backend. Defaults to SentenceTransformerEmbedder with ~pycode_kg.pycodekg.DEFAULT_MODEL.
  • table: LanceDB table name. Defaults to "pycodekg_nodes".
  • index_kinds: Node kinds to embed.
SemanticIndex( lancedb_dir: str | pathlib.Path, *, embedder: Embedder | None = None, table: str = 'pycodekg_nodes', index_kinds: Sequence[str] = ('module', 'class', 'function', 'method')) 
258    def __init__(
259        self,
260        lancedb_dir: str | Path,
261        *,
262        embedder: Embedder | None = None,
263        table: str = _DEFAULT_TABLE,
264        index_kinds: Sequence[str] = _DEFAULT_KINDS,
265    ) -> None:
266        """Initialise the semantic index.
267
268        :param lancedb_dir: Directory for the LanceDB database.
269        :param embedder: Embedding backend. Defaults to :class:`SentenceTransformerEmbedder`.
270        :param table: LanceDB table name. Defaults to ``"pycodekg_nodes"``.
271        :param index_kinds: Node kinds to include in the index.
272        """
273        self.lancedb_dir = Path(lancedb_dir)
274        self.embedder: Embedder = embedder or SentenceTransformerEmbedder()
275        self.table_name = table
276        self.index_kinds = tuple(index_kinds)
277        self._tbl = None  # lazy LanceDB table handle

Initialise the semantic index.

Parameters
  • lancedb_dir: Directory for the LanceDB database.
  • embedder: Embedding backend. Defaults to SentenceTransformerEmbedder.
  • table: LanceDB table name. Defaults to "pycodekg_nodes".
  • index_kinds: Node kinds to include in the index.
lancedb_dir
embedder: Embedder
table_name
index_kinds
def build( self, store: GraphStore, *, wipe: bool = False, batch_size: int = 256, quiet: bool = True) -> dict: 
283    def build(
284        self,
285        store: GraphStore,
286        *,
287        wipe: bool = False,
288        batch_size: int = 256,
289        quiet: bool = True,
290    ) -> dict:
291        """
292        Build (or rebuild) the vector index from *store*.
293
294        :param store: Authoritative :class:`~pycode_kg.store.GraphStore`.
295        :param wipe: If ``True``, delete all existing vectors first.
296        :param batch_size: Number of nodes to embed per batch.
297        :param quiet: If ``True`` (default), suppress progress output from LanceDB and
298                      sentence-transformers during ingestion.
299        :return: Stats dict with ``indexed_rows``, ``dim``, ``table``,
300                 ``lancedb_dir``, ``kinds``.
301        """
302        if quiet:
303            suppress_ingestion_logging()
304
305        nodes = self._read_nodes(store)
306        tbl = self._open_table(wipe=wipe)
307
308        indexed = 0
309        for i in range(0, len(nodes), batch_size):
310            chunk = nodes[i : i + batch_size]
311            texts = [_build_index_text(n) for n in chunk]
312            vecs = self.embedder.embed_texts(texts)
313
314            # upsert: delete existing IDs then add fresh rows
315            ids = [n["id"] for n in chunk]
316            if ids:
317                pred = " OR ".join([f"id = '{_escape(nid)}'" for nid in ids])
318                tbl.delete(pred)
319
320            rows = [
321                {
322                    "id": n["id"],
323                    "kind": n["kind"],
324                    "name": n["name"],
325                    "qualname": n["qualname"] or "",
326                    "module_path": n["module_path"] or "",
327                    "text": text,
328                    "vector": vec,
329                }
330                for n, text, vec in zip(chunk, texts, vecs)
331            ]
332            tbl.add(rows)
333            indexed += len(rows)
334
335        self._tbl = tbl
336        return {
337            "indexed_rows": indexed,
338            "dim": self.embedder.dim,
339            "table": self.table_name,
340            "lancedb_dir": str(self.lancedb_dir),
341            "kinds": list(self.index_kinds),
342        }

Build (or rebuild) the vector index from store.

Parameters
  • store: Authoritative ~pycode_kg.store.GraphStore.
  • wipe: If True, delete all existing vectors first.
  • batch_size: Number of nodes to embed per batch.
