  • quiet: If True (default), suppress progress output from LanceDB and sentence-transformers during ingestion.
Returns

Stats dict with indexed_rows, dim, table, lancedb_dir, kinds.

def search(self, query: str, k: int = 8) -> list[SeedHit]: 
348    def search(self, query: str, k: int = 8) -> list[SeedHit]:
349        """
350        Semantic vector search.
351
352        :param query: Natural-language query string.
353        :param k: Number of results to return.
354        :return: List of :class:`SeedHit` ordered by ascending distance.
355        """
356        tbl = self._get_table()
357        qvec = self.embedder.embed_query(query)
358        raw = tbl.search(qvec).limit(k).to_list()
359
360        hits: list[SeedHit] = []
361        for rank, row in enumerate(raw):
362            dist = _extract_distance(row, rank)
363            hits.append(
364                SeedHit(
365                    id=row["id"],
366                    kind=row.get("kind", ""),
367                    name=row.get("name", ""),
368                    qualname=row.get("qualname", ""),
369                    module_path=row.get("module_path", ""),
370                    distance=dist,
371                    rank=rank,
372                )
373            )
374        return hits

Semantic vector search.

Parameters
  • query: Natural-language query string.
  • k: Number of results to return.
Returns

List of SeedHit ordered by ascending distance.

@dataclass
class SeedHit: 
200@dataclass
201class SeedHit:
202    """
203    A single result from a semantic vector search.
204
205    :param id: Node ID.
206    :param kind: Node kind (``module``, ``class``, ``function``, ``method``).
207    :param name: Short name.
208    :param qualname: Qualified name.
209    :param module_path: Repo-relative module path.
210    :param distance: Vector distance (lower = more similar).
211    :param rank: Zero-based rank in the result list.
212    """
213
214    id: str
215    kind: str
216    name: str
217    qualname: str
218    module_path: str
219    distance: float
220    rank: int

A single result from a semantic vector search.

Parameters
  • id: Node ID.
  • kind: Node kind (module, class, function, method).
  • name: Short name.
  • qualname: Qualified name.
  • module_path: Repo-relative module path.
  • distance: Vector distance (lower = more similar).
  • rank: Zero-based rank in the result list.
SeedHit( id: str, kind: str, name: str, qualname: str, module_path: str, distance: float, rank: int)
id: str
kind: str
name: str
qualname: str
module_path: str
distance: float
rank: int
class KGModule(abc.ABC): 
102class KGModule(ABC):
103    """Abstract base class for a production-grade knowledge graph module.
104
105    Provides the full build/query/pack infrastructure so domain authors
106    only need to implement :meth:`make_extractor` and :meth:`kind`.
107
108    Typical usage by a domain author::
109
110        class TypeScriptKG(KGModule):
111            def make_extractor(self):
112                return TypeScriptExtractor(self.repo_root)
113            def kind(self):
114                return "code"
115            def analyze(self):
116                return "# TypeScript KG Analysis\\n..."
117
118        kg = TypeScriptKG("/path/to/ts-repo")
119        kg.build(wipe=True)
120        result = kg.query("authentication middleware")
121        pack = kg.pack("error handling")
122
123    :param repo_root: Repository root directory.
124    :param db_path: SQLite database path (defaults to ``.<kind>kg/graph.sqlite``
125                    under ``repo_root``; set ``_default_dir`` in subclass to override).
126    :param lancedb_dir: LanceDB directory (defaults to ``.<kind>kg/lancedb``).
127    :param model: Sentence-transformer model name for embedding.
128    :param table: LanceDB table name.
129    """
130
131    #: Override in subclass to change the default artefact directory name.
132    #: e.g. ``_default_dir = ".tskg"`` for a TypeScript KG.
133    _default_dir: str = ".pycodekg"
134
135    def __init__(
136        self,
137        repo_root: str | Path,
138        db_path: str | Path | None = None,
139        lancedb_dir: str | Path | None = None,
140        *,
141        model: str = DEFAULT_MODEL,
142        table: str = "kg_nodes",
143    ) -> None:
144        """Initialise KGModule and resolve all paths.
145
146        :param repo_root: Repository root directory; resolved to an absolute path.
147        :param db_path: Path to the SQLite database file.
148        :param lancedb_dir: Directory used by LanceDB for the vector index.
149        :param model: Sentence-transformer model name.
150        :param table: LanceDB table name.
151        """
152        self.repo_root = Path(repo_root).resolve()
153        _dir = self.repo_root / self._default_dir
154        self.db_path = Path(db_path) if db_path is not None else _dir / "graph.sqlite"
155        self.lancedb_dir = Path(lancedb_dir) if lancedb_dir is not None else _dir / "lancedb"
156        self.model_name = model
157        self.table_name = table
158
159        # Lazy-initialised layers
160        self._store: GraphStore | None = None
161        self._index: SemanticIndex | None = None
162        self._embedder: Embedder | None = None
163
164    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
165    # Abstract interface — domain authors implement these
166    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
167
168    @abstractmethod
169    def make_extractor(self) -> KGExtractor:
170        """Return the domain-specific extractor for this module.
171
172        Called once per :meth:`build_graph` invocation.  The extractor
173        determines what nodes and edges are harvested from the source.
174
175        :return: A :class:`~pycode_kg.module.extractor.KGExtractor` instance.
176        """
177
178    @abstractmethod
179    def kind(self) -> str:
180        """Return the KG kind string for this module.
181
182        For code KGs return ``"code"``, for document KGs ``"doc"``, for
183        domain-specific KGs ``"meta"`` (or register a new value in
184        ``kg_rag.primitives.KGKind``).
185
186        :return: Kind string used in registry entries and MCP tool names.
187        """
188
189    @abstractmethod
190    def analyze(self) -> str:
191        """Run a full analysis of this KG and return a Markdown report.
192
193        The report format is domain-specific but must:
194
195        1. Begin with ``# <KG Type> Analysis Report``
196        2. Include the KG name/path for identification
197        3. Include structural metrics (node count, edge count)
198        4. Include domain-appropriate quality signals
199
200        Must not raise — return a Markdown error message on failure.
201
202        :return: Markdown-formatted analysis report string.
203        """
204
205    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
206    # Layer accessors (lazy init)
207    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
208
209    @property
210    def store(self) -> GraphStore:
211        """SQLite persistence layer (lazy-initialised)."""
212        if self._store is None:
213            self._store = GraphStore(self.db_path)
214        return self._store
215
216    @property
217    def embedder(self) -> Embedder:
218        """Embedding backend (lazy-initialised, shared between index and query)."""
219        if self._embedder is None:
220            suppress_ingestion_logging()
221            self._embedder = SentenceTransformerEmbedder(self.model_name)
222        return self._embedder
223
224    @property
225    def index(self) -> SemanticIndex:
226        """LanceDB semantic index (lazy-initialised)."""
227        if self._index is None:
228            extractor = self.make_extractor()
229            self._index = SemanticIndex(
230                self.lancedb_dir,
231                embedder=self.embedder,
232                table=self.table_name,
233                index_kinds=extractor.meaningful_node_kinds(),
234            )
235        return self._index
236
237    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
238    # Build
239    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
240
241    def build(self, *, wipe: bool = False) -> BuildStats:
242        """Full pipeline: extraction → SQLite → LanceDB.
243
244        :param wipe: Clear existing data before writing.
245        :return: :class:`~pycode_kg.module.types.BuildStats`.
246        """
247        graph_stats = self.build_graph(wipe=wipe)
248        index_stats = self.build_index(wipe=wipe)
249        graph_stats.indexed_rows = index_stats.indexed_rows
250        graph_stats.index_dim = index_stats.index_dim
251        return graph_stats
252
253    def build_graph(self, *, wipe: bool = False) -> BuildStats:
254        """Extraction → SQLite only (no vector indexing).
255
256        Calls :meth:`make_extractor`, drains the iterator into two lists
257        of :class:`~pycode_kg.module.extractor.NodeSpec` and
258        :class:`~pycode_kg.module.extractor.EdgeSpec`, converts them to the
259        v0 :class:`~pycode_kg.pycodekg.Node` / :class:`~pycode_kg.pycodekg.Edge`
260        primitives, writes to SQLite, then calls :meth:`_post_build_hook`
261        for any domain-specific post-processing (e.g. symbol resolution).
262
263        :param wipe: Clear existing graph before writing.
264        :return: :class:`~pycode_kg.module.types.BuildStats`
265                 (``indexed_rows`` will be ``None``).
266        """
267        extractor = self.make_extractor()
268        node_specs: list[NodeSpec] = []
269        edge_specs: list[EdgeSpec] = []
270        for item in extractor.extract():
271            if isinstance(item, NodeSpec):
272                node_specs.append(item)
273            else:
274                edge_specs.append(item)
275
276        nodes = [_nodespec_to_node(s) for s in node_specs]
277        edges = [_edgespec_to_edge(s) for s in edge_specs]
278
279        self.store.write(nodes, edges, wipe=wipe)
280        self._post_build_hook(self.store)
281
282        s = self.store.stats()
283        return BuildStats(
284            repo_root=str(self.repo_root),
285            db_path=str(self.db_path),
286            total_nodes=s["total_nodes"],
287            total_edges=s["total_edges"],
288            node_counts=s["node_counts"],
289            edge_counts=s["edge_counts"],
290        )
291
292    def build_index(self, *, wipe: bool = False) -> BuildStats:
293        """SQLite → LanceDB only (graph must already exist).
294
295        :param wipe: Delete existing vectors before indexing.
296        :return: :class:`~pycode_kg.module.types.BuildStats` with
297                 ``indexed_rows`` and ``index_dim`` set.
298        """
299        idx_stats = self.index.build(self.store, wipe=wipe)
300        s = self.store.stats()
301        return BuildStats(
302            repo_root=str(self.repo_root),
303            db_path=str(self.db_path),
304            total_nodes=s["total_nodes"],
305            total_edges=s["total_edges"],
306            node_counts=s["node_counts"],
307            edge_counts=s["edge_counts"],
308            indexed_rows=idx_stats["indexed_rows"],
309            index_dim=idx_stats["dim"],
310        )
311
312    def _post_build_hook(self, store: GraphStore) -> None:
313        """Hook called after ``store.write()`` in :meth:`build_graph`.
314
315        Override in subclasses for domain-specific post-processing, e.g.
316        symbol resolution (``store.resolve_symbols()`` for Python KGs).
317
318        :param store: The :class:`~pycode_kg.store.GraphStore` just written to.
319        """
320
321    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
322    # Query
323    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
324
325    def query(
326        self,
327        q: str,
328        *,
329        k: int = 8,
330        hop: int = 1,
331        rels: tuple[str, ...] = DEFAULT_RELS,
332        include_symbols: bool = False,
333        max_nodes: int = 25,
334        min_score: float = 0.0,
335        max_per_module: int | None = None,
336        rerank_mode: str = "legacy",
337        rerank_semantic_weight: float = 0.7,
338        rerank_lexical_weight: float = 0.3,
339    ) -> QueryResult:
340        """Hybrid query: semantic seeding + structural graph expansion.
341
342        Seeds with vector ANN search (LanceDB), expands through the graph
343        (SQLite) for ``hop`` hops along ``rels`` edge types, then reranks
344        results by the selected strategy.
345
346        :param q: Natural-language query.
347        :param k: Top-K semantic hits.
348        :param hop: Graph expansion hops (0 = pure semantic).
349        :param rels: Edge types to follow during expansion.
350        :param include_symbols: Include unresolved stub nodes in results.
351        :param max_nodes: Maximum nodes to return.
352        :param min_score: Minimum semantic score for seed inclusion.
353        :param max_per_module: Optional cap on returned nodes per module path.
354        :param rerank_mode: ``'legacy'`` | ``'semantic'`` | ``'hybrid'``.
355        :param rerank_semantic_weight: Semantic weight for ``'hybrid'`` mode.
356        :param rerank_lexical_weight: Lexical weight for ``'hybrid'`` mode.
357        :return: :class:`~pycode_kg.module.types.QueryResult`.
358        """
359        q_norm = normalize_query_text(q)
360        hits = self.index.search(q_norm, k=k)
361        if min_score > 0.0:
362            hits = [h for h in hits if semantic_score_from_distance(h.distance) >= min_score]
363        seed_ids: set[str] = {h.id for h in hits}
364        seed_rank: dict[str, float] = {h.id: h.distance for h in hits}
365        q_toks = query_tokens(q_norm)
366
367        meta = self.store.expand(seed_ids, hop=hop, rels=rels)
368        all_ids = set(meta.keys())
369
370        nodes: list[dict] = []
371        kept_ids: set[str] = set()
372        module_counts: dict[str, int] = {}
373
374        def _rank_key(nid: str) -> tuple[float, float, int, int, str]:
375            prov = meta[nid]
376            dist = seed_rank.get(prov.via_seed, 1e9)
377            n = self.store.node(nid)
378            kind = n["kind"] if n else "symbol"
379            sem = semantic_score_from_distance(dist)
380            lex = lexical_overlap_score(q_toks, n or {})
381            hybrid = (
382                rerank_semantic_weight * sem + rerank_lexical_weight * lex
383                if (rerank_semantic_weight + rerank_lexical_weight) > 0
384                else sem
385            )
386            if rerank_mode == "hybrid":
387                return (-hybrid, float(prov.best_hop), self._kind_priority(kind), 0, nid)
388            if rerank_mode == "semantic":
389                return (-sem, float(prov.best_hop), self._kind_priority(kind), 0, nid)
390            return (float(prov.best_hop), dist, self._kind_priority(kind), 0, nid)
391
392        for nid in sorted(all_ids, key=_rank_key):
393            if len(nodes) >= max_nodes:
394                break
395            n = self.store.node(nid)
396            if not n:
397                continue
398            if not include_symbols and n["kind"] == "symbol":
399                continue
400            module_path = n.get("module_path") or ""
401            if max_per_module is not None and module_path:
402                if module_counts.get(module_path, 0) >= max_per_module:
403                    continue
404                module_counts[module_path] = module_counts.get(module_path, 0) + 1
405
406            prov = meta[nid]
407            dist = seed_rank.get(prov.via_seed, 1e9)
408            sem = semantic_score_from_distance(dist)
409            lex = lexical_overlap_score(q_toks, n)
410            n["relevance"] = {
411                "score": (
412                    rerank_semantic_weight * sem + rerank_lexical_weight * lex
413                    if rerank_mode == "hybrid"
414                    else sem
415                ),
416                "semantic": sem,
417                "lexical": lex,
418                "docstring_signal": docstring_signal(n.get("docstring")),
419                "hop": prov.best_hop,
420                "via_seed": prov.via_seed,
421                "mode": rerank_mode,
422            }
423            kept_ids.add(nid)
424            nodes.append(n)
425
426        edges = self.store.edges_within(kept_ids)
427        return QueryResult(
428            query=q,
429            seeds=len(seed_ids),
430            expanded_nodes=len(all_ids),
431            returned_nodes=len(nodes),
432            hop=hop,
433            rels=list(rels),
434            nodes=nodes,
435            edges=edges,
436        )
437
438    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
439    # Snippet pack
440    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
441
442    def pack(
443        self,
444        q: str,
445        *,
446        k: int = 8,
447        hop: int = 1,
448        rels: tuple[str, ...] = DEFAULT_RELS,
449        include_symbols: bool = False,
450        context: int = 5,
451        max_lines: int = 60,
452        max_nodes: int | None = 15,
453        min_score: float = 0.0,
454        max_per_module: int | None = None,
455        rerank_mode: str = "legacy",
456        rerank_semantic_weight: float = 0.7,
457        rerank_lexical_weight: float = 0.3,
458        missing_lineno_policy: str = "cap_or_skip",
459    ) -> SnippetPack:
460        """Hybrid query + source-grounded snippet extraction.
461
462        :param q: Natural-language query.
463        :param k: Top-K semantic hits.
464        :param hop: Graph expansion hops.
465        :param rels: Edge types to follow during expansion.
466        :param include_symbols: Include unresolved stub nodes.
467        :param context: Extra context lines around each definition span.
468        :param max_lines: Maximum lines per snippet block.
469        :param max_nodes: Maximum nodes to return (``None`` = no limit).
470        :param min_score: Minimum semantic score for seed inclusion.
471        :param max_per_module: Optional cap on returned nodes per module.
472        :param rerank_mode: ``'legacy'`` | ``'semantic'`` | ``'hybrid'``.
473        :param rerank_semantic_weight: Semantic weight for ``'hybrid'`` mode.
474        :param rerank_lexical_weight: Lexical weight for ``'hybrid'`` mode.
475        :param missing_lineno_policy: ``'cap_or_skip'`` (default) or ``'legacy'``.
476        :return: :class:`~pycode_kg.module.types.SnippetPack`.
477        """
478        q_norm = normalize_query_text(q)
479        hits = self.index.search(q_norm, k=k)
480        if min_score > 0.0:
481            hits = [h for h in hits if semantic_score_from_distance(h.distance) >= min_score]
482        seed_rank: dict[str, dict] = {h.id: {"rank": h.rank, "dist": h.distance} for h in hits}
483        seed_ids: set[str] = set(seed_rank.keys())
484        q_toks = query_tokens(q_norm)
485        warnings: list[str] = []
486
487        meta = self.store.expand(seed_ids, hop=hop, rels=rels)
488        all_ids = set(meta.keys())
489
490        raw_nodes: list[dict] = []
491        for nid in sorted(all_ids):
492            n = self.store.node(nid)
493            if not n:
494                continue
495            if not include_symbols and n["kind"] == "symbol":
496                continue
497
498            prov = meta[nid]
499            base_dist = seed_rank.get(prov.via_seed, {"dist": 1e9})["dist"]
500            kind_pri = self._kind_priority(n["kind"])
501            sem = semantic_score_from_distance(base_dist)
502            lex = lexical_overlap_score(q_toks, n)
503            hybrid = (
504                rerank_semantic_weight * sem + rerank_lexical_weight * lex
505                if (rerank_semantic_weight + rerank_lexical_weight) > 0
506                else sem
507            )
508            if rerank_mode == "hybrid":
509                n["_rank_key"] = (-hybrid, float(prov.best_hop), kind_pri, 0, n["id"])
510            elif rerank_mode == "semantic":
511                n["_rank_key"] = (-sem, float(prov.best_hop), kind_pri, 0, n["id"])
512            else:
513                n["_rank_key"] = (float(prov.best_hop), base_dist, kind_pri, 0, n["id"])
514            n["_best_hop"] = prov.best_hop
515            n["_via_seed"] = prov.via_seed
516            n["relevance"] = {
517                "score": hybrid if rerank_mode == "hybrid" else sem,
518                "semantic": sem,
519                "lexical": lex,
520                "docstring_signal": docstring_signal(n.get("docstring")),
521                "hop": prov.best_hop,
522                "via_seed": prov.via_seed,
523                "mode": rerank_mode,
524            }
525            raw_nodes.append(n)
526
527        # Attach spans (needed for dedup)
528        file_cache: dict[str, list[str]] = {}
529        spans_by_qualname: dict[tuple[str, str], tuple[int, int]] = {}
530        for n in raw_nodes:
531            mp = n.get("module_path")
532            if not mp:
533                n["_span"] = None
534                continue
535            if mp not in file_cache:
536                file_cache[mp] = read_lines(safe_join(self.repo_root, mp))
537            lines = file_cache[mp]
538            lineno = n.get("lineno")
539            if n.get("kind") != "module" and lineno is None and missing_lineno_policy != "legacy":
540                qualname = n.get("qualname") or ""
541                parent_span = None
542                if qualname and "." in qualname:
543                    parent_key = (mp, qualname.rsplit(".", 1)[0])
544                    parent_span = spans_by_qualname.get(parent_key)
545                if parent_span:
546                    fallback_cap = min(max_lines, max(20, context * 4))
547                    p_start, p_end = parent_span
548                    capped_end = min(p_end, p_start + fallback_cap - 1)
549                    n["_span"] = (p_start, capped_end)
550                    warnings.append(
551                        f"Missing line metadata for `{n['id']}`; "
552                        f"using capped parent span {p_start}-{capped_end}."
553                    )
554                else:
555                    n["_span"] = None
556                    warnings.append(
557                        f"Missing line metadata for `{n['id']}`; "
558                        "snippet omitted (no parent span available)."
559                    )
560            else:
561                n["_span"] = compute_span(
562                    n["kind"],
563                    lineno,
564                    n.get("end_lineno"),
565                    context=context,
566                    max_lines=max_lines,
567                    file_nlines=len(lines),
568                )
569            if n.get("qualname") and n.get("_span"):
570                spans_by_qualname[(mp, n["qualname"])] = n["_span"]
571
572        raw_nodes.sort(key=lambda x: x["_rank_key"])
573
574        # Deduplicate by file + overlapping span
575        kept: list[dict] = []
576        kept_by_file: dict[str, list[tuple[tuple[int, int], str]]] = {}
577        module_counts: dict[str, int] = {}
578
579        for n in raw_nodes:
580            if max_nodes is not None and len(kept) >= max_nodes:
581                break
582            mp = n.get("module_path") or ""
583            span = n.get("_span")
584
585            if not mp or not span or span[1] < span[0]:
586                kept.append(n)
587                continue
588
589            if any(spans_overlap(span, s2) for s2, _ in kept_by_file.get(mp, [])):
590                continue
591
592            if max_per_module is not None and mp:
593                if module_counts.get(mp, 0) >= max_per_module:
594                    continue
595                module_counts[mp] = module_counts.get(mp, 0) + 1
596
597            kept.append(n)
598            kept_by_file.setdefault(mp, []).append((span, n["id"]))
599
600        kept_ids: set[str] = {n["id"] for n in kept}
601        edges = self.store.edges_within(kept_ids)
602
603        # Attach snippets
604        for n in kept:
605            mp = n.get("module_path")
606            span = n.get("_span")
607            if not mp or not span:
608                continue
609            if mp not in file_cache:
610                file_cache[mp] = read_lines(safe_join(self.repo_root, mp))
611            lines = file_cache[mp]
612            start, end = span
613
614            if n.get("kind") == "module" and len(lines) > max_lines:
615                contained = [
616                    self.store.node(cn_id)
617                    for cn_id in (
618                        row[0]
619                        for row in self.store.con.execute(
620                            "SELECT dst FROM edges WHERE src = ? AND rel = 'CONTAINS'",
621                            (n["id"],),
622                        ).fetchall()
623                    )
624                    if self.store.node(cn_id) is not None
625                ]
626                contained_nodes: list[dict] = [c for c in contained if c is not None]
627                n["snippet"] = make_module_summary(
628                    mp, lines, n.get("docstring"), contained_nodes, max_lines
629                )
630            elif end >= start and lines:
631                n["snippet"] = make_snippet(mp, lines, start, end)
632
633        # Strip internal keys
634        for n in kept:
635            for key in [k for k in n if k.startswith("_")]:
636                del n[key]
637
638        return SnippetPack(
639            query=q,
640            seeds=len(seed_ids),
641            expanded_nodes=len(all_ids),
642            returned_nodes=len(kept),
643            hop=hop,
644            rels=list(rels),
645            model=self.model_name,
646            nodes=kept,
647            edges=edges,
648            warnings=warnings,
649        )
650
651    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
652    # Convenience
653    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
654
655    def callers(self, node_id: str, *, rel: str = "CALLS") -> list[dict]:
656        """Return all nodes that call *node_id*, resolving through stubs.
657
658        :param node_id: Target node identifier.
659        :param rel: Relation type to invert (default ``"CALLS"``).
660        :return: Deduplicated list of caller node dicts.
661        """
662        return self.store.callers_of(node_id, rel=rel)
663
664    def stats(self) -> dict:
665        """Return store statistics (node/edge counts by kind/relation).
666
667        :return: Dictionary from :meth:`~pycode_kg.store.GraphStore.stats`.
668        """
669        return self.store.stats()
670
671    def node(self, node_id: str) -> dict | None:
672        """Fetch a single node by ID from the store.
673
674        :param node_id: Stable node identifier.
675        :return: Node dict or ``None`` if not found.
676        """
677        return self.store.node(node_id)
678
679    def close(self) -> None:
680        """Close the underlying SQLite connection."""
681        if self._store is not None:
682            self._store.close()
683
684    def __enter__(self) -> KGModule:
685        """Enter the runtime context.
686
687        :return: This :class:`KGModule` instance.
688        """
689        return self
690
691    def __exit__(self, *_: object) -> None:
692        """Exit the runtime context and close the SQLite connection."""
693        self.close()
694
695    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
696    # Domain hook — subclasses override for node kind ordering
697    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
698
699    def _kind_priority(self, kind: str) -> int:
700        """Return the sort priority for a node kind (lower = higher priority).
701
702        Used as a tiebreaker in query/pack ranking when scores are equal.
703        Default: all unknown kinds sort last (priority 99).
704
705        Override in subclasses for domain-specific ordering::
706
707            def _kind_priority(self, kind):
708                return {"function": 0, "class": 1, "module": 2}.get(kind, 99)
709
710        :param kind: Node kind string.
711        :return: Integer priority (lower = ranked first).
712        """
713        return 99

Abstract base class for a production-grade knowledge graph module.

Provides the full build/query/pack infrastructure so domain authors only need to implement make_extractor() and kind().

Typical usage by a domain author::

class TypeScriptKG(KGModule):
    def make_extractor(self):
        return TypeScriptExtractor(self.repo_root)
    def kind(self):
        return "code"
    def analyze(self):
        return "# TypeScript KG Analysis\n..."

kg = TypeScriptKG("/path/to/ts-repo")
kg.build(wipe=True)
result = kg.query("authentication middleware")
pack = kg.pack("error handling")
Parameters
  • repo_root: Repository root directory.
  • db_path: SQLite database path (defaults to .<kind>kg/graph.sqlite under repo_root; set _default_dir in subclass to override).
  • lancedb_dir: LanceDB directory (defaults to .<kind>kg/lancedb).
  • model: Sentence-transformer model name for embedding.
  • table: LanceDB table name.
KGModule( repo_root: str | pathlib.Path, db_path: str | pathlib.Path | None = None, lancedb_dir: str | pathlib.Path | None = None, *, model: str = 'BAAI/bge-small-en-v1.5', table: str = 'kg_nodes') 
135    def __init__(
136        self,
137        repo_root: str | Path,
138        db_path: str | Path | None = None,
139        lancedb_dir: str | Path | None = None,
140        *,
141        model: str = DEFAULT_MODEL,
142        table: str = "kg_nodes",
143    ) -> None:
144        """Initialise KGModule and resolve all paths.
145
146        :param repo_root: Repository root directory; resolved to an absolute path.
147        :param db_path: Path to the SQLite database file.
148        :param lancedb_dir: Directory used by LanceDB for the vector index.
149        :param model: Sentence-transformer model name.
150        :param table: LanceDB table name.
151        """
152        self.repo_root = Path(repo_root).resolve()
153        _dir = self.repo_root / self._default_dir
154        self.db_path = Path(db_path) if db_path is not None else _dir / "graph.sqlite"
155        self.lancedb_dir = Path(lancedb_dir) if lancedb_dir is not None else _dir / "lancedb"
156        self.model_name = model
157        self.table_name = table
158
159        # Lazy-initialised layers
160        self._store: GraphStore | None = None
161        self._index: SemanticIndex | None = None
162        self._embedder: Embedder | None = None

Initialise KGModule and resolve all paths.

Parameters
  • repo_root: Repository root directory; resolved to an absolute path.
  • db_path: Path to the SQLite database file.
  • lancedb_dir: Directory used by LanceDB for the vector index.
  • model: Sentence-transformer model name.
  • table: LanceDB table name.
repo_root
db_path
lancedb_dir
model_name
table_name
@abstractmethod
def make_extractor(self) -> KGExtractor: 
168    @abstractmethod
169    def make_extractor(self) -> KGExtractor:
170        """Return the domain-specific extractor for this module.
171
172        Called once per :meth:`build_graph` invocation.  The extractor
173        determines what nodes and edges are harvested from the source.
174
175        :return: A :class:`~pycode_kg.module.extractor.KGExtractor` instance.
176        """

Return the domain-specific extractor for this module.

Called once per build_graph() invocation. The extractor determines what nodes and edges are harvested from the source.

Returns

A ~pycode_kg.module.extractor.KGExtractor instance.

@abstractmethod
def kind(self) -> str: 
178    @abstractmethod
179    def kind(self) -> str:
180        """Return the KG kind string for this module.
181
182        For code KGs return ``"code"``, for document KGs ``"doc"``, for
183        domain-specific KGs ``"meta"`` (or register a new value in
184        ``kg_rag.primitives.KGKind``).
185
186        :return: Kind string used in registry entries and MCP tool names.
187        """

Return the KG kind string for this module.

For code KGs return "code", for document KGs "doc", for domain-specific KGs "meta" (or register a new value in kg_rag.primitives.KGKind).

Returns

Kind string used in registry entries and MCP tool names.

@abstractmethod
def analyze(self) -> str: 
189    @abstractmethod
190    def analyze(self) -> str:
191        """Run a full analysis of this KG and return a Markdown report.
192
193        The report format is domain-specific but must:
194
195        1. Begin with ``# <KG Type> Analysis Report``
196        2. Include the KG name/path for identification
197        3. Include structural metrics (node count, edge count)
198        4. Include domain-appropriate quality signals
199
200        Must not raise — return a Markdown error message on failure.
201
202        :return: Markdown-formatted analysis report string.
203        """

Run a full analysis of this KG and return a Markdown report.

The report format is domain-specific but must:

  1. Begin with # <KG Type> Analysis Report
  2. Include the KG name/path for identification
  3. Include structural metrics (node count, edge count)
  4. Include domain-appropriate quality signals

Must not raise — return a Markdown error message on failure.

Returns

Markdown-formatted analysis report string.

store: GraphStore 
209    @property
210    def store(self) -> GraphStore:
211        """SQLite persistence layer (lazy-initialised)."""
212        if self._store is None:
213            self._store = GraphStore(self.db_path)
214        return self._store

SQLite persistence layer (lazy-initialised).

embedder: Embedder 
216    @property
217    def embedder(self) -> Embedder:
218        """Embedding backend (lazy-initialised, shared between index and query)."""
219        if self._embedder is None:
220            suppress_ingestion_logging()
221            self._embedder = SentenceTransformerEmbedder(self.model_name)
222        return self._embedder

Embedding backend (lazy-initialised, shared between index and query).

index: SemanticIndex 
224    @property
225    def index(self) -> SemanticIndex:
226        """LanceDB semantic index (lazy-initialised)."""
227        if self._index is None:
228            extractor = self.make_extractor()
229            self._index = SemanticIndex(
230                self.lancedb_dir,
231                embedder=self.embedder,
232                table=self.table_name,
233                index_kinds=extractor.meaningful_node_kinds(),
234            )
235        return self._index

LanceDB semantic index (lazy-initialised).

def build(self, *, wipe: bool = False) -> BuildStats: 
241    def build(self, *, wipe: bool = False) -> BuildStats:
242        """Full pipeline: extraction → SQLite → LanceDB.
243
244        :param wipe: Clear existing data before writing.
245        :return: :class:`~pycode_kg.module.types.BuildStats`.
246        """
247        graph_stats = self.build_graph(wipe=wipe)
248        index_stats = self.build_index(wipe=wipe)
249        graph_stats.indexed_rows = index_stats.indexed_rows
250        graph_stats.index_dim = index_stats.index_dim
251        return graph_stats

Full pipeline: extraction → SQLite → LanceDB.

Parameters
  • wipe: Clear existing data before writing.
Returns

~pycode_kg.module.types.BuildStats.

def build_graph(self, *, wipe: bool = False) -> BuildStats: 
253    def build_graph(self, *, wipe: bool = False) -> BuildStats:
254        """Extraction → SQLite only (no vector indexing).
255
256        Calls :meth:`make_extractor`, drains the iterator into two lists
257        of :class:`~pycode_kg.module.extractor.NodeSpec` and
258        :class:`~pycode_kg.module.extractor.EdgeSpec`, converts them to the
259        v0 :class:`~pycode_kg.pycodekg.Node` / :class:`~pycode_kg.pycodekg.Edge`
260        primitives, writes to SQLite, then calls :meth:`_post_build_hook`
261        for any domain-specific post-processing (e.g. symbol resolution).
262
263        :param wipe: Clear existing graph before writing.
264        :return: :class:`~pycode_kg.module.types.BuildStats`
265                 (``indexed_rows`` will be ``None``).
266        """
267        extractor = self.make_extractor()
268        node_specs: list[NodeSpec] = []
269        edge_specs: list[EdgeSpec] = []
270        for item in extractor.extract():
271            if isinstance(item, NodeSpec):
272                node_specs.append(item)
273            else:
274                edge_specs.append(item)
275
276        nodes = [_nodespec_to_node(s) for s in node_specs]
277        edges = [_edgespec_to_edge(s) for s in edge_specs]
278
279        self.store.write(nodes, edges, wipe=wipe)
280        self._post_build_hook(self.store)
281
282        s = self.store.stats()
283        return BuildStats(
284            repo_root=str(self.repo_root),
285            db_path=str(self.db_path),
286            total_nodes=s["total_nodes"],
287            total_edges=s["total_edges"],
288            node_counts=s["node_counts"],
289            edge_counts=s["edge_counts"],
290        )

Extraction → SQLite only (no vector indexing).

Calls make_extractor(), drains the iterator into two lists of ~pycode_kg.module.extractor.NodeSpec and ~pycode_kg.module.extractor.EdgeSpec, converts them to the v0 ~pycode_kg.pycodekg.Node / ~pycode_kg.pycodekg.Edge primitives, writes to SQLite, then calls _post_build_hook() for any domain-specific post-processing (e.g. symbol resolution).

Parameters
  • wipe: Clear existing graph before writing.
Returns

~pycode_kg.module.types.BuildStats (indexed_rows will be None).

def build_index(self, *, wipe: bool = False) -> BuildStats: 
292    def build_index(self, *, wipe: bool = False) -> BuildStats:
293        """SQLite → LanceDB only (graph must already exist).
294
295        :param wipe: Delete existing vectors before indexing.
296        :return: :class:`~pycode_kg.module.types.BuildStats` with
297                 ``indexed_rows`` and ``index_dim`` set.
298        """
299        idx_stats = self.index.build(self.store, wipe=wipe)
300        s = self.store.stats()
301        return BuildStats(
302            repo_root=str(self.repo_root),
303            db_path=str(self.db_path),
304            total_nodes=s["total_nodes"],
305            total_edges=s["total_edges"],
306            node_counts=s["node_counts"],
307            edge_counts=s["edge_counts"],
308            indexed_rows=idx_stats["indexed_rows"],
309            index_dim=idx_stats["dim"],
310        )

SQLite → LanceDB only (graph must already exist).

Parameters
  • wipe: Delete existing vectors before indexing.
Returns

~pycode_kg.module.types.BuildStats with indexed_rows and index_dim set.

def query( self, q: str, *, k: int = 8, hop: int = 1, rels: tuple[str, ...] = ('CONTAINS', 'CALLS', 'IMPORTS', 'INHERITS'), include_symbols: bool = False, max_nodes: int = 25, min_score: float = 0.0, max_per_module: int | None = None, rerank_mode: str = 'legacy', rerank_semantic_weight: float = 0.7, rerank_lexical_weight: float = 0.3) -> QueryResult: 
325    def query(
326        self,
327        q: str,
328        *,
329        k: int = 8,
330        hop: int = 1,
331        rels: tuple[str, ...] = DEFAULT_RELS,
332        include_symbols: bool = False,
333        max_nodes: int = 25,
334        min_score: float = 0.0,
335        max_per_module: int | None = None,
336        rerank_mode: str = "legacy",
337        rerank_semantic_weight: float = 0.7,
338        rerank_lexical_weight: float = 0.3,
339    ) -> QueryResult:
340        """Hybrid query: semantic seeding + structural graph expansion.
341
342        Seeds with vector ANN search (LanceDB), expands through the graph
343        (SQLite) for ``hop`` hops along ``rels`` edge types, then reranks
344        results by the selected strategy.
345
346        :param q: Natural-language query.
347        :param k: Top-K semantic hits.
348        :param hop: Graph expansion hops (0 = pure semantic).
349        :param rels: Edge types to follow during expansion.
350        :param include_symbols: Include unresolved stub nodes in results.
351        :param max_nodes: Maximum nodes to return.
352        :param min_score: Minimum semantic score for seed inclusion.
353        :param max_per_module: Optional cap on returned nodes per module path.
354        :param rerank_mode: ``'legacy'`` | ``'semantic'`` | ``'hybrid'``.
355        :param rerank_semantic_weight: Semantic weight for ``'hybrid'`` mode.
356        :param rerank_lexical_weight: Lexical weight for ``'hybrid'`` mode.
357        :return: :class:`~pycode_kg.module.types.QueryResult`.
358        """
359        q_norm = normalize_query_text(q)
360        hits = self.index.search(q_norm, k=k)
361        if min_score > 0.0:
362            hits = [h for h in hits if semantic_score_from_distance(h.distance) >= min_score]
363        seed_ids: set[str] = {h.id for h in hits}
364        seed_rank: dict[str, float] = {h.id: h.distance for h in hits}
365        q_toks = query_tokens(q_norm)
366
367        meta = self.store.expand(seed_ids, hop=hop, rels=rels)
368        all_ids = set(meta.keys())
369
370        nodes: list[dict] = []
371        kept_ids: set[str] = set()
372        module_counts: dict[str, int] = {}
373
374        def _rank_key(nid: str) -> tuple[float, float, int, int, str]:
375            prov = meta[nid]
376            dist = seed_rank.get(prov.via_seed, 1e9)
377            n = self.store.node(nid)
378            kind = n["kind"] if n else "symbol"
379            sem = semantic_score_from_distance(dist)
380            lex = lexical_overlap_score(q_toks, n or {})
381            hybrid = (
382                rerank_semantic_weight * sem + rerank_lexical_weight * lex
383                if (rerank_semantic_weight + rerank_lexical_weight) > 0
384                else sem
385            )
386            if rerank_mode == "hybrid":
387                return (-hybrid, float(prov.best_hop), self._kind_priority(kind), 0, nid)
388            if rerank_mode == "semantic":
389                return (-sem, float(prov.best_hop), self._kind_priority(kind), 0, nid)
390            return (float(prov.best_hop), dist, self._kind_priority(kind), 0, nid)
391
392        for nid in sorted(all_ids, key=_rank_key):
393            if len(nodes) >= max_nodes:
394                break
395            n = self.store.node(nid)
396            if not n:
397                continue
398            if not include_symbols and n["kind"] == "symbol":
399                continue
400            module_path = n.get("module_path") or ""
401            if max_per_module is not None and module_path:
402                if module_counts.get(module_path, 0) >= max_per_module:
403                    continue
404                module_counts[module_path] = module_counts.get(module_path, 0) + 1
405
406            prov = meta[nid]
407            dist = seed_rank.get(prov.via_seed, 1e9)
408            sem = semantic_score_from_distance(dist)
409            lex = lexical_overlap_score(q_toks, n)
410            n["relevance"] = {
411                "score": (
412                    rerank_semantic_weight * sem + rerank_lexical_weight * lex
413                    if rerank_mode == "hybrid"
414                    else sem
415                ),
416                "semantic": sem,
417                "lexical": lex,
418                "docstring_signal": docstring_signal(n.get("docstring")),
419                "hop": prov.best_hop,
420                "via_seed": prov.via_seed,
421                "mode": rerank_mode,
422            }
423            kept_ids.add(nid)
424            nodes.append(n)
425
426        edges = self.store.edges_within(kept_ids)
427        return QueryResult(
428            query=q,
429            seeds=len(seed_ids),
430            expanded_nodes=len(all_ids),
431            returned_nodes=len(nodes),
432            hop=hop,
433            rels=list(rels),
434            nodes=nodes,
435            edges=edges,
436        )

Hybrid query: semantic seeding + structural graph expansion.

Seeds with vector ANN search (LanceDB), expands through the graph (SQLite) for hop hops along rels edge types, then reranks results by the selected strategy.

Parameters
  • q: Natural-language query.
  • k: Top-K semantic hits.
  • hop: Graph expansion hops (0 = pure semantic).
  • rels: Edge types to follow during expansion.
  • include_symbols: Include unresolved stub nodes in results.
  • max_nodes: Maximum nodes to return.
  • min_score: Minimum semantic score for seed inclusion.
  • max_per_module: Optional cap on returned nodes per module path.
  • rerank_mode: 'legacy' | 'semantic' | 'hybrid'.
  • rerank_semantic_weight: Semantic weight for 'hybrid' mode.
  • rerank_lexical_weight: Lexical weight for 'hybrid' mode.
Returns

~pycode_kg.module.types.QueryResult.

def pack( self, q: str, *, k: int = 8, hop: int = 1, rels: tuple[str, ...] = ('CONTAINS', 'CALLS', 'IMPORTS', 'INHERITS'), include_symbols: bool = False, context: int = 5, max_lines: int = 60, max_nodes: int | None = 15, min_score: float = 0.0, max_per_module: int | None = None, rerank_mode: str = 'legacy', rerank_semantic_weight: float = 0.7, rerank_lexical_weight: float = 0.3, missing_lineno_policy: str = 'cap_or_skip') -> SnippetPack: 
442    def pack(
443        self,
444        q: str,
445        *,
446        k: int = 8,
447        hop: int = 1,
448        rels: tuple[str, ...] = DEFAULT_RELS,
449        include_symbols: bool = False,
450        context: int = 5,
451        max_lines: int = 60,
452        max_nodes: int | None = 15,
453        min_score: float = 0.0,
454        max_per_module: int | None = None,
455        rerank_mode: str = "legacy",
456        rerank_semantic_weight: float = 0.7,
457        rerank_lexical_weight: float = 0.3,
458        missing_lineno_policy: str = "cap_or_skip",
459    ) -> SnippetPack:
460        """Hybrid query + source-grounded snippet extraction.
461
462        :param q: Natural-language query.
463        :param k: Top-K semantic hits.
464        :param hop: Graph expansion hops.
465        :param rels: Edge types to follow during expansion.
466        :param include_symbols: Include unresolved stub nodes.
467        :param context: Extra context lines around each definition span.
468        :param max_lines: Maximum lines per snippet block.
469        :param max_nodes: Maximum nodes to return (``None`` = no limit).
470        :param min_score: Minimum semantic score for seed inclusion.
471        :param max_per_module: Optional cap on returned nodes per module.
472        :param rerank_mode: ``'legacy'`` | ``'semantic'`` | ``'hybrid'``.
473        :param rerank_semantic_weight: Semantic weight for ``'hybrid'`` mode.
474        :param rerank_lexical_weight: Lexical weight for ``'hybrid'`` mode.
475        :param missing_lineno_policy: ``'cap_or_skip'`` (default) or ``'legacy'``.
476        :return: :class:`~pycode_kg.module.types.SnippetPack`.
477        """
478        q_norm = normalize_query_text(q)
479        hits = self.index.search(q_norm, k=k)
480        if min_score > 0.0:
481            hits = [h for h in hits if semantic_score_from_distance(h.distance) >= min_score]
482        seed_rank: dict[str, dict] = {h.id: {"rank": h.rank, "dist": h.distance} for h in hits}
483        seed_ids: set[str] = set(seed_rank.keys())
484        q_toks = query_tokens(q_norm)
485        warnings: list[str] = []
486
487        meta = self.store.expand(seed_ids, hop=hop, rels=rels)
488        all_ids = set(meta.keys())
489
490        raw_nodes: list[dict] = []
491        for nid in sorted(all_ids):
492            n = self.store.node(nid)
493            if not n:
494                continue
495            if not include_symbols and n["kind"] == "symbol":
496                continue
497
498            prov = meta[nid]
499            base_dist = seed_rank.get(prov.via_seed, {"dist": 1e9})["dist"]
500            kind_pri = self._kind_priority(n["kind"])
501            sem = semantic_score_from_distance(base_dist)
502            lex = lexical_overlap_score(q_toks, n)
503            hybrid = (
504                rerank_semantic_weight * sem + rerank_lexical_weight * lex
505                if (rerank_semantic_weight + rerank_lexical_weight) > 0
506                else sem
507            )
508            if rerank_mode == "hybrid":
509                n["_rank_key"] = (-hybrid, float(prov.best_hop), kind_pri, 0, n["id"])
510            elif rerank_mode == "semantic":
511                n["_rank_key"] = (-sem, float(prov.best_hop), kind_pri, 0, n["id"])
512            else:
513                n["_rank_key"] = (float(prov.best_hop), base_dist, kind_pri, 0, n["id"])
514            n["_best_hop"] = prov.best_hop
515            n["_via_seed"] = prov.via_seed
516            n["relevance"] = {
517                "score": hybrid if rerank_mode == "hybrid" else sem,
518                "semantic": sem,
519                "lexical": lex,
520                "docstring_signal": docstring_signal(n.get("docstring")),
521                "hop": prov.best_hop,
522                "via_seed": prov.via_seed,
523                "mode": rerank_mode,
524            }
525            raw_nodes.append(n)
526
527        # Attach spans (needed for dedup)
528        file_cache: dict[str, list[str]] = {}
529        spans_by_qualname: dict[tuple[str, str], tuple[int, int]] = {}
530        for n in raw_nodes:
531            mp = n.get("module_path")
532            if not mp:
533                n["_span"] = None
534                continue
535            if mp not in file_cache:
536                file_cache[mp] = read_lines(safe_join(self.repo_root, mp))
537            lines = file_cache[mp]
538            lineno = n.get("lineno")
539            if n.get("kind") != "module" and lineno is None and missing_lineno_policy != "legacy":
540                qualname = n.get("qualname") or ""
541                parent_span = None
542                if qualname and "." in qualname:
543                    parent_key = (mp, qualname.rsplit(".", 1)[0])
544                    parent_span = spans_by_qualname.get(parent_key)
545                if parent_span:
546                    fallback_cap = min(max_lines, max(20, context * 4))
547                    p_start, p_end = parent_span
548                    capped_end = min(p_end, p_start + fallback_cap - 1)
549                    n["_span"] = (p_start, capped_end)
550                    warnings.append(
551                        f"Missing line metadata for `{n['id']}`; "
552                        f"using capped parent span {p_start}-{capped_end}."
553                    )
554                else:
555                    n["_span"] = None
556                    warnings.append(
557                        f"Missing line metadata for `{n['id']}`; "
558                        "snippet omitted (no parent span available)."
559                    )
560            else:
561                n["_span"] = compute_span(
562                    n["kind"],
563                    lineno,
564                    n.get("end_lineno"),
565                    context=context,
566                    max_lines=max_lines,
567                    file_nlines=len(lines),
568                )
569            if n.get("qualname") and n.get("_span"):
570                spans_by_qualname[(mp, n["qualname"])] = n["_span"]
571
572        raw_nodes.sort(key=lambda x: x["_rank_key"])
573
574        # Deduplicate by file + overlapping span
575        kept: list[dict] = []
576        kept_by_file: dict[str, list[tuple[tuple[int, int], str]]] = {}
577        module_counts: dict[str, int] = {}
578
579        for n in raw_nodes:
580            if max_nodes is not None and len(kept) >= max_nodes:
581                break
582            mp = n.get("module_path") or ""
583            span = n.get("_span")
584
585            if not mp or not span or span[1] < span[0]:
586                kept.append(n)
587                continue
588
589            if any(spans_overlap(span, s2) for s2, _ in kept_by_file.get(mp, [])):
590                continue
591
592            if max_per_module is not None and mp:
593                if module_counts.get(mp, 0) >= max_per_module:
594                    continue
595                module_counts[mp] = module_counts.get(mp, 0) + 1
596
597            kept.append(n)
598            kept_by_file.setdefault(mp, []).append((span, n["id"]))
599
600        kept_ids: set[str] = {n["id"] for n in kept}
601        edges = self.store.edges_within(kept_ids)
602
603        # Attach snippets
604        for n in kept:
605            mp = n.get("module_path")
606            span = n.get("_span")
607            if not mp or not span:
608                continue
609            if mp not in file_cache:
610                file_cache[mp] = read_lines(safe_join(self.repo_root, mp))
611            lines = file_cache[mp]
612            start, end = span
613
614            if n.get("kind") == "module" and len(lines) > max_lines:
615                contained = [
616                    self.store.node(cn_id)
617                    for cn_id in (
618                        row[0]
619                        for row in self.store.con.execute(
620                            "SELECT dst FROM edges WHERE src = ? AND rel = 'CONTAINS'",
621                            (n["id"],),
622                        ).fetchall()
623                    )
624                    if self.store.node(cn_id) is not None
625                ]
626                contained_nodes: list[dict] = [c for c in contained if c is not None]
627                n["snippet"] = make_module_summary(
628                    mp, lines, n.get("docstring"), contained_nodes, max_lines
629                )
630            elif end >= start and lines:
631                n["snippet"] = make_snippet(mp, lines, start, end)
632
633        # Strip internal keys
634        for n in kept:
635            for key in [k for k in n if k.startswith("_")]:
636                del n[key]
637
638        return SnippetPack(
639            query=q,
640            seeds=len(seed_ids),
641            expanded_nodes=len(all_ids),
642            returned_nodes=len(kept),
643            hop=hop,
644            rels=list(rels),
645            model=self.model_name,
646            nodes=kept,
647            edges=edges,
648            warnings=warnings,
649        )

Hybrid query + source-grounded snippet extraction.

Parameters
  • q: Natural-language query.
  • k: Top-K semantic hits.
  • hop: Graph expansion hops.
  • rels: Edge types to follow during expansion.
  • include_symbols: Include unresolved stub nodes.
  • context: Extra context lines around each definition span.
  • max_lines: Maximum lines per snippet block.
  • max_nodes: Maximum nodes to return (None = no limit).
  • min_score: Minimum semantic score for seed inclusion.
  • max_per_module: Optional cap on returned nodes per module.
  • rerank_mode: 'legacy' | 'semantic' | 'hybrid'.
  • rerank_semantic_weight: Semantic weight for 'hybrid' mode.
  • rerank_lexical_weight: Lexical weight for 'hybrid' mode.
  • missing_lineno_policy: 'cap_or_skip' (default) or 'legacy'.
Returns

~pycode_kg.module.types.SnippetPack.

def callers(self, node_id: str, *, rel: str = 'CALLS') -> list[dict]: 
655    def callers(self, node_id: str, *, rel: str = "CALLS") -> list[dict]:
656        """Return all nodes that call *node_id*, resolving through stubs.
657
658        :param node_id: Target node identifier.
659        :param rel: Relation type to invert (default ``"CALLS"``).
660        :return: Deduplicated list of caller node dicts.
661        """
662        return self.store.callers_of(node_id, rel=rel)

Return all nodes that call node_id, resolving through stubs.

Parameters
  • node_id: Target node identifier.
  • rel: Relation type to invert (default "CALLS").
Returns

Deduplicated list of caller node dicts.

def stats(self) -> dict: 
664    def stats(self) -> dict:
665        """Return store statistics (node/edge counts by kind/relation).
666
667        :return: Dictionary from :meth:`~pycode_kg.store.GraphStore.stats`.
668        """
669        return self.store.stats()

Return store statistics (node/edge counts by kind/relation).

Returns

Dictionary from ~pycode_kg.store.GraphStore.stats().

def node(self, node_id: str) -> dict | None: 
671    def node(self, node_id: str) -> dict | None:
672        """Fetch a single node by ID from the store.
673
674        :param node_id: Stable node identifier.
675        :return: Node dict or ``None`` if not found.
676        """
677        return self.store.node(node_id)

Fetch a single node by ID from the store.

Parameters
  • node_id: Stable node identifier.
Returns

Node dict or None if not found.

def close(self) -> None: 
679    def close(self) -> None:
680        """Close the underlying SQLite connection."""
681        if self._store is not None:
682            self._store.close()

Close the underlying SQLite connection.

class KGExtractor(abc.ABC): 
 83class KGExtractor(ABC):
 84    """Abstract extraction protocol for a knowledge graph domain.
 85
 86    Subclass this to teach :class:`~pycode_kg.module.base.KGModule` how to
 87    traverse your source and emit :class:`NodeSpec` / :class:`EdgeSpec`
 88    objects.  The base infrastructure (:class:`~pycode_kg.store.GraphStore`,
 89    :class:`~pycode_kg.index.SemanticIndex`, snapshot management, MCP server,
 90    CLI) is provided by :class:`~pycode_kg.module.base.KGModule` — you only
 91    implement the domain-specific parsing.
 92
 93    Minimal subclass::
 94
 95        class MyExtractor(KGExtractor):
 96            def node_kinds(self): return ["entity", "relation"]
 97            def edge_kinds(self): return ["LINKED_TO"]
 98            def extract(self):
 99                for item in my_parser(self.repo_path):
100                    yield NodeSpec(node_id=item.id, kind="entity", ...)
101
102    :param repo_path: Absolute path to the repository or corpus root.
103    :param config: Domain-specific configuration dict.
104    """
105
106    def __init__(self, repo_path: Path, config: dict[str, Any] | None = None) -> None:
107        """Initialise the extractor.
108
109        :param repo_path: Path to the repository or corpus root.
110        :param config: Optional domain-specific configuration dict.
111        """
112        self.repo_path = Path(repo_path).resolve()
113        self.config = config or {}
114
115    @abstractmethod
116    def node_kinds(self) -> list[str]:
117        """Return the canonical list of node kinds this extractor emits.
118
119        Example: ``['module', 'class', 'function', 'method']``
120
121        Used to parameterize the LanceDB ``index_kinds`` and the MCP
122        ``graph_stats`` tool enum.
123        """
124
125    @abstractmethod
126    def edge_kinds(self) -> list[str]:
127        """Return the canonical list of edge relation types this extractor emits.
128
129        Example: ``['CALLS', 'IMPORTS', 'CONTAINS', 'INHERITS']``
130        """
131
132    @abstractmethod
133    def extract(self) -> Iterator[NodeSpec | EdgeSpec]:
134        """Traverse the source and yield nodes and edges.
135
136        May yield :class:`NodeSpec` and :class:`EdgeSpec` objects in any
137        order.  The build pipeline separates them into two lists before
138        calling :meth:`~pycode_kg.store.GraphStore.write`.
139
140        Implementations should be deterministic: the same source should
141        produce the same stream on every call.
142        """
143
144    def meaningful_node_kinds(self) -> list[str]:
145        """Return the subset of ``node_kinds()`` that are indexed semantically.
146
147        Default: all of :meth:`node_kinds`.  Override to exclude structural
148        stubs (e.g., unresolved import placeholders, synthetic wrapper nodes)
149        from the LanceDB index and from coverage metrics.
150
151        :return: List of node kind strings to index and measure.
152        """
153        return self.node_kinds()
154
155    def coverage_metric(self, nodes: list[NodeSpec]) -> float:
156        """Compute a coverage quality metric for snapshots (0.0–1.0).
157
158        Default: fraction of meaningful nodes that have a non-empty docstring.
159        Override for domain-appropriate metrics (e.g., pathway annotation
160        completeness, legal cross-reference density).
161
162        :param nodes: All extracted :class:`NodeSpec` objects.
163        :return: Coverage score in ``[0.0, 1.0]``.
164        """
165        meaningful = [n for n in nodes if n.kind in self.meaningful_node_kinds()]
166        if not meaningful:
167            return 0.0
168        covered = sum(1 for n in meaningful if n.docstring.strip())
169        return covered / len(meaningful)

Abstract extraction protocol for a knowledge graph domain.

Subclass this to teach ~pycode_kg.module.base.KGModule how to traverse your source and emit NodeSpec / EdgeSpec objects. The base infrastructure (~pycode_kg.store.GraphStore, ~pycode_kg.index.SemanticIndex, snapshot management, MCP server, CLI) is provided by ~pycode_kg.module.base.KGModule — you only implement the domain-specific parsing.

Minimal subclass::

class MyExtractor(KGExtractor):
    def node_kinds(self): return ["entity", "relation"]
    def edge_kinds(self): return ["LINKED_TO"]
    def extract(self):
        for item in my_parser(self.repo_path):
            yield NodeSpec(node_id=item.id, kind="entity", ...)
Parameters
  • repo_path: Absolute path to the repository or corpus root.
  • config: Domain-specific configuration dict.
KGExtractor(repo_path: pathlib.Path, config: dict[str, typing.Any] | None = None) 
106    def __init__(self, repo_path: Path, config: dict[str, Any] | None = None) -> None:
107        """Initialise the extractor.
108
109        :param repo_path: Path to the repository or corpus root.
110        :param config: Optional domain-specific configuration dict.
111        """
112        self.repo_path = Path(repo_path).resolve()
113        self.config = config or {}

Initialise the extractor.

Parameters
  • repo_path: Path to the repository or corpus root.
  • config: Optional domain-specific configuration dict.
repo_path
config
@abstractmethod
def node_kinds(self) -> list[str]: 
115    @abstractmethod
116    def node_kinds(self) -> list[str]:
117        """Return the canonical list of node kinds this extractor emits.
118
119        Example: ``['module', 'class', 'function', 'method']``
120
121        Used to parameterize the LanceDB ``index_kinds`` and the MCP
122        ``graph_stats`` tool enum.
123        """

Return the canonical list of node kinds this extractor emits.

Example: ['module', 'class', 'function', 'method']

Used to parameterize the LanceDB index_kinds and the MCP graph_stats tool enum.

@abstractmethod
def edge_kinds(self) -> list[str]: 
125    @abstractmethod
126    def edge_kinds(self) -> list[str]:
127        """Return the canonical list of edge relation types this extractor emits.
128
129        Example: ``['CALLS', 'IMPORTS', 'CONTAINS', 'INHERITS']``
130        """

Return the canonical list of edge relation types this extractor emits.

Example: ['CALLS', 'IMPORTS', 'CONTAINS', 'INHERITS']

@abstractmethod
def extract( self) -> Iterator[NodeSpec | EdgeSpec]: 
132    @abstractmethod
133    def extract(self) -> Iterator[NodeSpec | EdgeSpec]:
134        """Traverse the source and yield nodes and edges.
135
136        May yield :class:`NodeSpec` and :class:`EdgeSpec` objects in any
137        order.  The build pipeline separates them into two lists before
138        calling :meth:`~pycode_kg.store.GraphStore.write`.
139
140        Implementations should be deterministic: the same source should
141        produce the same stream on every call.
142        """

Traverse the source and yield nodes and edges.

May yield NodeSpec and EdgeSpec objects in any order. The build pipeline separates them into two lists before calling ~pycode_kg.store.GraphStore.write().

Implementations should be deterministic: the same source should produce the same stream on every call.

def meaningful_node_kinds(self) -> list[str]: 
144    def meaningful_node_kinds(self) -> list[str]:
145        """Return the subset of ``node_kinds()`` that are indexed semantically.
146
147        Default: all of :meth:`node_kinds`.  Override to exclude structural
148        stubs (e.g., unresolved import placeholders, synthetic wrapper nodes)
149        from the LanceDB index and from coverage metrics.
150
151        :return: List of node kind strings to index and measure.
152        """
153        return self.node_kinds()

Return the subset of node_kinds() that are indexed semantically.

Default: all of node_kinds(). Override to exclude structural stubs (e.g., unresolved import placeholders, synthetic wrapper nodes) from the LanceDB index and from coverage metrics.

Returns

List of node kind strings to index and measure.

def coverage_metric(self, nodes: list[NodeSpec]) -> float: 
155    def coverage_metric(self, nodes: list[NodeSpec]) -> float:
156        """Compute a coverage quality metric for snapshots (0.0–1.0).
157
158        Default: fraction of meaningful nodes that have a non-empty docstring.
159        Override for domain-appropriate metrics (e.g., pathway annotation
160        completeness, legal cross-reference density).
161
162        :param nodes: All extracted :class:`NodeSpec` objects.
163        :return: Coverage score in ``[0.0, 1.0]``.
164        """
165        meaningful = [n for n in nodes if n.kind in self.meaningful_node_kinds()]
166        if not meaningful:
167            return 0.0
168        covered = sum(1 for n in meaningful if n.docstring.strip())
169        return covered / len(meaningful)

Compute a coverage quality metric for snapshots (0.0–1.0).

Default: fraction of meaningful nodes that have a non-empty docstring. Override for domain-appropriate metrics (e.g., pathway annotation completeness, legal cross-reference density).

Parameters
  • nodes: All extracted NodeSpec objects.
Returns

Coverage score in [0.0, 1.0].

class PyCodeKGExtractor(pycode_kg.KGExtractor): 
177class PyCodeKGExtractor(KGExtractor):
178    """KGExtractor backed by CodeGraph (Python AST analysis).
179
180    Wraps :class:`~pycode_kg.graph.CodeGraph` and converts its :class:`Node`
181    and :class:`Edge` v0 primitives to :class:`NodeSpec` / :class:`EdgeSpec`
182    for the generic :class:`~pycode_kg.module.base.KGModule` build pipeline.
183
184    This is the extractor used by :class:`~pycode_kg.kg.PyCodeKG` and is the
185    reference implementation for source-code KG extractors.
186
187    :param repo_path: Path to the Python repository root.
188    :param include: Set of top-level directory names to include (all if empty).
189    :param exclude: Set of directory names to exclude at every depth.
190    :param config: Optional config dict (forwarded to super).
191    """
192
193    def __init__(
194        self,
195        repo_path: Path,
196        *,
197        include: set[str] | None = None,
198        exclude: set[str] | None = None,
199        config: dict[str, Any] | None = None,
200    ) -> None:
201        """Initialise the extractor for a Python repository.
202
203        :param repo_path: Path to the Python repository root.
204        :param include: Top-level directory names to include (empty = all).
205        :param exclude: Directory names to exclude at every walk depth.
206        :param config: Optional domain-specific configuration dict.
207        """
208        super().__init__(repo_path, config)
209        self._include: set[str] = include or set()
210        self._exclude: set[str] = exclude or set()
211
212    def node_kinds(self) -> list[str]:
213        """Return the Python AST node kinds.
214
215        :return: ``['module', 'class', 'function', 'method', 'symbol']``
216        """
217        return ["module", "class", "function", "method", "symbol"]
218
219    def edge_kinds(self) -> list[str]:
220        """Return the Python AST edge relation types.
221
222        :return: List of all relation strings emitted by the Python extractor.
223        """
224        return [
225            "CONTAINS",
226            "CALLS",
227            "IMPORTS",
228            "INHERITS",
229            "READS",
230            "WRITES",
231            "ATTR_ACCESS",
232            "DEPENDS_ON",
233            "RESOLVES_TO",
234        ]
235
236    def meaningful_node_kinds(self) -> list[str]:
237        """Return the node kinds indexed by LanceDB and counted in coverage.
238
239        Excludes ``'symbol'`` (unresolved import stubs).
240
241        :return: ``['module', 'class', 'function', 'method']``
242        """
243        return ["module", "class", "function", "method"]
244
245    def extract(self) -> Iterator[NodeSpec | EdgeSpec]:
246        """Run Python AST extraction and yield NodeSpec / EdgeSpec objects.
247
248        Delegates to :class:`~pycode_kg.graph.CodeGraph` for the AST walk, then
249        converts the v0-locked :class:`~pycode_kg.pycodekg.Node` and
250        :class:`~pycode_kg.pycodekg.Edge` primitives to the generic spec types.
251
252        :return: Iterator of :class:`NodeSpec` and :class:`EdgeSpec` objects.
253        """
254        from pycode_kg.graph import CodeGraph  # pylint: disable=import-outside-toplevel
255
256        graph = CodeGraph(self.repo_path, include=self._include, exclude=self._exclude)
257        nodes, edges = graph.result()
258
259        for n in nodes:
260            yield NodeSpec(
261                node_id=n.id,
262                kind=n.kind,
263                name=n.name,
264                qualname=n.qualname or "",
265                source_path=n.module_path or "",
266                lineno=n.lineno,
267                end_lineno=n.end_lineno,
268                docstring=n.docstring or "",
269            )
270
271        for e in edges:
272            yield EdgeSpec(
273                source_id=e.src,
274                target_id=e.dst,
275                relation=e.rel,
276                metadata={"evidence": e.evidence} if e.evidence else {},
277            )

KGExtractor backed by CodeGraph (Python AST analysis).

Wraps ~pycode_kg.graph.CodeGraph and converts its Node and Edge v0 primitives to NodeSpec / EdgeSpec for the generic ~pycode_kg.module.base.KGModule build pipeline.

This is the extractor used by ~pycode_kg.kg.PyCodeKG and is the reference implementation for source-code KG extractors.

Parameters
  • repo_path: Path to the Python repository root.
  • include: Set of top-level directory names to include (all if empty).
  • exclude: Set of directory names to exclude at every depth.
  • config: Optional config dict (forwarded to super).
PyCodeKGExtractor( repo_path: pathlib.Path, *, include: set[str] | None = None, exclude: set[str] | None = None, config: dict[str, typing.Any] | None = None) 
193    def __init__(
194        self,
195        repo_path: Path,
196        *,
197        include: set[str] | None = None,
198        exclude: set[str] | None = None,
199        config: dict[str, Any] | None = None,
200    ) -> None:
201        """Initialise the extractor for a Python repository.
202
203        :param repo_path: Path to the Python repository root.
204        :param include: Top-level directory names to include (empty = all).
205        :param exclude: Directory names to exclude at every walk depth.
206        :param config: Optional domain-specific configuration dict.
207        """
208        super().__init__(repo_path, config)
209        self._include: set[str] = include or set()
210        self._exclude: set[str] = exclude or set()

Initialise the extractor for a Python repository.

Parameters
  • repo_path: Path to the Python repository root.
  • include: Top-level directory names to include (empty = all).
  • exclude: Directory names to exclude at every walk depth.
  • config: Optional domain-specific configuration dict.
def node_kinds(self) -> list[str]: 
212    def node_kinds(self) -> list[str]:
213        """Return the Python AST node kinds.
214
215        :return: ``['module', 'class', 'function', 'method', 'symbol']``
216        """
217        return ["module", "class", "function", "method", "symbol"]

Return the Python AST node kinds.

Returns

['module', 'class', 'function', 'method', 'symbol']

def edge_kinds(self) -> list[str]: 
219    def edge_kinds(self) -> list[str]:
220        """Return the Python AST edge relation types.
221
222        :return: List of all relation strings emitted by the Python extractor.
223        """
224        return [
225            "CONTAINS",
226            "CALLS",
227            "IMPORTS",
228            "INHERITS",
229            "READS",
230            "WRITES",
231            "ATTR_ACCESS",
232            "DEPENDS_ON",
233            "RESOLVES_TO",
234        ]

Return the Python AST edge relation types.

Returns

List of all relation strings emitted by the Python extractor.

def meaningful_node_kinds(self) -> list[str]: 
236    def meaningful_node_kinds(self) -> list[str]:
237        """Return the node kinds indexed by LanceDB and counted in coverage.
238
239        Excludes ``'symbol'`` (unresolved import stubs).
240
241        :return: ``['module', 'class', 'function', 'method']``
242        """
243        return ["module", "class", "function", "method"]

Return the node kinds indexed by LanceDB and counted in coverage.

Excludes 'symbol' (unresolved import stubs).

Returns

['module', 'class', 'function', 'method']

def extract( self) -> Iterator[NodeSpec | EdgeSpec]: 
245    def extract(self) -> Iterator[NodeSpec | EdgeSpec]:
246        """Run Python AST extraction and yield NodeSpec / EdgeSpec objects.
247
248        Delegates to :class:`~pycode_kg.graph.CodeGraph` for the AST walk, then
249        converts the v0-locked :class:`~pycode_kg.pycodekg.Node` and
250        :class:`~pycode_kg.pycodekg.Edge` primitives to the generic spec types.
251
252        :return: Iterator of :class:`NodeSpec` and :class:`EdgeSpec` objects.
253        """
254        from pycode_kg.graph import CodeGraph  # pylint: disable=import-outside-toplevel
255
256        graph = CodeGraph(self.repo_path, include=self._include, exclude=self._exclude)
257        nodes, edges = graph.result()
258
259        for n in nodes:
260            yield NodeSpec(
261                node_id=n.id,
262                kind=n.kind,
263                name=n.name,
264                qualname=n.qualname or "",
265                source_path=n.module_path or "",
266                lineno=n.lineno,
267                end_lineno=n.end_lineno,
268                docstring=n.docstring or "",
269            )
270
271        for e in edges:
272            yield EdgeSpec(
273                source_id=e.src,
274                target_id=e.dst,
275                relation=e.rel,
276                metadata={"evidence": e.evidence} if e.evidence else {},
277            )

Run Python AST extraction and yield NodeSpec / EdgeSpec objects.

Delegates to ~pycode_kg.graph.CodeGraph for the AST walk, then converts the v0-locked ~pycode_kg.pycodekg.Node and ~pycode_kg.pycodekg.Edge primitives to the generic spec types.

Returns

Iterator of NodeSpec and EdgeSpec objects.

@dataclass
class NodeSpec: 
31@dataclass
32class NodeSpec:
33    """Specification for a single graph node emitted by a KGExtractor.
34
35    This is the canonical intermediate type between domain-specific source
36    parsing and the generic GraphStore persistence layer.
37
38    :param node_id: Stable unique ID (e.g. ``'fn:src/foo.py:bar'``).
39    :param kind: Domain node type (``'function'``, ``'gene'``, ``'statute'``, …).
40    :param name: Human-readable short name.
41    :param qualname: Fully-qualified name within its source (may equal ``name``).
42    :param source_path: Repo-relative path to source file or document.
43    :param lineno: 1-based start line (source-code KGs); ``None`` for doc/domain KGs.
44    :param end_lineno: 1-based end line; ``None`` if not applicable.
45    :param docstring: Docstring, description, or excerpt used for embedding.
46    :param metadata: Domain-specific extension data (serialized as JSON in the store).
47    """
48
49    node_id: str
50    kind: str
51    name: str
52    qualname: str
53    source_path: str
54    lineno: int | None = None
55    end_lineno: int | None = None
56    docstring: str = ""
57    metadata: dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)

Specification for a single graph node emitted by a KGExtractor.

This is the canonical intermediate type between domain-specific source parsing and the generic GraphStore persistence layer.

Parameters
  • **node_id: Stable unique ID (e.g. 'fn:src/foo.py**: bar').
  • kind: Domain node type ('function', 'gene', 'statute', …).
  • name: Human-readable short name.
  • qualname: Fully-qualified name within its source (may equal name).
  • source_path: Repo-relative path to source file or document.
  • lineno: 1-based start line (source-code KGs); None for doc/domain KGs.
  • end_lineno: 1-based end line; None if not applicable.
  • docstring: Docstring, description, or excerpt used for embedding.
  • metadata: Domain-specific extension data (serialized as JSON in the store).
NodeSpec( node_id: str, kind: str, name: str, qualname: str, source_path: str, lineno: int | None = None, end_lineno: int | None = None, docstring: str = '', metadata: dict[str, typing.Any] = <factory>)
node_id: str
kind: str
name: str
qualname: str
source_path: str
lineno: int | None = None
end_lineno: int | None = None
docstring: str = ''
metadata: dict[str, typing.Any]
@dataclass
class EdgeSpec: 
60@dataclass
61class EdgeSpec:
62    """Specification for a single directed graph edge emitted by a KGExtractor.
63
64    :param source_id: ``node_id`` of the source node.
65    :param target_id: ``node_id`` of the target node (may be an unresolved stub ID).
66    :param relation: Edge type (``'CALLS'``, ``'IMPORTS'``, ``'CONTAINS'``, …).
67    :param weight: Edge weight for PageRank (default 1.0).
68    :param metadata: Domain-specific edge data (serialized as evidence JSON in the store).
69    """
70
71    source_id: str
72    target_id: str
73    relation: str
74    weight: float = 1.0
75    metadata: dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)

Specification for a single directed graph edge emitted by a KGExtractor.

Parameters
  • source_id: node_id of the source node.
  • target_id: node_id of the target node (may be an unresolved stub ID).
  • relation: Edge type ('CALLS', 'IMPORTS', 'CONTAINS', …).
  • weight: Edge weight for PageRank (default 1.0).
  • metadata: Domain-specific edge data (serialized as evidence JSON in the store).
EdgeSpec( source_id: str, target_id: str, relation: str, weight: float = 1.0, metadata: dict[str, typing.Any] = <factory>)
source_id: str
target_id: str
relation: str
weight: float = 1.0
metadata: dict[str, typing.Any]
class PyCodeKG(pycode_kg.KGModule): 
 65class PyCodeKG(KGModule):
 66    """
 67    Top-level orchestrator for the Code Knowledge Graph.
 68
 69    Subclasses :class:`~pycode_kg.module.base.KGModule` and provides the
 70    Python-AST-specific extraction layer via :class:`PyCodeKGExtractor`.
 71    All generic infrastructure — SQLite persistence, LanceDB indexing,
 72    hybrid query, snippet packing, snapshots — is inherited from
