 73    :class:`~pycode_kg.module.base.KGModule`.
 74
 75    Typical usage::
 76
 77        kg = PyCodeKG(repo_root="/path/to/repo")
 78        stats = kg.build(wipe=True)
 79        print(stats)
 80
 81        result = kg.query("database connection setup", k=8, hop=1)
 82        result.print_summary()
 83
 84        pack = kg.pack("configuration loading", k=8, hop=1)
 85        pack.save("context.md")
 86
 87    :param repo_root: Repository root directory.
 88    :param db_path: SQLite database path (defaults to
 89        ``<repo_root>/.pycodekg/graph.sqlite``).
 90    :param lancedb_dir: LanceDB directory (defaults to
 91        ``<repo_root>/.pycodekg/lancedb``).
 92    :param model: Sentence-transformer model name.
 93    :param table: LanceDB table name.
 94    """
 95
 96    _default_dir = ".pycodekg"
 97
 98    def __init__(
 99        self,
100        repo_root: str | Path,
101        db_path: str | Path | None = None,
102        lancedb_dir: str | Path | None = None,
103        *,
104        model: str = DEFAULT_MODEL,
105        table: str = "pycodekg_nodes",
106    ) -> None:
107        """Initialise ``PyCodeKG`` and resolve all paths.
108
109        :param repo_root: Repository root directory; resolved to an absolute path.
110        :param db_path: Path to the SQLite database file.
111        :param lancedb_dir: Directory used by LanceDB for the vector index.
112        :param model: Sentence-transformer model name used for embedding.
113        :param table: LanceDB table name for the node index.
114        """
115        super().__init__(
116            repo_root,
117            db_path=db_path,
118            lancedb_dir=lancedb_dir,
119            model=model,
120            table=table,
121        )
122        # Backwards-compatible cached CodeGraph (direct access still works)
123        self._graph: CodeGraph | None = None
124
125    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
126    # KGModule abstract interface
127    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
128
129    def make_extractor(self) -> KGExtractor:
130        """Return a PyCodeKGExtractor for this repository.
131
132        :return: :class:`PyCodeKGExtractor` targeting :attr:`repo_root`.
133        """
134        return PyCodeKGExtractor(self.repo_root)
135
136    def kind(self) -> str:
137        """Return the KG kind string.
138
139        :return: ``"code"``
140        """
141        return "code"
142
143    def analyze(self, *, include_edge_provenance: bool = False) -> str:
144        """Run full nine-phase architectural analysis and return Markdown.
145
146        Delegates to :class:`~pycode_kg.pycodekg_thorough_analysis.PyCodeKGAnalyzer`.
147        This is the same analysis produced by ``pycodekg analyze``.
148
149        :param include_edge_provenance: Annotate edges with confidence metadata.
150        :return: Markdown-formatted architectural analysis report.
151        """
152        try:
153            from io import StringIO  # pylint: disable=import-outside-toplevel
154
155            from rich.console import Console  # pylint: disable=import-outside-toplevel
156
157            from pycode_kg.pycodekg_thorough_analysis import (  # pylint: disable=import-outside-toplevel
158                PyCodeKGAnalyzer,
159            )
160
161            silent = Console(file=StringIO(), highlight=False)
162            analyzer = PyCodeKGAnalyzer(self, console=silent)
163            analyzer.run_analysis()
164            return analyzer.to_markdown()
165        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001  # pylint: disable=broad-exception-caught
166            return f"# PyCodeKG Analysis\n\nAnalysis failed: {exc}\n"
167
168    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
169    # Python-specific overrides
170    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
171
172    def _kind_priority(self, kind: str) -> int:
173        """Return Python-specific node kind sort priority.
174
175        :param kind: Node kind string.
176        :return: Integer priority (lower = ranked first).
177        """
178        return _PYCODEKG_KIND_PRIORITY.get(kind, 99)
179
180    def _post_build_hook(self, store: GraphStore) -> None:
181        """Resolve Python symbol stubs after writing nodes/edges.
182
183        Called by :meth:`~pycode_kg.module.base.KGModule.build_graph` after
184        ``store.write()``.  Links ``sym:`` import stubs to their definitions
185        via ``RESOLVES_TO`` edges.
186
187        :param store: The :class:`~pycode_kg.store.GraphStore` just written to.
188        """
189        store.resolve_symbols()
190
191    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
192    # Backwards-compatible CodeGraph property
193    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
194
195    @property
196    def graph(self) -> CodeGraph:
197        """Python AST extraction layer (lazy, cached).
198
199        Kept for backwards compatibility with code that accesses
200        ``kg.graph`` directly.  The build pipeline uses
201        :meth:`make_extractor` instead.
202        """
203        if self._graph is None:
204            self._graph = CodeGraph(self.repo_root)
205        return self._graph
206
207    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
208    # Repr
209    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
210
211    def __repr__(self) -> str:
212        """Return an unambiguous string representation.
213
214        :return: ``PyCodeKG(repo_root=..., db_path=..., lancedb_dir=..., model=...)``.
215        """
216        return (
217            f"PyCodeKG(repo_root={self.repo_root!r}, "
218            f"db_path={self.db_path!r}, "
219            f"lancedb_dir={self.lancedb_dir!r}, "
220            f"model={self.model_name!r})"
221        )

Top-level orchestrator for the Code Knowledge Graph.

Subclasses ~pycode_kg.module.base.KGModule and provides the Python-AST-specific extraction layer via PyCodeKGExtractor. All generic infrastructure — SQLite persistence, LanceDB indexing, hybrid query, snippet packing, snapshots — is inherited from ~pycode_kg.module.base.KGModule.

Typical usage::

kg = PyCodeKG(repo_root="/path/to/repo")
stats = kg.build(wipe=True)
print(stats)

result = kg.query("database connection setup", k=8, hop=1)
result.print_summary()

pack = kg.pack("configuration loading", k=8, hop=1)
pack.save("context.md")
Parameters
  • repo_root: Repository root directory.
  • db_path: SQLite database path (defaults to <repo_root>/.pycodekg/graph.sqlite).
  • lancedb_dir: LanceDB directory (defaults to <repo_root>/.pycodekg/lancedb).
  • model: Sentence-transformer model name.
  • table: LanceDB table name.
PyCodeKG( repo_root: str | pathlib.Path, db_path: str | pathlib.Path | None = None, lancedb_dir: str | pathlib.Path | None = None, *, model: str = 'BAAI/bge-small-en-v1.5', table: str = 'pycodekg_nodes') 
 98    def __init__(
 99        self,
100        repo_root: str | Path,
101        db_path: str | Path | None = None,
102        lancedb_dir: str | Path | None = None,
103        *,
104        model: str = DEFAULT_MODEL,
105        table: str = "pycodekg_nodes",
106    ) -> None:
107        """Initialise ``PyCodeKG`` and resolve all paths.
108
109        :param repo_root: Repository root directory; resolved to an absolute path.
110        :param db_path: Path to the SQLite database file.
111        :param lancedb_dir: Directory used by LanceDB for the vector index.
112        :param model: Sentence-transformer model name used for embedding.
113        :param table: LanceDB table name for the node index.
114        """
115        super().__init__(
116            repo_root,
117            db_path=db_path,
118            lancedb_dir=lancedb_dir,
119            model=model,
120            table=table,
121        )
122        # Backwards-compatible cached CodeGraph (direct access still works)
123        self._graph: CodeGraph | None = None

Initialise PyCodeKG and resolve all paths.

Parameters
  • repo_root: Repository root directory; resolved to an absolute path.
  • db_path: Path to the SQLite database file.
  • lancedb_dir: Directory used by LanceDB for the vector index.
  • model: Sentence-transformer model name used for embedding.
  • table: LanceDB table name for the node index.
def make_extractor(self) -> KGExtractor: 
129    def make_extractor(self) -> KGExtractor:
130        """Return a PyCodeKGExtractor for this repository.
131
132        :return: :class:`PyCodeKGExtractor` targeting :attr:`repo_root`.
133        """
134        return PyCodeKGExtractor(self.repo_root)

Return a PyCodeKGExtractor for this repository.

Returns

PyCodeKGExtractor targeting repo_root.

def kind(self) -> str: 
136    def kind(self) -> str:
137        """Return the KG kind string.
138
139        :return: ``"code"``
140        """
141        return "code"

Return the KG kind string.

Returns

"code"

def analyze(self, *, include_edge_provenance: bool = False) -> str: 
143    def analyze(self, *, include_edge_provenance: bool = False) -> str:
144        """Run full nine-phase architectural analysis and return Markdown.
145
146        Delegates to :class:`~pycode_kg.pycodekg_thorough_analysis.PyCodeKGAnalyzer`.
147        This is the same analysis produced by ``pycodekg analyze``.
148
149        :param include_edge_provenance: Annotate edges with confidence metadata.
150        :return: Markdown-formatted architectural analysis report.
151        """
152        try:
153            from io import StringIO  # pylint: disable=import-outside-toplevel
154
155            from rich.console import Console  # pylint: disable=import-outside-toplevel
156
157            from pycode_kg.pycodekg_thorough_analysis import (  # pylint: disable=import-outside-toplevel
158                PyCodeKGAnalyzer,
159            )
160
161            silent = Console(file=StringIO(), highlight=False)
162            analyzer = PyCodeKGAnalyzer(self, console=silent)
163            analyzer.run_analysis()
164            return analyzer.to_markdown()
165        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001  # pylint: disable=broad-exception-caught
166            return f"# PyCodeKG Analysis\n\nAnalysis failed: {exc}\n"

Run full nine-phase architectural analysis and return Markdown.

Delegates to ~pycode_kg.pycodekg_thorough_analysis.PyCodeKGAnalyzer. This is the same analysis produced by pycodekg analyze.

Parameters
  • include_edge_provenance: Annotate edges with confidence metadata.
Returns

Markdown-formatted architectural analysis report.

graph: CodeGraph 
195    @property
196    def graph(self) -> CodeGraph:
197        """Python AST extraction layer (lazy, cached).
198
199        Kept for backwards compatibility with code that accesses
200        ``kg.graph`` directly.  The build pipeline uses
201        :meth:`make_extractor` instead.
202        """
203        if self._graph is None:
204            self._graph = CodeGraph(self.repo_root)
205        return self._graph

Python AST extraction layer (lazy, cached).

Kept for backwards compatibility with code that accesses kg.graph directly. The build pipeline uses make_extractor() instead.

@dataclass
class BuildStats: 
26@dataclass
27class BuildStats:
28    """
29    Statistics returned by :meth:`KGModule.build` and related methods.
30
31    :param repo_root: Repository root that was analysed.
32    :param db_path: Path to the SQLite database.
33    :param total_nodes: Total nodes written to SQLite.
34    :param total_edges: Total edges written to SQLite.
35    :param node_counts: Node counts broken down by kind.
36    :param edge_counts: Edge counts broken down by relation.
37    :param indexed_rows: Number of nodes embedded into LanceDB
38                         (``None`` if the index was not built).
39    :param index_dim: Embedding dimension (``None`` if not built).
40    """
41
42    repo_root: str
43    db_path: str
44    total_nodes: int
45    total_edges: int
46    node_counts: dict[str, int]
47    edge_counts: dict[str, int]
48    indexed_rows: int | None = None
49    index_dim: int | None = None
50
51    def to_dict(self) -> dict:
52        """Serialise the stats to a plain dictionary.
53
54        :return: Dictionary containing all ``BuildStats`` fields.
55        """
56        return {
57            "repo_root": self.repo_root,
58            "db_path": self.db_path,
59            "total_nodes": self.total_nodes,
60            "total_edges": self.total_edges,
61            "node_counts": self.node_counts,
62            "edge_counts": self.edge_counts,
63            "indexed_rows": self.indexed_rows,
64            "index_dim": self.index_dim,
65        }
66
67    def __str__(self) -> str:
68        """Render a human-readable multi-line summary of the build statistics.
69
70        :return: Formatted string with repo root, db path, node/edge counts,
71                 and (if available) indexed vector count and dimension.
72        """
73        lines = [
74            f"repo_root   : {self.repo_root}",
75            f"db_path     : {self.db_path}",
76            f"nodes       : {self.total_nodes}  {self.node_counts}",
77            f"edges       : {self.total_edges}  {self.edge_counts}",
78        ]
79        if self.indexed_rows is not None:
80            lines.append(f"indexed     : {self.indexed_rows} vectors  dim={self.index_dim}")
81        return "\n".join(lines)

Statistics returned by KGModule.build() and related methods.

Parameters
  • repo_root: Repository root that was analysed.
  • db_path: Path to the SQLite database.
  • total_nodes: Total nodes written to SQLite.
  • total_edges: Total edges written to SQLite.
  • node_counts: Node counts broken down by kind.
  • edge_counts: Edge counts broken down by relation.
  • indexed_rows: Number of nodes embedded into LanceDB (None if the index was not built).
  • index_dim: Embedding dimension (None if not built).
BuildStats( repo_root: str, db_path: str, total_nodes: int, total_edges: int, node_counts: dict[str, int], edge_counts: dict[str, int], indexed_rows: int | None = None, index_dim: int | None = None)
repo_root: str
db_path: str
total_nodes: int
total_edges: int
node_counts: dict[str, int]
edge_counts: dict[str, int]
indexed_rows: int | None = None
index_dim: int | None = None
def to_dict(self) -> dict: 
51    def to_dict(self) -> dict:
52        """Serialise the stats to a plain dictionary.
53
54        :return: Dictionary containing all ``BuildStats`` fields.
55        """
56        return {
57            "repo_root": self.repo_root,
58            "db_path": self.db_path,
59            "total_nodes": self.total_nodes,
60            "total_edges": self.total_edges,
61            "node_counts": self.node_counts,
62            "edge_counts": self.edge_counts,
63            "indexed_rows": self.indexed_rows,
64            "index_dim": self.index_dim,
65        }

Serialise the stats to a plain dictionary.

Returns

Dictionary containing all BuildStats fields.

@dataclass
class QueryResult: 
 84@dataclass
 85class QueryResult:
 86    """
 87    Result of a hybrid query (:meth:`KGModule.query`).
 88
 89    :param query: Original query string.
 90    :param seeds: Number of semantic seed nodes.
 91    :param expanded_nodes: Total nodes after graph expansion.
 92    :param returned_nodes: Nodes returned after filtering.
 93    :param hop: Hop count used.
 94    :param rels: Edge relations used for expansion.
 95    :param nodes: List of node dicts (sorted by rank).
 96    :param edges: List of edge dicts within the returned node set.
 97    """
 98
 99    query: str
100    seeds: int
101    expanded_nodes: int
102    returned_nodes: int
103    hop: int
104    rels: list[str]
105    nodes: list[dict]
106    edges: list[dict]
107
108    def to_dict(self) -> dict:
109        """Serialise the query result to a plain dictionary.
110
111        :return: Dictionary containing all ``QueryResult`` fields.
112        """
113        return {
114            "query": self.query,
115            "seeds": self.seeds,
116            "expanded_nodes": self.expanded_nodes,
117            "returned_nodes": self.returned_nodes,
118            "hop": self.hop,
119            "rels": self.rels,
120            "nodes": self.nodes,
121            "edges": self.edges,
122        }
123
124    def to_json(self, *, indent: int = 2) -> str:
125        """Serialise to JSON string."""
126        return json.dumps(self.to_dict(), indent=indent, ensure_ascii=False)
127
128    def print_summary(self) -> None:
129        """Print a human-readable summary of the query result to stdout."""
130        sep = "=" * 80
131        print(sep)
132        print(f"QUERY: {self.query}")
133        print(
134            f"Seeds: {self.seeds} | Expanded: {self.expanded_nodes} "
135            f"| Returned: {self.returned_nodes} | hop={self.hop}"
136        )
137        print(f"Rels: {', '.join(self.rels)}")
138        print(sep)
139        for n in self.nodes:
140            print(
141                f"{n['kind']:8s} {(n.get('module_path') or ''):40s} "
142                f"{n.get('qualname') or n['name']}  [{n['id']}]"
143            )
144            if n.get("docstring"):
145                ds0 = n["docstring"].strip().splitlines()[0]
146                print(f"    {ds0[:120]}")
147            print()
148        print("-" * 80)
149        print(f"EDGES (within returned set): {len(self.edges)}")
150        print("-" * 80)
151        for e in sorted(self.edges, key=lambda x: (x["rel"], x["src"], x["dst"])):
152            print(f"  {e['src']} -[{e['rel']}]-> {e['dst']}")
153        print(sep)

Result of a hybrid query (KGModule.query()).

Parameters
  • query: Original query string.
  • seeds: Number of semantic seed nodes.
  • expanded_nodes: Total nodes after graph expansion.
  • returned_nodes: Nodes returned after filtering.
  • hop: Hop count used.
  • rels: Edge relations used for expansion.
  • nodes: List of node dicts (sorted by rank).
  • edges: List of edge dicts within the returned node set.
QueryResult( query: str, seeds: int, expanded_nodes: int, returned_nodes: int, hop: int, rels: list[str], nodes: list[dict], edges: list[dict])
query: str
seeds: int
expanded_nodes: int
returned_nodes: int
hop: int
rels: list[str]
nodes: list[dict]
edges: list[dict]
def to_dict(self) -> dict: 
108    def to_dict(self) -> dict:
109        """Serialise the query result to a plain dictionary.
110
111        :return: Dictionary containing all ``QueryResult`` fields.
112        """
113        return {
114            "query": self.query,
115            "seeds": self.seeds,
116            "expanded_nodes": self.expanded_nodes,
117            "returned_nodes": self.returned_nodes,
118            "hop": self.hop,
119            "rels": self.rels,
120            "nodes": self.nodes,
121            "edges": self.edges,
122        }

Serialise the query result to a plain dictionary.

Returns

Dictionary containing all QueryResult fields.

def to_json(self, *, indent: int = 2) -> str: 
124    def to_json(self, *, indent: int = 2) -> str:
125        """Serialise to JSON string."""
126        return json.dumps(self.to_dict(), indent=indent, ensure_ascii=False)

Serialise to JSON string.

def print_summary(self) -> None: 
128    def print_summary(self) -> None:
129        """Print a human-readable summary of the query result to stdout."""
130        sep = "=" * 80
131        print(sep)
132        print(f"QUERY: {self.query}")
133        print(
134            f"Seeds: {self.seeds} | Expanded: {self.expanded_nodes} "
135            f"| Returned: {self.returned_nodes} | hop={self.hop}"
136        )
137        print(f"Rels: {', '.join(self.rels)}")
138        print(sep)
139        for n in self.nodes:
140            print(
141                f"{n['kind']:8s} {(n.get('module_path') or ''):40s} "
142                f"{n.get('qualname') or n['name']}  [{n['id']}]"
143            )
144            if n.get("docstring"):
145                ds0 = n["docstring"].strip().splitlines()[0]
146                print(f"    {ds0[:120]}")
147            print()
148        print("-" * 80)
149        print(f"EDGES (within returned set): {len(self.edges)}")
150        print("-" * 80)
151        for e in sorted(self.edges, key=lambda x: (x["rel"], x["src"], x["dst"])):
152            print(f"  {e['src']} -[{e['rel']}]-> {e['dst']}")
153        print(sep)

Print a human-readable summary of the query result to stdout.

@dataclass
class Snippet: 
156@dataclass
157class Snippet:
158    """
159    A source-grounded snippet.
160
161    :param path: Repo-relative file path.
162    :param start: 1-based start line (inclusive).
163    :param end: 1-based end line (inclusive).
164    :param text: Line-numbered source text.
165    """
166
167    path: str
168    start: int
169    end: int
170    text: str
171
172    def to_dict(self) -> dict:
173        """Serialise the snippet to a plain dictionary.
174
175        :return: Dictionary with ``path``, ``start``, ``end``, and ``text`` keys.
176        """
177        return {"path": self.path, "start": self.start, "end": self.end, "text": self.text}

A source-grounded snippet.

Parameters
  • path: Repo-relative file path.
  • start: 1-based start line (inclusive).
  • end: 1-based end line (inclusive).
  • text: Line-numbered source text.
Snippet(path: str, start: int, end: int, text: str)
path: str
start: int
end: int
text: str
def to_dict(self) -> dict: 
172    def to_dict(self) -> dict:
173        """Serialise the snippet to a plain dictionary.
174
175        :return: Dictionary with ``path``, ``start``, ``end``, and ``text`` keys.
176        """
177        return {"path": self.path, "start": self.start, "end": self.end, "text": self.text}

Serialise the snippet to a plain dictionary.

Returns

Dictionary with path, start, end, and text keys.

@dataclass
class SnippetPack: 
180@dataclass
181class SnippetPack:
182    """
183    Result of :meth:`KGModule.pack` — nodes with attached source snippets.
184
185    :param query: Original query string.
186    :param seeds: Number of semantic seed nodes.
187    :param expanded_nodes: Total nodes after graph expansion.
188    :param returned_nodes: Nodes returned after deduplication.
189    :param hop: Hop count used.
190    :param rels: Edge relations used for expansion.
191    :param model: Embedding model name.
192    :param nodes: Node dicts, each optionally containing a ``snippet`` key.
193    :param edges: Edge dicts within the returned node set.
194    :param warnings: Non-fatal issues encountered during packing.
195    """
196
197    query: str
198    seeds: int
199    expanded_nodes: int
200    returned_nodes: int
201    hop: int
202    rels: list[str]
203    model: str
204    nodes: list[dict]
205    edges: list[dict]
206    warnings: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
207
208    def to_dict(self) -> dict:
209        """Serialise the snippet pack to a plain dictionary."""
210        return {
211            "query": self.query,
212            "seeds": self.seeds,
213            "expanded_nodes": self.expanded_nodes,
214            "returned_nodes": self.returned_nodes,
215            "hop": self.hop,
216            "rels": self.rels,
217            "model": self.model,
218            "nodes": self.nodes,
219            "edges": self.edges,
220            "warnings": self.warnings,
221        }
222
223    def to_json(self, *, indent: int = 2) -> str:
224        """Serialise to JSON string."""
225        return json.dumps(self.to_dict(), indent=indent, ensure_ascii=False)
226
227    def to_markdown(self) -> str:
228        """Render the snippet pack as a Markdown context document.
229
230        :return: Markdown-formatted string representing the full context pack.
231        """
232        out: list[str] = []
233        out.append("# KGModule Snippet Pack\n")
234        out.append(f"**Query:** `{self.query}`  ")
235        out.append(f"**Seeds:** {self.seeds}  ")
236        out.append(f"**Expanded nodes:** {self.expanded_nodes} (returned: {self.returned_nodes})  ")
237        out.append(f"**hop:** {self.hop}  ")
238        out.append(f"**rels:** {', '.join(self.rels)}  ")
239        out.append(f"**model:** {self.model}  ")
240        out.append("\n---\n")
241
242        if self.warnings:
243            out.append("## Warnings\n")
244            for w in self.warnings:
245                out.append(f"- {w}")
246            out.append("")
247
248        out.append("## Nodes\n")
249        for n in self.nodes:
250            out.append(f"### {n['kind']} — `{n.get('qualname') or n['name']}`")
251            out.append(f"- id: `{n['id']}`")
252            if n.get("module_path"):
253                out.append(f"- module: `{n['module_path']}`")
254            if n.get("lineno") is not None:
255                out.append(f"- line: {n['lineno']}")
256            if n.get("docstring"):
257                ds0 = n["docstring"].strip().splitlines()[0]
258                out.append(f"- doc: {ds0[:140]}")
259            if n.get("relevance"):
260                rel = n["relevance"]
261                _score = rel.get("score", 0.0)
262                _tier = "HIGH" if _score >= 0.60 else ("MEDIUM" if _score >= 0.45 else "LOW")
263                out.append(
264                    "- relevance: "
265                    f"{_score:.3f} [{_tier}] "
266                    f"(semantic={rel.get('semantic', 0.0):.3f}, "
267                    f"lexical={rel.get('lexical', 0.0):.3f}, "
268                    f"docstring_signal={rel.get('docstring_signal', 0.0):.3f}, "
269                    f"hop={rel.get('hop', 0)})"
270                )
271            sn = n.get("snippet")
272            if sn:
273                out.append("")
274                out.append(f"```\n{sn['text']}\n```")
275            out.append("")
276
277        out.append("\n---\n")
278        out.append("## Edges\n")
279        for e in self.edges:
280            out.append(f"- `{e['src']}` -[{e['rel']}]-> `{e['dst']}`")
281        out.append("")
282        return "\n".join(out)
283
284    def save(self, path: str | Path, *, fmt: str = "md") -> None:
285        """Write the pack to a file.
286
287        :param path: Output file path.
288        :param fmt: ``"md"`` for Markdown or ``"json"`` for JSON.
289        """
290        text = self.to_markdown() if fmt == "md" else self.to_json()
291        Path(path).write_text(text, encoding="utf-8")

Result of KGModule.pack() — nodes with attached source snippets.

Parameters
  • query: Original query string.
  • seeds: Number of semantic seed nodes.
  • expanded_nodes: Total nodes after graph expansion.
  • returned_nodes: Nodes returned after deduplication.
  • hop: Hop count used.
  • rels: Edge relations used for expansion.
  • model: Embedding model name.
  • nodes: Node dicts, each optionally containing a snippet key.
  • edges: Edge dicts within the returned node set.
  • warnings: Non-fatal issues encountered during packing.
SnippetPack( query: str, seeds: int, expanded_nodes: int, returned_nodes: int, hop: int, rels: list[str], model: str, nodes: list[dict], edges: list[dict], warnings: list[str] = <factory>)
query: str
seeds: int
expanded_nodes: int
returned_nodes: int
hop: int
rels: list[str]
model: str
nodes: list[dict]
edges: list[dict]
warnings: list[str]
def to_dict(self) -> dict: 
208    def to_dict(self) -> dict:
209        """Serialise the snippet pack to a plain dictionary."""
210        return {
211            "query": self.query,
212            "seeds": self.seeds,
213            "expanded_nodes": self.expanded_nodes,
214            "returned_nodes": self.returned_nodes,
215            "hop": self.hop,
216            "rels": self.rels,
217            "model": self.model,
218            "nodes": self.nodes,
219            "edges": self.edges,
220            "warnings": self.warnings,
221        }

Serialise the snippet pack to a plain dictionary.

def to_json(self, *, indent: int = 2) -> str: 
223    def to_json(self, *, indent: int = 2) -> str:
224        """Serialise to JSON string."""
225        return json.dumps(self.to_dict(), indent=indent, ensure_ascii=False)

Serialise to JSON string.

def to_markdown(self) -> str: 
227    def to_markdown(self) -> str:
228        """Render the snippet pack as a Markdown context document.
229
230        :return: Markdown-formatted string representing the full context pack.
231        """
232        out: list[str] = []
233        out.append("# KGModule Snippet Pack\n")
234        out.append(f"**Query:** `{self.query}`  ")
235        out.append(f"**Seeds:** {self.seeds}  ")
236        out.append(f"**Expanded nodes:** {self.expanded_nodes} (returned: {self.returned_nodes})  ")
237        out.append(f"**hop:** {self.hop}  ")
238        out.append(f"**rels:** {', '.join(self.rels)}  ")
239        out.append(f"**model:** {self.model}  ")
240        out.append("\n---\n")
241
242        if self.warnings:
243            out.append("## Warnings\n")
244            for w in self.warnings:
245                out.append(f"- {w}")
246            out.append("")
247
248        out.append("## Nodes\n")
249        for n in self.nodes:
250            out.append(f"### {n['kind']} — `{n.get('qualname') or n['name']}`")
251            out.append(f"- id: `{n['id']}`")
252            if n.get("module_path"):
253                out.append(f"- module: `{n['module_path']}`")
254            if n.get("lineno") is not None:
255                out.append(f"- line: {n['lineno']}")
256            if n.get("docstring"):
257                ds0 = n["docstring"].strip().splitlines()[0]
258                out.append(f"- doc: {ds0[:140]}")
259            if n.get("relevance"):
260                rel = n["relevance"]
261                _score = rel.get("score", 0.0)
262                _tier = "HIGH" if _score >= 0.60 else ("MEDIUM" if _score >= 0.45 else "LOW")
263                out.append(
264                    "- relevance: "
265                    f"{_score:.3f} [{_tier}] "
266                    f"(semantic={rel.get('semantic', 0.0):.3f}, "
267                    f"lexical={rel.get('lexical', 0.0):.3f}, "
268                    f"docstring_signal={rel.get('docstring_signal', 0.0):.3f}, "
269                    f"hop={rel.get('hop', 0)})"
270                )
271            sn = n.get("snippet")
272            if sn:
273                out.append("")
274                out.append(f"```\n{sn['text']}\n```")
275            out.append("")
276
277        out.append("\n---\n")
278        out.append("## Edges\n")
279        for e in self.edges:
280            out.append(f"- `{e['src']}` -[{e['rel']}]-> `{e['dst']}`")
281        out.append("")
282        return "\n".join(out)

Render the snippet pack as a Markdown context document.

Returns

Markdown-formatted string representing the full context pack.

def save(self, path: str | pathlib.Path, *, fmt: str = 'md') -> None: 
284    def save(self, path: str | Path, *, fmt: str = "md") -> None:
285        """Write the pack to a file.
286
287        :param path: Output file path.
288        :param fmt: ``"md"`` for Markdown or ``"json"`` for JSON.
289        """
290        text = self.to_markdown() if fmt == "md" else self.to_json()
291        Path(path).write_text(text, encoding="utf-8")

Write the pack to a file.

Parameters
  • path: Output file path.
  • fmt: "md" for Markdown or "json" for JSON.
class Snapshot(kg_snapshot.snapshots.Snapshot): 
156class Snapshot(_BaseSnapshot):
157    """pycode-kg Snapshot with attribute-accessible metrics and delta fields.
158
159    Subclasses the shared ``kg_rag.snapshots.Snapshot`` so that:
160
161    - ``snapshot.metrics`` returns a :class:`SnapshotMetrics` dataclass built
162      lazily from the underlying metrics dict.
163    - ``snapshot.vs_previous`` / ``snapshot.vs_baseline`` return
164      :class:`SnapshotDelta` dataclasses (or ``None``) built lazily from the
165      underlying delta dicts.
166    - All serialization goes through the shared ``to_dict`` / ``from_dict``
167      which store plain dicts, ensuring on-disk format compatibility.
168    """
169
170    # Class-level type declarations for the raw storage attributes
171    _metrics_raw: dict[str, Any]
172    _vs_previous_raw: dict[str, Any] | None
173    _vs_baseline_raw: dict[str, Any] | None
174
175    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
176    # Attribute-access shims
177    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
178
179    @property  # type: ignore[override]
180    def metrics(self) -> SnapshotMetrics:  # type: ignore[override]
181        return metrics_from_dict(self._metrics_raw)
182
183    @metrics.setter
184    def metrics(self, value: SnapshotMetrics | dict[str, Any]) -> None:
185        self._metrics_raw = metrics_to_dict(value) if isinstance(value, SnapshotMetrics) else value
186
187    @property  # type: ignore[override]
188    def vs_previous(self) -> SnapshotDelta | None:  # type: ignore[override]
189        return delta_from_dict(self._vs_previous_raw)
190
191    @vs_previous.setter
192    def vs_previous(self, value: SnapshotDelta | dict[str, Any] | None) -> None:
193        self._vs_previous_raw = delta_to_dict(value) if isinstance(value, SnapshotDelta) else value
194
195    @property  # type: ignore[override]
196    def vs_baseline(self) -> SnapshotDelta | None:  # type: ignore[override]
197        return delta_from_dict(self._vs_baseline_raw)
198
199    @vs_baseline.setter
200    def vs_baseline(self, value: SnapshotDelta | dict[str, Any] | None) -> None:
201        self._vs_baseline_raw = delta_to_dict(value) if isinstance(value, SnapshotDelta) else value
202
203    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
204    # __init__: store raw dicts, pass them to super
205    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
206
207    def __init__(
208        self,
209        branch: str,
210        timestamp: str,
211        metrics: SnapshotMetrics | dict[str, Any],
212        version: str = "",
213        hotspots: list[dict[str, Any]] | None = None,
214        issues: list[str] | None = None,
215        vs_previous: SnapshotDelta | dict[str, Any] | None = None,
216        vs_baseline: SnapshotDelta | dict[str, Any] | None = None,
217        tree_hash: str = "",
218    ) -> None:
219        self._metrics_raw = (
220            metrics_to_dict(metrics) if isinstance(metrics, SnapshotMetrics) else (metrics or {})
221        )
222        self._vs_previous_raw = (
223            delta_to_dict(vs_previous) if isinstance(vs_previous, SnapshotDelta) else vs_previous
224        )
225        self._vs_baseline_raw = (
226            delta_to_dict(vs_baseline) if isinstance(vs_baseline, SnapshotDelta) else vs_baseline
227        )
228        super().__init__(
229            branch=branch,
230            timestamp=timestamp,
231            metrics=self._metrics_raw,
232            version=version,
233            hotspots=hotspots or [],
234            issues=issues or [],
235            vs_previous=self._vs_previous_raw,
236            vs_baseline=self._vs_baseline_raw,
237            tree_hash=tree_hash,
238        )
239
240    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
241    # to_dict / from_dict — keep shared dict format on disk
242    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
243
244    def to_dict(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
245        """Serialize to JSON-compatible dict (plain dicts for all nested fields)."""
246        return {
247            "key": self.tree_hash,
248            "branch": self.branch,
249            "timestamp": self.timestamp,
250            "version": self.version,
251            "metrics": self._metrics_raw,
252            "hotspots": self.hotspots,
253            "issues": self.issues,
254            "vs_previous": self._vs_previous_raw,
255            "vs_baseline": self._vs_baseline_raw,
256        }
257
258    @staticmethod
259    def from_dict(data: dict[str, Any]) -> Snapshot:
260        """Reconstruct from a dict loaded from JSON."""
261        raw = dict(data)
262
263        metrics_data = raw.pop("metrics", {})
264        vs_prev_data = raw.pop("vs_previous", None)
265        vs_base_data = raw.pop("vs_baseline", None)
266
267        # Normalise legacy 'tree_hash' field → 'key'
268        if "key" not in raw and "tree_hash" in raw:
269            raw["key"] = raw.pop("tree_hash")
270        else:
271            raw.pop("tree_hash", None)
272
273        key = raw.pop("key", "")
274        raw.pop("commit", None)  # drop legacy field
275        raw.setdefault("version", "")
276
277        return Snapshot(
278            tree_hash=key,
279            metrics=metrics_data,
280            vs_previous=vs_prev_data,
281            vs_baseline=vs_base_data,
282            branch=raw.pop("branch", ""),
283            timestamp=raw.pop("timestamp", ""),
284            version=raw.pop("version", ""),
285            hotspots=raw.pop("hotspots", []),
286            issues=raw.pop("issues", []),
287        )

pycode-kg Snapshot with attribute-accessible metrics and delta fields.

Subclasses the shared kg_rag.snapshots.Snapshot so that:

  • snapshot.metrics returns a SnapshotMetrics dataclass built lazily from the underlying metrics dict.
  • snapshot.vs_previous / snapshot.vs_baseline return SnapshotDelta dataclasses (or None) built lazily from the underlying delta dicts.
  • All serialization goes through the shared to_dict / from_dict which store plain dicts, ensuring on-disk format compatibility.
Snapshot( branch: str, timestamp: str, metrics: SnapshotMetrics | dict[str, typing.Any], version: str = '', hotspots: list[dict[str, typing.Any]] | None = None, issues: list[str] | None = None, vs_previous: SnapshotDelta | dict[str, typing.Any] | None = None, vs_baseline: SnapshotDelta | dict[str, typing.Any] | None = None, tree_hash: str = '') 
207    def __init__(
208        self,
209        branch: str,
210        timestamp: str,
211        metrics: SnapshotMetrics | dict[str, Any],
212        version: str = "",
213        hotspots: list[dict[str, Any]] | None = None,
214        issues: list[str] | None = None,
215        vs_previous: SnapshotDelta | dict[str, Any] | None = None,
216        vs_baseline: SnapshotDelta | dict[str, Any] | None = None,
217        tree_hash: str = "",
218    ) -> None:
219        self._metrics_raw = (
220            metrics_to_dict(metrics) if isinstance(metrics, SnapshotMetrics) else (metrics or {})
221        )
222        self._vs_previous_raw = (
223            delta_to_dict(vs_previous) if isinstance(vs_previous, SnapshotDelta) else vs_previous
224        )
225        self._vs_baseline_raw = (
226            delta_to_dict(vs_baseline) if isinstance(vs_baseline, SnapshotDelta) else vs_baseline
227        )
228        super().__init__(
229            branch=branch,
230            timestamp=timestamp,
231            metrics=self._metrics_raw,
232            version=version,
233            hotspots=hotspots or [],
234            issues=issues or [],
235            vs_previous=self._vs_previous_raw,
236            vs_baseline=self._vs_baseline_raw,
237            tree_hash=tree_hash,
238        )
metrics: SnapshotMetrics 
179    @property  # type: ignore[override]
180    def metrics(self) -> SnapshotMetrics:  # type: ignore[override]
181        return metrics_from_dict(self._metrics_raw)
vs_previous: SnapshotDelta | None 
187    @property  # type: ignore[override]
188    def vs_previous(self) -> SnapshotDelta | None:  # type: ignore[override]
189        return delta_from_dict(self._vs_previous_raw)
vs_baseline: SnapshotDelta | None 
195    @property  # type: ignore[override]
196    def vs_baseline(self) -> SnapshotDelta | None:  # type: ignore[override]
197        return delta_from_dict(self._vs_baseline_raw)
def to_dict(self) -> dict[str, typing.Any]: 
244    def to_dict(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
245        """Serialize to JSON-compatible dict (plain dicts for all nested fields)."""
246        return {
247            "key": self.tree_hash,
248            "branch": self.branch,
249            "timestamp": self.timestamp,
250            "version": self.version,
251            "metrics": self._metrics_raw,
252            "hotspots": self.hotspots,
253            "issues": self.issues,
254            "vs_previous": self._vs_previous_raw,
255            "vs_baseline": self._vs_baseline_raw,
256        }

Serialize to JSON-compatible dict (plain dicts for all nested fields).

@staticmethod
def from_dict(data: dict[str, typing.Any]) -> Snapshot: 
258    @staticmethod
259    def from_dict(data: dict[str, Any]) -> Snapshot:
260        """Reconstruct from a dict loaded from JSON."""
261        raw = dict(data)
262
263        metrics_data = raw.pop("metrics", {})
264        vs_prev_data = raw.pop("vs_previous", None)
265        vs_base_data = raw.pop("vs_baseline", None)
266
267        # Normalise legacy 'tree_hash' field → 'key'
268        if "key" not in raw and "tree_hash" in raw:
269            raw["key"] = raw.pop("tree_hash")
270        else:
271            raw.pop("tree_hash", None)
272
273        key = raw.pop("key", "")
274        raw.pop("commit", None)  # drop legacy field
275        raw.setdefault("version", "")
276
277        return Snapshot(
278            tree_hash=key,
279            metrics=metrics_data,
280            vs_previous=vs_prev_data,
281            vs_baseline=vs_base_data,
282            branch=raw.pop("branch", ""),
283            timestamp=raw.pop("timestamp", ""),
284            version=raw.pop("version", ""),
285            hotspots=raw.pop("hotspots", []),
286            issues=raw.pop("issues", []),
287        )

Reconstruct from a dict loaded from JSON.

@dataclass
class SnapshotDelta: 
76@dataclass
77class SnapshotDelta:
78    """Deltas comparing this snapshot to a baseline or previous snapshot."""
79
80    nodes: int = 0
81    edges: int = 0
82    coverage_delta: float = 0.0
83    critical_issues_delta: int = 0

Deltas comparing this snapshot to a baseline or previous snapshot.

SnapshotDelta( nodes: int = 0, edges: int = 0, coverage_delta: float = 0.0, critical_issues_delta: int = 0)
nodes: int = 0
edges: int = 0
coverage_delta: float = 0.0
critical_issues_delta: int = 0
@dataclass
class SnapshotManifest: 
140@dataclass
141class SnapshotManifest:
142    """Index of all snapshots, with fast lookup by tree hash."""
143
144    format_version: str = "1.0"
145    last_update: str = ""
146    snapshots: list[dict[str, Any]] = field(default_factory=list)
147
148    def to_dict(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
149        """Serialize to dict."""
150        return {
151            "format": self.format_version,
152            "last_update": self.last_update,
153            "snapshots": self.snapshots,
154        }
155
156    @staticmethod
157    def from_dict(data: dict[str, Any]) -> SnapshotManifest:
158        """Reconstruct from dict."""
159        return SnapshotManifest(
160            format_version=data.get("format", "1.0"),
161            last_update=data.get("last_update", ""),
162            snapshots=data.get("snapshots", []),
163        )

Index of all snapshots, with fast lookup by tree hash.

SnapshotManifest( format_version: str = '1.0', last_update: str = '', snapshots: list[dict[str, typing.Any]] = <factory>)
format_version: str = '1.0'
last_update: str = ''
snapshots: list[dict[str, typing.Any]]
def to_dict(self) -> dict[str, typing.Any]: 
148    def to_dict(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
149        """Serialize to dict."""
150        return {
151            "format": self.format_version,
152            "last_update": self.last_update,
153            "snapshots": self.snapshots,
154        }

Serialize to dict.

@staticmethod
def from_dict(data: dict[str, typing.Any]) -> kg_snapshot.snapshots.SnapshotManifest: 
156    @staticmethod
157    def from_dict(data: dict[str, Any]) -> SnapshotManifest:
158        """Reconstruct from dict."""
159        return SnapshotManifest(
160            format_version=data.get("format", "1.0"),
161            last_update=data.get("last_update", ""),
162            snapshots=data.get("snapshots", []),
163        )

Reconstruct from dict.

class SnapshotManager(kg_snapshot.snapshots.SnapshotManager): 
295class SnapshotManager(_BaseSnapshotManager):
296    """pycode-kg specific snapshot manager.
297
298    Extends the shared :class:`kg_rag.snapshots.SnapshotManager` with:
299
300    - ``package_name="pycode-kg"`` for automatic version detection.
301    - Domain-specific delta fields: ``coverage_delta`` and
302      ``critical_issues_delta`` in addition to the base ``nodes`` / ``edges``.
303    - ``_collect_module_node_counts()`` — SQLite query for per-module node
304      counts, stored in snapshot metrics under ``"module_node_counts"``.
305
306    The ``capture()`` signature is extended with named keyword arguments
307    (``coverage``, ``critical_issues``, ``complexity_median``) so that
308    existing call-sites in the CLI do not need to be changed.
309    """
310
311    def __init__(
312        self,
313        snapshots_dir: Path | str,
314        *,
315        db_path: Path | str | None = None,
316        package_name: str = "pycode-kg",
317    ) -> None:
318        super().__init__(snapshots_dir, package_name=package_name, db_path=db_path)
319
320    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
321    # Helper: wrap a base Snapshot in the local subclass
322    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
323
324    @staticmethod
325    def _wrap_snapshot(base: _BaseSnapshot) -> Snapshot:
326        return Snapshot(
327            branch=base.branch,
328            timestamp=base.timestamp,
329            version=base.version,
330            metrics=base.metrics,
331            hotspots=base.hotspots,
332            issues=base.issues,
333            vs_previous=base.vs_previous,
334            vs_baseline=base.vs_baseline,
335            tree_hash=base.tree_hash,
336        )
337
338    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
339    # capture — extended signature for backwards compat
340    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
341
342    def capture(  # type: ignore[override]
343        self,
344        version: str | None = None,
345        branch: str | None = None,
346        graph_stats_dict: dict[str, Any] | None = None,
347        coverage: float = 0.0,
348        critical_issues: int = 0,
349        complexity_median: float = 0.0,
350        hotspots: list[dict[str, Any]] | None = None,
351        issues: list[str] | None = None,
352        tree_hash: str = "",
353    ) -> Snapshot:
354        """Capture a pycode-kg snapshot.
355
356        Merges ``graph_stats_dict`` with pycode-kg-specific metric fields
357        (``docstring_coverage``, ``critical_issues``, ``complexity_median``,
358        ``module_node_counts``) into a single metrics dict, then delegates to
359        the shared manager and returns a pycode-kg :class:`Snapshot` instance.
360
361        :param version: Version string; auto-detected from package if None.
362        :param branch: Git branch; auto-detected if None.
363        :param graph_stats_dict: Output from the KG ``stats()`` method.
364        :param coverage: Docstring coverage fraction (0.0–1.0).
365        :param critical_issues: Number of critical issues detected.
366        :param complexity_median: Median fan-in across functions.
367        :param hotspots: Top hotspot entries.
368        :param issues: Issue description strings.
369        :param tree_hash: Git tree hash; auto-detected if not provided.
370        :return: New :class:`Snapshot` instance (not yet persisted).
371        """
372        # Collect per-module counts from SQLite (returns {} if db unavailable)
373        module_node_counts = self._collect_module_node_counts()
374
375        # Call the base implementation passing extra fields via **extra_metrics
376        base_snap = super().capture(
377            version=version,
378            branch=branch,
379            graph_stats_dict=graph_stats_dict,
380            tree_hash=tree_hash,
381            hotspots=hotspots,
382            issues=issues,
383            # Extra domain-specific metric fields:
384            docstring_coverage=coverage,
385            critical_issues=critical_issues,
386            complexity_median=complexity_median,
387            module_node_counts=module_node_counts,
388        )
389
390        return self._wrap_snapshot(base_snap)
391
392    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
393    # Override load_snapshot to return pycode-kg Snapshot instances
394    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
395
396    def load_snapshot(self, key: str) -> Snapshot | None:
397        """Load a snapshot by key, returning a pycode-kg Snapshot instance."""
398        base_snap = super().load_snapshot(key)
399        return self._wrap_snapshot(base_snap) if base_snap is not None else None
400
401    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
402    # save_snapshot — convert dataclass fields to plain dicts for manifest
403    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
404
405    def save_snapshot(self, snapshot: Snapshot, *, force: bool = False) -> Path | None:  # type: ignore[override]
406        """Persist snapshot, normalising metrics to a plain dict for the manifest.
407
408        The base :meth:`~kg_snapshot.snapshots.SnapshotManager.save_snapshot`
409        writes ``snapshot.metrics`` directly into the manifest dict.  For
410        pycode-kg :class:`Snapshot` instances that property returns a
411        :class:`SnapshotMetrics` dataclass — not JSON-serializable.  This
412        override uses the raw dict for manifest entries while preserving the
413        base's dedup behaviour.
414
415        :param snapshot: Snapshot to persist.
416        :param force: If ``True``, always write a new history entry.
417        :return: Path to the saved JSON file, or ``None`` if unchanged (dedup).
418        :raises ValueError: If ``total_nodes`` is 0.
419        """
420        metrics_dict = snapshot._metrics_raw
421        if metrics_dict.get("total_nodes", 0) == 0:
422            raise ValueError(
423                "Refusing to save degenerate snapshot with 0 nodes. "
424                "Build the KG before capturing a snapshot."
425            )
426
427        manifest = self.load_manifest()
428
429        # Dedup: if version + metrics unchanged, refresh the latest entry in-place.
430        if not force and manifest.snapshots:
431            latest = max(manifest.snapshots, key=lambda x: x.get("timestamp", ""))
432            if snapshot.version == latest.get("version", "") and not self._metrics_changed(
433                metrics_dict, latest.get("metrics", {})
434            ):
435                old_key = latest["key"]
436                old_file = self.snapshots_dir / latest.get("file", f"{old_key}.json")
437                snapshot_file = self.snapshots_dir / f"{snapshot.key}.json"
438                snapshot_file.write_text(
439                    json.dumps(snapshot.to_dict(), indent=2) + "\n",
440                    encoding="utf-8",
441                )
442                if old_key != snapshot.key and old_file.exists():
443                    old_file.unlink()
444                latest.update(
445                    key=snapshot.key,
446                    branch=snapshot.branch,
447                    timestamp=snapshot.timestamp,
448                    file=snapshot_file.name,
449                )
450                manifest.last_update = datetime.now(UTC).isoformat()
451                self._save_manifest(manifest)
452                return snapshot_file
453
454        # Normal path: new or changed snapshot.
455        snapshot_file = self.snapshots_dir / f"{snapshot.key}.json"
456        snapshot_file.write_text(json.dumps(snapshot.to_dict(), indent=2) + "\n", encoding="utf-8")
457
458        existing_idx = next(
459            (i for i, s in enumerate(manifest.snapshots) if s.get("key") == snapshot.key),
460            None,
461        )
462        vs_prev = snapshot._vs_previous_raw
463        vs_base = snapshot._vs_baseline_raw
464        manifest_entry: dict[str, Any] = {
465            "key": snapshot.key,
466            "branch": snapshot.branch,
467            "timestamp": snapshot.timestamp,
468            "version": snapshot.version,
469            "file": snapshot_file.name,
470            "metrics": metrics_dict,
471            "deltas": {"vs_previous": vs_prev, "vs_baseline": vs_base},
472        }
473        if existing_idx is not None:
474            manifest.snapshots[existing_idx] = manifest_entry
475        else:
476            manifest.snapshots.append(manifest_entry)
477        manifest.last_update = datetime.now(UTC).isoformat()
478        self._save_manifest(manifest)
479        return snapshot_file
480
481    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
482    # Delta computation — adds coverage_delta and critical_issues_delta
483    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
484
485    @staticmethod
486    def _as_metrics_dict(m: SnapshotMetrics | dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
487        """Return a plain metrics dict whether ``m`` is a dataclass or already a dict."""
488        return metrics_to_dict(m) if isinstance(m, SnapshotMetrics) else m  # type: ignore[arg-type]
489
490    def _compute_delta(self, snap_new: Snapshot, snap_old: Snapshot) -> dict[str, Any]:
491        """Compute pycode-kg metrics delta including coverage and issue count."""
492        return self._compute_delta_from_metrics(
493            self._as_metrics_dict(snap_new.metrics),
494            self._as_metrics_dict(snap_old.metrics),
495        )
496
497    def _compute_delta_from_metrics(
498        self, new_m: dict[str, Any], old_m: dict[str, Any]
499    ) -> dict[str, Any]:
500        """Compute delta dict from two raw metrics dicts.
501
502        Includes the base ``nodes`` / ``edges`` plus the pycode-kg-specific
503        ``coverage_delta`` and ``critical_issues_delta`` fields.
504        """
505        return {
506            "nodes": new_m.get("total_nodes", 0) - old_m.get("total_nodes", 0),
507            "edges": new_m.get("total_edges", 0) - old_m.get("total_edges", 0),
508            "coverage_delta": new_m.get("docstring_coverage", 0.0)
509            - old_m.get("docstring_coverage", 0.0),
510            "critical_issues_delta": new_m.get("critical_issues", 0)
511            - old_m.get("critical_issues", 0),
512        }
513
514    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
515    # diff_snapshots — includes module_node_counts_delta and issues_delta
516    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
517
518    def diff_snapshots(self, key_a: str, key_b: str) -> dict[str, Any]:
519        """Compare two snapshots side-by-side.
520
521        Extends the shared diff with ``module_node_counts_delta`` and
522        ``issues_delta`` (introduced / resolved issue strings).
523
524        :param key_a: First snapshot key (tree hash).
525        :param key_b: Second snapshot key (tree hash).
526        :return: Dict with metrics from both, computed deltas.
527        """
528        snap_a = self.load_snapshot(key_a)
529        snap_b = self.load_snapshot(key_b)
530
531        if not snap_a or not snap_b:
532            return {"error": "One or both snapshots not found"}
533
534        m_a = self._as_metrics_dict(snap_a.metrics)
535        m_b = self._as_metrics_dict(snap_b.metrics)
536
537        all_node_kinds = set(m_a.get("node_counts", {})) | set(m_b.get("node_counts", {}))
538        all_edge_rels = set(m_a.get("edge_counts", {})) | set(m_b.get("edge_counts", {}))
539
540        node_counts_delta = {
541            k: m_b.get("node_counts", {}).get(k, 0) - m_a.get("node_counts", {}).get(k, 0)
542            for k in all_node_kinds
543        }
544        edge_counts_delta = {
545            k: m_b.get("edge_counts", {}).get(k, 0) - m_a.get("edge_counts", {}).get(k, 0)
546            for k in all_edge_rels
547        }
548
549        all_modules = set(m_a.get("module_node_counts", {})) | set(
550            m_b.get("module_node_counts", {})
551        )
552        module_node_counts_delta = {
553            mod: m_b.get("module_node_counts", {}).get(mod, 0)
554            - m_a.get("module_node_counts", {}).get(mod, 0)
555            for mod in all_modules
556            if m_b.get("module_node_counts", {}).get(mod, 0)
557            != m_a.get("module_node_counts", {}).get(mod, 0)
558        }
559
560        issues_a = set(snap_a.issues)
561        issues_b = set(snap_b.issues)
562
563        return {
564            "a": {"key": snap_a.key, "metrics": m_a, "issues": snap_a.issues},
565            "b": {"key": snap_b.key, "metrics": m_b, "issues": snap_b.issues},
566            "delta": self._compute_delta_from_metrics(m_b, m_a),
567            "node_counts_delta": node_counts_delta,
568            "edge_counts_delta": edge_counts_delta,
569            "module_node_counts_delta": module_node_counts_delta,
570            "issues_delta": {
571                "introduced": list(issues_b - issues_a),
572                "resolved": list(issues_a - issues_b),
573            },
574        }
575
576    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
577    # SQLite per-module node counts
578    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
579
580    def _collect_module_node_counts(self) -> dict[str, int]:
581        """Query SQLite for per-module node counts.
582
583        :return: Dict mapping ``module_path`` to node count, or ``{}`` if the
584                 database is unavailable or the query fails.
585        """
586        if not self.db_path or not self.db_path.exists():
587            return {}
588        try:
589            with sqlite3.connect(self.db_path) as conn:
590                rows = conn.execute(
591                    "SELECT module_path, COUNT(*) FROM nodes GROUP BY module_path"
592                ).fetchall()
593            return {row[0]: row[1] for row in rows if row[0]}
594        except sqlite3.Error:
595            return {}

pycode-kg specific snapshot manager.

Extends the shared kg_rag.snapshots.SnapshotManager with:

  • package_name="pycode-kg" for automatic version detection.
  • Domain-specific delta fields: coverage_delta and critical_issues_delta in addition to the base nodes / edges.
  • _collect_module_node_counts() — SQLite query for per-module node counts, stored in snapshot metrics under "module_node_counts".

The capture() signature is extended with named keyword arguments (coverage, critical_issues, complexity_median) so that existing call-sites in the CLI do not need to be changed.

SnapshotManager( snapshots_dir: pathlib.Path | str, *, db_path: pathlib.Path | str | None = None, package_name: str = 'pycode-kg') 
311    def __init__(
312        self,
313        snapshots_dir: Path | str,
314        *,
315        db_path: Path | str | None = None,
316        package_name: str = "pycode-kg",
317    ) -> None:
318        super().__init__(snapshots_dir, package_name=package_name, db_path=db_path)
def capture( self, version: str | None = None, branch: str | None = None, graph_stats_dict: dict[str, typing.Any] | None = None, coverage: float = 0.0, critical_issues: int = 0, complexity_median: float = 0.0, hotspots: list[dict[str, typing.Any]] | None = None, issues: list[str] | None = None, tree_hash: str = '') -> Snapshot: 
342    def capture(  # type: ignore[override]
343        self,
344        version: str | None = None,
345        branch: str | None = None,
346        graph_stats_dict: dict[str, Any] | None = None,
347        coverage: float = 0.0,
348        critical_issues: int = 0,
349        complexity_median: float = 0.0,
350        hotspots: list[dict[str, Any]] | None = None,
351        issues: list[str] | None = None,
352        tree_hash: str = "",
353    ) -> Snapshot:
354        """Capture a pycode-kg snapshot.
355
356        Merges ``graph_stats_dict`` with pycode-kg-specific metric fields
357        (``docstring_coverage``, ``critical_issues``, ``complexity_median``,
358        ``module_node_counts``) into a single metrics dict, then delegates to
359        the shared manager and returns a pycode-kg :class:`Snapshot` instance.
360
361        :param version: Version string; auto-detected from package if None.
362        :param branch: Git branch; auto-detected if None.
363        :param graph_stats_dict: Output from the KG ``stats()`` method.
364        :param coverage: Docstring coverage fraction (0.0–1.0).
365        :param critical_issues: Number of critical issues detected.
366        :param complexity_median: Median fan-in across functions.
367        :param hotspots: Top hotspot entries.
368        :param issues: Issue description strings.
369        :param tree_hash: Git tree hash; auto-detected if not provided.
370        :return: New :class:`Snapshot` instance (not yet persisted).
371        """
372        # Collect per-module counts from SQLite (returns {} if db unavailable)
373        module_node_counts = self._collect_module_node_counts()
374
375        # Call the base implementation passing extra fields via **extra_metrics
376        base_snap = super().capture(
377            version=version,
378            branch=branch,
379            graph_stats_dict=graph_stats_dict,
380            tree_hash=tree_hash,
381            hotspots=hotspots,
382            issues=issues,
383            # Extra domain-specific metric fields:
384            docstring_coverage=coverage,
385            critical_issues=critical_issues,
386            complexity_median=complexity_median,
387            module_node_counts=module_node_counts,
388        )
389
390        return self._wrap_snapshot(base_snap)

Capture a pycode-kg snapshot.

Merges graph_stats_dict with pycode-kg-specific metric fields (docstring_coverage, critical_issues, complexity_median, module_node_counts) into a single metrics dict, then delegates to the shared manager and returns a pycode-kg Snapshot instance.

Parameters
  • version: Version string; auto-detected from package if None.
  • branch: Git branch; auto-detected if None.
  • graph_stats_dict: Output from the KG stats() method.
  • coverage: Docstring coverage fraction (0.0–1.0).
  • critical_issues: Number of critical issues detected.
  • complexity_median: Median fan-in across functions.
  • hotspots: Top hotspot entries.
  • issues: Issue description strings.
  • tree_hash: Git tree hash; auto-detected if not provided.
Returns

New Snapshot instance (not yet persisted).

def load_snapshot(self, key: str) -> Snapshot | None: 
396    def load_snapshot(self, key: str) -> Snapshot | None:
397        """Load a snapshot by key, returning a pycode-kg Snapshot instance."""
398        base_snap = super().load_snapshot(key)
399        return self._wrap_snapshot(base_snap) if base_snap is not None else None

Load a snapshot by key, returning a pycode-kg Snapshot instance.

def save_snapshot( self, snapshot: Snapshot, *, force: bool = False) -> pathlib.Path | None: 
405    def save_snapshot(self, snapshot: Snapshot, *, force: bool = False) -> Path | None:  # type: ignore[override]
406        """Persist snapshot, normalising metrics to a plain dict for the manifest.
407
408        The base :meth:`~kg_snapshot.snapshots.SnapshotManager.save_snapshot`
409        writes ``snapshot.metrics`` directly into the manifest dict.  For
410        pycode-kg :class:`Snapshot` instances that property returns a
411        :class:`SnapshotMetrics` dataclass — not JSON-serializable.  This
412        override uses the raw dict for manifest entries while preserving the
413        base's dedup behaviour.
414
415        :param snapshot: Snapshot to persist.
416        :param force: If ``True``, always write a new history entry.
417        :return: Path to the saved JSON file, or ``None`` if unchanged (dedup).
418        :raises ValueError: If ``total_nodes`` is 0.
419        """
420        metrics_dict = snapshot._metrics_raw
421        if metrics_dict.get("total_nodes", 0) == 0:
422            raise ValueError(
423                "Refusing to save degenerate snapshot with 0 nodes. "
424                "Build the KG before capturing a snapshot."
425            )
426
427        manifest = self.load_manifest()
428
429        # Dedup: if version + metrics unchanged, refresh the latest entry in-place.
430        if not force and manifest.snapshots:
431            latest = max(manifest.snapshots, key=lambda x: x.get("timestamp", ""))
432            if snapshot.version == latest.get("version", "") and not self._metrics_changed(
433                metrics_dict, latest.get("metrics", {})
434            ):
435                old_key = latest["key"]
436                old_file = self.snapshots_dir / latest.get("file", f"{old_key}.json")
437                snapshot_file = self.snapshots_dir / f"{snapshot.key}.json"
438                snapshot_file.write_text(
439                    json.dumps(snapshot.to_dict(), indent=2) + "\n",
440                    encoding="utf-8",
441                )
442                if old_key != snapshot.key and old_file.exists():
443                    old_file.unlink()
444                latest.update(
445                    key=snapshot.key,
446                    branch=snapshot.branch,
447                    timestamp=snapshot.timestamp,
448                    file=snapshot_file.name,
449                )
450                manifest.last_update = datetime.now(UTC).isoformat()
451                self._save_manifest(manifest)
452                return snapshot_file
453
454        # Normal path: new or changed snapshot.
455        snapshot_file = self.snapshots_dir / f"{snapshot.key}.json"
456        snapshot_file.write_text(json.dumps(snapshot.to_dict(), indent=2) + "\n", encoding="utf-8")
457
458        existing_idx = next(
459            (i for i, s in enumerate(manifest.snapshots) if s.get("key") == snapshot.key),
460            None,
461        )
462        vs_prev = snapshot._vs_previous_raw
463        vs_base = snapshot._vs_baseline_raw
464        manifest_entry: dict[str, Any] = {
465            "key": snapshot.key,
466            "branch": snapshot.branch,
467            "timestamp": snapshot.timestamp,
468            "version": snapshot.version,
469            "file": snapshot_file.name,
470            "metrics": metrics_dict,
471            "deltas": {"vs_previous": vs_prev, "vs_baseline": vs_base},
472        }
473        if existing_idx is not None:
474            manifest.snapshots[existing_idx] = manifest_entry
475        else:
476            manifest.snapshots.append(manifest_entry)
477        manifest.last_update = datetime.now(UTC).isoformat()
478        self._save_manifest(manifest)
479        return snapshot_file

Persist snapshot, normalising metrics to a plain dict for the manifest.

The base ~kg_snapshot.snapshots.SnapshotManager.save_snapshot() writes snapshot.metrics directly into the manifest dict. For pycode-kg Snapshot instances that property returns a SnapshotMetrics dataclass — not JSON-serializable. This override uses the raw dict for manifest entries while preserving the base's dedup behaviour.

Parameters
  • snapshot: Snapshot to persist.
  • force: If True, always write a new history entry.
Returns

Path to the saved JSON file, or None if unchanged (dedup).

Raises
  • ValueError: If total_nodes is 0.
def diff_snapshots(self, key_a: str, key_b: str) -> dict[str, typing.Any]: 
518    def diff_snapshots(self, key_a: str, key_b: str) -> dict[str, Any]:
519        """Compare two snapshots side-by-side.
520
521        Extends the shared diff with ``module_node_counts_delta`` and
522        ``issues_delta`` (introduced / resolved issue strings).
523
524        :param key_a: First snapshot key (tree hash).
525        :param key_b: Second snapshot key (tree hash).
526        :return: Dict with metrics from both, computed deltas.
527        """
528        snap_a = self.load_snapshot(key_a)
529        snap_b = self.load_snapshot(key_b)
530
531        if not snap_a or not snap_b:
532            return {"error": "One or both snapshots not found"}
533
534        m_a = self._as_metrics_dict(snap_a.metrics)
535        m_b = self._as_metrics_dict(snap_b.metrics)
536
537        all_node_kinds = set(m_a.get("node_counts", {})) | set(m_b.get("node_counts", {}))
538        all_edge_rels = set(m_a.get("edge_counts", {})) | set(m_b.get("edge_counts", {}))
539
540        node_counts_delta = {
541            k: m_b.get("node_counts", {}).get(k, 0) - m_a.get("node_counts", {}).get(k, 0)
542            for k in all_node_kinds
543        }
544        edge_counts_delta = {
545            k: m_b.get("edge_counts", {}).get(k, 0) - m_a.get("edge_counts", {}).get(k, 0)
546            for k in all_edge_rels
547        }
548
549        all_modules = set(m_a.get("module_node_counts", {})) | set(
550            m_b.get("module_node_counts", {})
551        )
552        module_node_counts_delta = {
553            mod: m_b.get("module_node_counts", {}).get(mod, 0)
554            - m_a.get("module_node_counts", {}).get(mod, 0)
555            for mod in all_modules
556            if m_b.get("module_node_counts", {}).get(mod, 0)
557            != m_a.get("module_node_counts", {}).get(mod, 0)
558        }
559
560        issues_a = set(snap_a.issues)
561        issues_b = set(snap_b.issues)
562
563        return {
564            "a": {"key": snap_a.key, "metrics": m_a, "issues": snap_a.issues},
565            "b": {"key": snap_b.key, "metrics": m_b, "issues": snap_b.issues},
566            "delta": self._compute_delta_from_metrics(m_b, m_a),
567            "node_counts_delta": node_counts_delta,
568            "edge_counts_delta": edge_counts_delta,
569            "module_node_counts_delta": module_node_counts_delta,
570            "issues_delta": {
571                "introduced": list(issues_b - issues_a),
572                "resolved": list(issues_a - issues_b),
573            },
574        }

Compare two snapshots side-by-side.

Extends the shared diff with module_node_counts_delta and issues_delta (introduced / resolved issue strings).

Parameters
  • key_a: First snapshot key (tree hash).
  • key_b: Second snapshot key (tree hash).
Returns

Dict with metrics from both, computed deltas.

@dataclass
class SnapshotMetrics: 
61@dataclass
62class SnapshotMetrics:
63    """Core metrics captured in a pycode-kg snapshot."""
64
65    total_nodes: int
66    total_edges: int
67    meaningful_nodes: int
68    docstring_coverage: float  # 0.0 to 1.0
69    node_counts: dict[str, int]
70    edge_counts: dict[str, int]
71    critical_issues: int  # number of critical issues found
72    complexity_median: float  # median fan-in across functions
73    module_node_counts: dict[str, int] = field(default_factory=dict)  # nodes per module path

Core metrics captured in a pycode-kg snapshot.

SnapshotMetrics( total_nodes: int, total_edges: int, meaningful_nodes: int, docstring_coverage: float, node_counts: dict[str, int], edge_counts: dict[str, int], critical_issues: int, complexity_median: float, module_node_counts: dict[str, int] = <factory>)
total_nodes: int
total_edges: int
meaningful_nodes: int
docstring_coverage: float
node_counts: dict[str, int]
edge_counts: dict[str, int]
critical_issues: int
complexity_median: float
module_node_counts: dict[str, int]
